observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to (a) instruct the LDRRMO to maximize the utilization of LDRRM fund, monitor its monthly utilization and ensure timely and full implementation of all projects identified during the budget year to warrant the goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be achieved; and (b) convene with the MDRRM Council to review and evaluate planned PPAs to ensure that identified PPAs are viable for implementation and develop strategies to identify and  address the delay of implementation

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the LDRRMO to comply with the submission of the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Audit Team.


We recommended and the Management agreed to (i) appropriate and utilize solely the LDRRMF to programs, projects and activities that are supportive of the disaster risk management activities as provided under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-01; and (ii) provide appropriation for LDRRM office from General Fund proper necessary in the conduct of its day to day operations.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the remaining balance of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) and Mitigation Fund-Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) at the end of the year to ensure that funds will be utilized only to activities that support disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction:
The DRRM Officer submitted the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF.




No action was taken by the Management.




The Municipal Account transferred the unexpended balances of 30% QRF and 70% MF-MOOE on March, 2021.
observations.reason:
Only 70.98% of the 70% - MF were utilized by the Municipality for the year. 







The Management still charges ineligible expenses against the LDRRM Funds.




The unexpended balance of 30% QRF and 70% MF-MOOE were not transferred to Trust Liability –DRRMF account by year end.

Reiterated in Finding No. 10, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a)	direct both the MPDC and Municipal Engineer to ensure maximum utilization of 20% development fund by closely monitoring the timely and full implementation of its identified PPAs during the budget year in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality for the benefit of its constituents;

b)	instruct the MPDC to ensure that issues on the programs, projects and activities have already been settled prior to inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and implementation of projects to avoid delay in the execution of projects; and

c)	make representation with the Municipal Development Council to strictly program and utilize the 20% Development Fund only to allowable PPAs enumerated under Section 3 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 to come up with projects with developmental impact that would improve the socio-economic condition of the greater number of its constituents.
observations.managementAction:
No action was taken by the Management.




No action was taken by the Management.


Only allowable PPAs were charged to 20% DF current year appropriation.
observations.reason:
Utilization of the fund for year was only 34.87%.





Issues encountered in the implementation of the PPAs still remain unresolved.


Reiterated in Finding No. 11, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the concerned officers to maximize the utilization of GAD funds and ensure full implementation of the identified GAD programs, projects and activities to address gender gaps or issues sought by the said interventions and in order to fully realize the objectives of the Gender and Development Fund.


We recommended the Management to:

a)	direct the GAD focal person/official, with the aim of ensuring that the genuine local gender issues will be addressed, to conduct gender analysis of the agency’s programs and projects in the GPB to determine if the PPAs indicated therein addresses genuine local gender issues;

b)	 institute effective measures to monitor and ensure that PPAs are aligned to GAD-related undertakings; and

c)	observe strict adherence to the procedures and guidelines set forth in the IRR of RA No. 9710 in order to attain/achieve the objective of the “Magna Carta of Women”.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the members of GAD Focal Point System Council to:

a.	institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning and policy formulation;

b.	undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing and emerging gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and

c.	revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, which serve as guide in identifying the data requirements for particular gender issues.
observations.managementAction:
No action was taken by the Management.




No action was taken by the Management.


No action was taken by the Management.





The Management was not able to conduct survey to update their sex-disaggregated database due to COVID-19 restrictions. Further, the survey was taken over by the PSA for CY 2021.


No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason:
Only 44.25% of the total fund was utilized for the year. 





PPAs for CY 2020 amounting to ₱2,670,000.00 do not clearly address gender-related issues and concerns.







GAD Database was set up but the sex-disaggregated data was not updated.


Reiterated in Finding No. 12, Part II of this report.

The DRRM Officer Mr. Melvin Vitto was designated as the Acting MENRO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies, medicines, agriculture and marine supplies and other supplies and materials regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period as inventory, except those purchased through petty cash fund, following the perpetual inventory method and an inventory control account should be maintained for each inventory item pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume 1.

We recommend that management provide a safe and secure Municipal Treasurer’s Office or install security measures in the present MTO to safeguard the government funds from the risk of loss due to theft.
observations.managementAction: Window grills were already installed. Security guards stationed near the MTO night and day.
observations.reason: Window grills were already installed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer refrain from the practice of pre-signing checks without supporting vouchers and documents attached thereto to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: A meeting was called for the purpose to stop the practice of pre-signed checks without DVs
observations.reason: The LGU stopped the practiced of pre-signing checks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the Municipal Mayor to strictly implement the use of the biometric system in monitoring the time and attendance of the employees to achieve the intended purpose of the system. Also, direct the Human Resource Management Officer to require all employees to submit the manually prepared DTR completely filled up and strictly in accordance with the machine generated time records and at the same time maintain an updated logbook that will be regularly checked and inspected. If the employee is on field or official travel, require the employee to support the DTR with locator slips or travel order and certificate of appearance and in case the employee is on leave, attach the approved leave application in the DTR.

We further recommend that all Department Heads be required to use the biometric machine for the establishment of a reliable number of days worked.
observations.managementAction:
A meeting was called for the purpose.

All department heads rank in file employees and job orders was required to use biometrics except those who are in the field and others who can’t use for valid reason. 

A locator slip and logbook time in time out is use as alternative to biometrics in case in the field or their workplaces are away from the municipal office.

Strictly monitored the employees time in time out. Ensure DTRs and Biometrics matched with their time.
observations.reason: The LGU complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Mayor to direct the Human Resource Management Officer to strictly impose the submission of DTR on time as required. Moreover, cash advances should not be considered as liquidated if the approved DTRs were not yet submitted, and avoid granting cash advances for the same purpose if the previous cash advance was not yet liquidated/settled.
observations.managementAction: HRMO strictly imposed the submission of approved DTR on time as required and have it on file and attached to the payroll.
observations.reason: The LGU complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to strictly adhere to Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 on the guidelines on Monetization of Leave Credits and comply with the documentary requirements set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-001. We also recommend the Municipal Mayor to direct the Human Resource Management Officer to strictly follow the conditions set in the monetization of 50 percent or more of the accumulated vacation and sick leave balances and see to it that the employee presents proof of valid and justifiable reason.
observations.managementAction: HRMO is directed to strictly follow the conditions set in the monetization of 50 percent or more of the accumulated vacation and sick leave balances and see to it that the employee presents proof of valid and justifiable reason.
observations.reason: The LGU complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to strictly adhere Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 on the guidelines on payment of terminal leave benefits and comply with the documentary requirements set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: HRMO strictly adhere on the guidelines on payment of terminal leave benefits and comply with the documentary requirements
observations.reason: The LGU complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to review the long outstanding/stale checks specifically those checks where the bank made an error to debit/paid the checks which is not issued by the LGU and make a reconciliation with the bank immediately. Further, require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnished the accountant with the original DVs and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.
observations.managementAction: A meeting was called for the purpose. Municipal Accountant reviewed and reconciled the long overdue outstanding checks and stale checks on the bank reconciling item.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the General Services Officer to follow-up from the supplier the registration with the LTO of the motor vehicles acquired in CY 2012 to 2018. We also recommend the renewal of registration with the LTO of all service motor vehicles in compliance with RA No. 4136 to avoid penalties for late payment and payment of damages for third party liabilities in case of accidents.

observations.reason: All vehicles are registered and updated as to renewal.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management require all transfer of funds to barangays, to implement the projects and program of the Municipality, be covered by a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) and other documentary requirements as enumerated in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to establish proper accountability and monitoring of the proper utilization of the funds.

b.  	The non-implementation or non-liquidation of the transferred funds within the allotted time frame would disqualify the delinquent barangay/s from being a recipient of LGU-Buenavista for its future projects.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of officers and employees with unsettled cash advances, pursuant to Section 37 of Presidential Decree 1445. 

For cash advances of an Accountable Officer whose whereabouts could no longer be traced, the agency may file a request for authority to write-off dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Some of the concerned AOs already settled their unliquidated cash advances by submitting the required documents and others have paid through monthly salary deductions.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR 2020

observations.managementAction: Some of the concerned employees already submitted the lacking documents, while some did not especially those who are no longer connected with the LGU.

observations.recommendation: Stop immediately the charging of expenses arising from any pursuit of master’s degree and direct the Municipal Accountant to reject any of the said claims.
Require the Market Supervisor to make sure that all stallholders acquire or renew Contracts of Lease with complete details in conformity to the provisions stated in the enacted Market Code.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the following: (a) unexpended balances of LDRRMF by year of transfer; (b) receipts of NDRRMF by transferring agency; (c) DRRMF from other LGUs by LGU; and (d) other sources by donor. 

Direct the MDRRM Council to include in the LDRRMF Investment plan under a separate caption, the PPAs charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; review and update the plan annually to increase the disaster-resilience capability of the Municipality.

Require the MDRRM Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer for the prior years’ unexpended/unobligated balances of LDRRMF.

Review the GAD Plan and Budget to determine programs, projects and activities that are responsive to gender issues and concerns faced by their women and men stakeholders, as well as their clients and constituents; and 

Conduct Gender Sensitivity Training, Gender Analysis, Gender-responsive Planning and Budgeting and hire gender specialists/experts to capacitate the   LGU GAD Focal Point System’s on Gender and Development.

Ensure that future realignment or changes of appropriations/funds or projects be done by way of appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget and be transmitted to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for review.
Refrain from entering similar kind of transaction in the future.

Advise the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation and submission of the financial reports and Bank Reconciliation Statements and adhere to the prescribed period for submission of the required reports to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.

Moreover, avail of the eNGAS and eBudget from the COA to facilitate systematic generation of financial reports.

Direct the Disbursing Officer to liquidate his cash advance within five (5) days after each fifteen (15) day/end of the month pay period.

Advise the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the requirements on the prescribed period for submission of the required reports and transaction documents to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.

Ensure that the SEF shall be utilized solely for its purpose; i.e., programs, projects and activities enumerated in Section 272 of RA 7160, DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
Hasten the implementation of the desired projects out from the fund transfers from the Department of Trade and Industry and other NGAs.   Monitor the status of the other projects and its completion.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to:
• submit to the SA and ATL at the beginning of the year, the list of all ongoing government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year;
• inform the Auditors within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation; and
• regularly submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: No management action was made.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of the contracts together with the supporting documents within five working days from approval thereof. Also, instruct the BAC to strictly adhere and comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the prompt submission of perfected contracts and its supporting documents, to ensure timely review, evaluation and verification thereof.
observations.managementAction: No management action was made.
observations.reason: Not submitted due to oversight.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

provide allowance for impairment for dormant receivables and cash advance accounts which had been in the books for more than 10 years in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statements as required under PPSAS 29. Compute/determine impairment in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards or Philippine Financial Reporting Standards; and

comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the possible request for the write off of the specified dormant receivables and cash advances accounts to the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: No action taken on the part of Management.
observations.reason: The Management still failed to recognize allowance for impairment of receivables.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following courses of action:

instruct the Inventory Committee to expedite the completion of physical inventory taking to facilitate the reconciliation of (i) the records between the GSO and the Accounting Office and to ascertain the reliability of the PPE accounts, and to identify and segregate those that are still serviceable from unserviceable; and (ii) submit the physical inventory report thereon to COA not later than January 31 of each year;

require the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation of PPEs as provided for in No.17 of PPSAS; and

create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee responsible for the identification and evaluation of reported unserviceable properties.
observations.managementAction: No action taken on part of the Management
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee still failed to conduct complete physical inventory taking and prepare the (RPCPPE) for CY 2019. Moreover, depreciation was not provided for all PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to:

compute the corresponding income/withholding tax on claims for overtime rendered by municipal employees and cause immediate remittance of the same with the Bureau of Internal Revenue; and

submit the corresponding accomplishment report, approved DTR and duly approved authority to render overtime.
observations.managementAction: No action taken on part of the Management.
observations.reason: Claims for overtime services in CY 2019 totaling ₱151,768.77 were not supported with complete documents and overtime pay of employee amounting to ₱10,512.90 were not deducted the corresponding income tax.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government thru the Office of Senior Citizens Affair and the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office submit the aforementioned documentary requirements listed above for audit.  Henceforth, all documentary requirement for the disbursement of Social Pension fund be attached to the liquidation report of cash advances to enhance transparency and accountability on the transaction.
We recommended that the Municipal Government adopt a system of payroll payment through the ATMs of banks.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive always ensure that only eligible NGOs/POs will be granted cash advances thru strict compliance to documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.  Also, always ensure that only projects/activities within the capability of the NGO/PO and in line with the SEF priority expenses as provided under DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, 01-A, and 01-B are granted to NGO/PO. Further, cause the liquidation of the above subject cash advances and stop releasing funds to NGOs/POs with unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended the following:

Waste materials and unserviceable properties should be properly disposed according to applicable rules and regulations on supply and property management in LGUs.

Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Waste Materials Reports (WMR) should be diligently prepared, copies of which should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer, who took charge of relief distribution and inventory, fully account the relief goods purchased and distributed by submitting the duly acknowledged distribution lists. Management also submit an explanation why relief packs distributed were insufficient compared to the number of families evacuated despite availability of relief items, and why many were left after the relief operations. Proper planning and execution of relief operations should be instituted to avoid too much surplus of relief goods.
We recommended to require the Binirayan Foundation, Inc. to submit immediately the fund utilization report copy furnished the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that prudence and judiciousness should always be observed in the disbursement of funds for travelling and training expenses. Only necessary travels and trainings should be allowed.  The Human Resources and Development Office under the Municipal General Services Office should develop an annual comprehensive training and development plan for all the employees of the Municipality which will be the basis of allowed trainings and seminars for the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer be directed to strictly adhere to the accounting policies on inventories as provided for in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs). The Perpetual Inventory System should be strictly followed as well as the Moving Average Method in costing inventories. Stock Cards, Supplies Ledger Cards and the Physical Count of Inventories should be maintained and reconciled. Physical count of inventories should be conducted semestrally and a report thereof should be made.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, and other concerned department heads and LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and implementation of the funds require the recipient NGO/PO – SAME to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 96-003 as amended by COA Circular No. 2007-001 and to ascertain the propriety of expenditures as specified in the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended and Management agreed that demand letters be sent to the concerned accountable personnel and refrain from granting additional cash advances prior to liquidation of the previous ones. Salaries of the concerned personnel with long-overdue cash advances should be withheld until the same are settled/liquidated.
observations.reason: Finding first covered in 2011 Annual Audit Report (AAR) Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee agreed to reconcile the PPE accounts and draw journal entry voucher (JEV) to adjust the accounts affected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: a. regularly provide data to the Provincial Accountant on the Province’s share in the real property and other tax collections made by the Municipality and ensure reconciliation of the balances at year-end; b. submit details of the recorded balances of fund transfers for those payables recorded in prior years; and c. follow up the recording of liquidation reports to ensure that these are recorded by the Provincial Accountant and immediately submit liquidation documents for those unliquidated fund transfers recorded as Due from LGUs in the Province’s books.
observations.reason: Finding first covered in 2016 AAR. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 2.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the disbursement vouchers, payrolls, official receipts, financial reports and statements within the prescribed timeframe for timely audit of accounts.
observations.reason: Finding first covered in 2016 AAR. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 5.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strictly enforce the provisions specified in the GAA relating to net take home pay to promote efficiency and effectiveness in public service by ensuring that government employees have enough to support their daily needs.
observations.reason: Finding first covered in 2017 AAR. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 8.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that copies of contracts be submitted in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 for timely review thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management  closely monitor and ensure that the LCPC planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the fund so that the intended purpose  is attained/achieved as required under Section 15 of RA 9344 and Section I and II of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Fast track the implementation.
observations.reason: The agency has not yet substantially utilized the LCPC Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management refrain from releasing more funds to these leagues unless the required official receipts of the government are issued by the recipient Leagues concerned proper accounting and uphold observance of accountability and transparency of all government transactions.


In any case, the LGU has to demand proper accounting and Liquidation reports from the concerned leagues as to how the funds released were utilized.
observations.managementAction: Brought the problem during meetings of different leagues. The LGU planned to ask for MOA.
observations.reason: The LGU continued to release funds to different leagues despite the non-issuance of the required official receipts.

observations.recommendation:
Compute depreciation expense for all PPE accounts.

Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE and devise some measures to reconcile such report with the accounting records, so that PPE account balances in the Statement of Financial Position can be validated.

Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the PPEs with their beginning balances found in the accounting system which could not be traced.

Take appropriate action to finish the project in order to serve its purpose and hasten the completion of the required TESDA accreditation documents. 

Submit the reasons on the slow progress of the project.
