observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Treasurer to update the entries in her cashbooks on a daily basis to
ensure that the daily balances of her accountability are correctly stated; and
• Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records on
cash accounts at least every end of the quarter to ensure the reliability of
cashbook balances.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant to file the request for write-off with the Commission on
Audit on the unaccounted receivables and those under the Carabao
Dispersal program; and
• Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in collecting receivables from recipients of
SEA loans and send demand letters.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile his ledger cards with the property cards maintained by the GSO-Designate
and make the necessary adjustments after reconciliation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO-Designate to submit the
IIRUP with complete documentation based on the results of the physical inventory
in order to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with
applicable rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to analyze the accounts, confirm with account owners the existence of
their claims from the Municipal Government, process payment if proper, otherwise,
revert the accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Engineer to submit the:
• list of all on-going government PPA and those that are to be implemented
during the year; and
• monthly monitoring report in compliance with the existing regulation.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer/General Services Officer to submit the advance copies of POs/perfected
contracts within five working days from issuance and inform the deliveries within
twenty-four hours from acceptance in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001
and COA Circular No 96-010, respectively.

We recommended the following:
The Accounting Office and MGSO shall perform reconciliation between their records of PPE. Any discrepancies found shall be verified, investigated and adjusted in the books of accounts, if necessary. Henceforth, perform periodic/regular reconciliation of property cards to ensure that all discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts.  The PC and PPELC shall always be reconciled with each other in compliance with the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs Volume 1;
Municipal General Services Office shall prepare and maintain Property Cards (Annex C.5) per category of PPE to account for the receipt and disposition of the same; and
Accounting Office shall prepare and maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (Annex C.6) for each class of PPE which serves as Subsidiary Ledgers to support the PPE accounts in the General Ledger.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce the COA Order of Execution and instruct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries or other compensation due the persons liable in satisfaction of the disallowance in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.  Also, employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle their disallowances and charges for which they are liable.

We recommended the following:
The Accounting Office and MGSO shall perform reconciliation between their records of Road Networks. Any discrepancies found shall be verified, investigated and adjusted in the books of accounts, if necessary. Henceforth, perform periodic/regular reconciliation of property cards to ensure that all discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts.  The LRN Ledger Card and Property Card shall always be reconciled with each other in compliance COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015;
Municipal General Services Office shall prepare and maintain a Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
Accounting Office shall prepare and maintain a Local Road Network Property Card Property which serves as Subsidiary Ledgers to support the PPE accounts in the General Ledger.

We recommended to instruct the Accounting Office to take actions on the following:
Exert effort to analyze the account and immediately prepare an adjusting entries to recognize in the books unrecorded audit disallowances on the basis of NFD, if any, and drop in the books of accounts unrecorded settlement thereof under the NSSDC to obtain fair presentation of the account in the financial statements;
Always ensure that audit disallowances that have become final and executory, on the basis of the NFD are recorded in the books of accounts and settlement thereof under the NSSDC are dropped therefrom; and
Maintain an updated subsidiary ledger/records for each official/employee determined to be liable for the amount disallowed to ensure that audit disallowances including their settlements, are properly monitored and reconciled with the SASDC issued by the Auditor.

We recommended to require the Accounting Office to revise the Notes to FS for CY 2019 to include additional disclosure on Road Networks account based on the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommended to require the Accounting Office to analyze and reconcile the balances presented in the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flow from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) (Note 25) with the balances per fund in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to comply with the submission of GAD Plan and Budget within five (5) working days from the receipt of the approved plan from the DILG, and the corresponding Accomplishment Report shall be furnished to the Audit Team within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We reiterated our recommendation that the accounting office exert extra effort to look into the validity and correctness of balances carried over from previous years prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Accountant and remit the amount or make adjusting entries in the books, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the accounting office exert extra effort to look into the validity and correctness of the balance carried over from previous years prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Accountant and remit the amount or make adjusting entries in the books, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the accounting office exert extra effort to look into the validity and correctness of balances carried over from previous years prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Accountant and remit the amount or prepare adjusting entries in the books, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the accounting office exert extra effort to look into the validity and correctness of balances carried over from previous years prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Accountant and remit the amount or make adjusting entries in the books, if warranted.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
a. exhaust all efforts to fully identify the items composing the accounts Other Land
Improvements and School Buildings, for the Inventory Committee to be able to
verify their location, existence, and conditions to include them in the RPCPPE.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory
Committee agreed to regularly reconcile the PPE accounts and draw a journal entry
voucher (JEV) to adjust the accounts affected.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. regularly provide data to the Provincial Accountant on the Province’s
share in the real property and other tax collections made by the
Municipality and ensure reconciliation of the balances at year-end;
b. submit details of the recorded balances of fund transfers for those
payables recorded in prior years; and
c. follow up the recording of liquidation reports to ensure that these are
recorded by the Provincial Accountant and immediately submit
liquidation documents for those unliquidated fund transfers recorded
as Due from LGUs in the Province's books.

We recommended that the Management send demand letters to the
concerned accountable personnel, and ensure that salaries of the concerned
personnel with long-overdue cash advances are withheld until the same are
settled/liquidated.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
agreed to immediately process the release of the remaining 2019 barangay RPT
and Community Tax shares in accordance with RA 7160. Subsequently, regular
releases shall be made within five (5) days after the end of each quarter.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the
disbursement vouchers, payrolls, official receipts, financial reports and
statements within the prescribed timeframe for timely verification of accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed that copies of contracts be
submitted in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 for timely review thereof.

We recommended that Management refrain from paying PS expenses of the
LEE from the funds transferred from the General Fund. It is also recommended
that the existing operations of the LEE be re-evaluated and appropriate actions be
undertaken, taking into consideration the options provided in the aforementioned
Circular when revenues cannot fully fund PS requirements.

We also recommended the refund of the amount representing the
disbursements of the LEE for PS expenses that was funded by the fund
transferred from the General Fund-Proper.

We recommended that the Management strictly enforce the provisions
specified in the GAA relating to net take home pay to promote efficiency and
effectiveness in public service by ensuring that government employees have
enough to support their daily needs.

We recommended that the Management ensure the full implementation of
the planned disaster-related PPAs to equip the Municipality and its constituents
an adequate knowledge in effectively managing the effects of a disaster.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer agreed to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust
Fund every end of the year.

We recommend that management exert extra effort to complete the conduct of the physical count of its property, plant and equipment and prepare a complete and detailed report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment, in accordance with Section 124, Volume 1 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume 1.

We recommend that management send demand letters to the concerned loan borrowers through the cooperatives who received the financial assistance for the CBRM livelihood projects. Further, intensify its efforts by instituting appropriate measures to ensure collection of the long outstanding receivables.
For accounts deemed uncollectible, request authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off dormant accounts guided by the provisions of COA Circular No.97-001 dated February 5, 1997.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) for a resolution requesting the write-off of the receivable account of ₱209,601.95 which remained dormant for several years and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor together with the supporting documents as required in the Paragraph 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommend that management review the operations of the Waterworks System. Consider increasing the water rates and other applicable fees and charges.
Waterworks Supervisor to check the accuracy and the reliability of the billing system and ensure that the meter matches the electric bill.
Conduct periodic and continuous monitoring and review of the implementation of strategies with the end view that the desired results are achieved.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to review the long outstanding/stale checks specifically those checks where the bank made an error to debit/paid the checks which is not issued by the LGU and make a reconciliation with the bank immediately. Further, require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnished the accountant with the original DVs and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.

We recommend that management observe the guiding principles as prescribed under the Local Government Code of 1991 and the DILG-DOF Joint Memorandum Circulars to formulate an updated Local Revenue Code.

We recommend management to require all transfer of funds to barangays, be covered by a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) and other documentary requirements as enumerated in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to establish proper accountability and monitoring of the proper utilization of the funds. Likewise direct concerned officials to monitor the implementation of the projects/programs and liquidation thereof.

We recommend the management to strictly adhere to Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 on the guidelines on Monetization of Leave Credits and comply with the documentary requirements set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-001. We also recommend the Municipal Mayor to direct the Human Resource Management Officer to strictly follow the conditions set in the monetization of 50 percent or more of the accumulated vacation and sick leave balances and see to it that the employee presents proof of valid and justifiable reason.

We recommend the management to strictly adhere Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 on the guidelines on payment of terminal leave benefits and comply with the documentary requirements set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommend the Municipal Mayor to strictly implement the use of the biometric system in monitoring the time and attendance of the employees to achieve the intended purpose of the system. Also, direct the Human Resource Management Officer to require all employees to submit the manually prepared DTR completely filled up and strictly in accordance with the machine generated time records and at the same time maintain an updated logbook that will be regularly checked and inspected. If the employee is on field or official travel, require the employee to support the DTR with locator slips or travel order and certificate of appearance and in case the employee is on leave, attach the approved leave application in the DTR.
We further recommend that all Department Heads be required to use the biometric machine for the establishment of a reliable number of days worked.

We recommend the Municipal Mayor to direct the Human Resource Management Officer to strictly impose the submission of DTR on time as required. Moreover, cash advances should not be considered as liquidated if the approved DTRs were not yet submitted, and avoid granting cash advances for the same purpose if the previous cash advance was not yet liquidated/settled.

We recommend the General Services Officer to follow-up from the supplier the registration with the LTO of the motor vehicles acquired in CY 2012 to 2018. We also recommend the renewal of registration with the LTO of all service motor vehicles in compliance with RA No. 4136 to avoid penalties for late payment and undue payment of damages for third party liabilities in case of accidents.

We recommend that management provide a safe and secure Municipal Treasurer’s Office or install security measures in the present MTO to safeguard the government funds from the risk of loss due to theft.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer refrain from the practice of pre-signing checks without supporting vouchers and documents attached thereto to protect the interest of the government.

We recommend that management consider the improvement of the existing clauses in the MOA to include provisions on:
The period to submit the liquidation reports by the concerned recipient barangay.
The non-implementation or non-liquidation of the transferred funds within the allotted time frame would disqualify the delinquent barangay/s from being a recipient of LGU-Buenavista for its future projects.

Compute depreciation expense for all PPE accounts.
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property/Equipment Ledger Cards and the GSO to maintain Property/Equipment Cards for each type of PPE.
Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE and devise some measures to reconcile such report with the accounting records so that PPE balances in the Statement of Financial Position can be validated.
Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements, the PPEs with their beginning balances found in the accounting system which could not be traced.

Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each type of supplies and other inventory items, to record acquisition, issuance and disposal.
Likewise, require the GSO to maintain Stock Cards per item of inventory and conduct periodic physical count so that RPCI can be prepared and submitted every semester.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of officers and employees with unsettled cash advances, pursuant to Section 37 of Presidential Decree 1445.
For cash advances of an Accountable Officer whose whereabouts could no longer be traced, the agency may file a request for authority to write-off dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Strictly comply with the regulations stated in COA Circulars 94-013 and 2012-001 to ensure that all resources are managed, expended or utilized in accordance with laws, rules and regulations, and safeguarded against loss or wastage, illegal or improper disposition.
Stop immediately the charging of expenses arising from any pursuit of master’s degree and direct the Municipal Accountant to reject any of the said claims.
Require the Market Supervisor to make sure that all stallholders acquire or renew Contracts of Lease with complete details in conformity to the provisions stated in the enacted Market Code.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the following: (a) unexpended balances of LDRRMF by year of transfer; (b) receipts of NDRRMF by transferring agency; (c) DRRMF from other LGUs by LGU; and (d) other sources by donor.
Direct the MDRRM Council to include in the LDRRMF Investment plan under a separate caption, the PPAs charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; review and update the plan annually to increase the disaster-resilience capability of the Municipality.
Require the MDRRM Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer for the prior years’ unexpended/unobligated balances of LDRRMF.

Review the GAD Plan and Budget to determine programs, projects and activities that are responsive to gender issues and concerns faced by their women and men stakeholders, as well as their clients and constituents; and
Conduct Gender Sensitivity Training, Gender Analysis, Gender-responsive Planning and Budgeting and hire gender specialists/experts to capacitate the   LGU GAD Focal Point System’s on Gender and Development.

Ensure that future realignment or changes of appropriations/funds or projects be done by way of appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget and be transmitted to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for review.
Refrain from entering similar kind of transaction in the future.

Advise the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation and submission of the financial reports and Bank Reconciliation Statements and adhere to the prescribed period for submission of the required reports to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.
Moreover, avail of the eNGAS and eBudget from the COA to facilitate systematic generation of financial reports.

Direct the Disbursing Officer to liquidate his cash advance within five (5) days after each fifteen (15) day/end of the month pay period.
Advise the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the requirements on the prescribed period for submission of the required reports and transaction documents to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.

Ensure that the SEF shall be utilized solely for its purpose; i.e., programs, projects and activities enumerated in Section 272 of RA 7160, DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
Hasten the implementation of the desired projects out from the fund transfers from the Department of Trade and Industry and other NGAs.   Monitor the status of the other projects and its completion.

Monitor and hasten the implementation of the planned and on-going developmental projects to ensure the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.
Impose Liquidated Damages to the Contractors of projects with delayed implementation and withhold the amount due them.
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure ownership of land and preparation of the area before pushing through the procurement process of the projects.

Demand from the contractor 100% completion of the project, otherwise, find ways to complete the project so that the LGUs development goals on reduction of mortality and accessibility to health services be realized.
Likewise, require payment on the Liquidated Damages imposed to the Contractor.

Require the designated focal person to hasten the preparation and submission of the required documents as stated above in order to attain full completion of the Buenavista Skills Training and Development Center the soonest possible time.
Require the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to ensure complete documentation before the start of the procurement process for the 2nd phase of the project or to any upcoming projects.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to withhold/deduct the sums of P181,001.54 and P138,627.56 from the amounts payable to the Contractor.
Moreover, instruct the Municipal Engineer to meticulously prepare and review the ABC of upcoming Infrastructure Projects so that the same technical observation will not occur to those projects.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to withhold/deduct the sum of P30,820.29 from the amounts payable to the Contractor.
Moreover, instruct the Municipal Engineer to meticulously prepare and review the ABC of upcoming Infrastructure Projects so that the same technical observation will not occur to those projects.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Property and Supply/General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of records of the PPE.
observations.managementAction: Management has already exerted efforts on the reconciliation of PPE balances.
observations.reason: There were PPE items which remain unreconciled. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3, Page No. 34)

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant to file the request to the Commission on Audit for the write-off of the unaccounted receivables and those under the Carabao Dispersal program in accordance with Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
• Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in collecting receivables from recipients of SEA loans and send demand letters.
observations.managementAction: No management action was made. The Municipality was able to collect P7,000.00.
observations.reason: The Municipality encountered difficulty in the collections of loans receivable due to financial constraints, change of address of the borrowers and other hindrances that contributed to non-repayments

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary corrections/adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts. Also require the Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate transfer of funds for fair presentation of the balance of cash available for each fund.
observations.managementAction: Corrections/adjustments were already made by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer/Accountant to deduct from the salary of concerned officials/employees to ensure collection of the amount which was improperly paid by the Municipality. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all unexpended balances of cash advances are returned or refunded immediately to the collecting officer and ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and regulation.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances of employees were already liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the remittances of barangay shares from real property taxes and to remit the same to the concerned barangay treasurers within five days after the end of each quarter, in compliance with Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.managementAction: Shares from the RPT of the Barangays were already remitted to concerned Barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to analyze the accounts, confirm with account owners the existence of their claims from the Municipal Government, process payment if proper, otherwise, revert the accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that they will be making necessary adjusting entries to revert the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable to Unappropriated Surplus of GF amounting to P116,266.67 since they have not received any communication from the supplier regarding the release of the retention money. Meanwhile, the amount of P3,698.54 will be coordinated with the office concerned for the release of the retention due to a request letter handed to the Municipality by the Adarna House, Inc. dated March 13, 2018.
observations.reason: There are still unreverted portion (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 5, Page No. 37)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to:
• Exercise prudence in the preparation of Program of Work of infrastructure projects to ensure efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the implementation of infrastructure projects;
• Reduce the contract cost of the Concreting of Dela Paz-Mabini (Spillway) FMR by P373,371.94 and the contracts Construction of Seawall by P731,977.55. Also, require the contractor to refund the excessive contact cost in the total amount of P1,105,349.49 if full payment has been made by the Municipality; and
• Submit the lacking technical documents for further review and evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The management is already practicing prudence in the preparation of Program of Work of infrastructure projects; The Municipal Engineer justified that the approved budget for the contracts and the subsequent contract costs of the two projects are in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and DPWH Notice of Department Order No. 197, series of 2016. Based on the management's analysis and review of the discrepancies on the audit findings, they find that the labor costs and quantities to be minimal for Item 105 - Subgrade Preparation and Portland Cement Concrete Pavement (PCCP) of the project Concreting of Dela Paz - Mabini (Spillway) Farm-to-Market Road. In addition, the Construction of Seawall which was also noted in audit to be overestimated by P731,977.55 for the Grouter Riprap item, the Municipal Engineer says that he believes that other work items were considered but were not made part of the report since the item of work in question amounts to only P125,800.00. The Municipality was able to submit the technical documents.
observations.reason: The projects shall be subjected for re-inspection and re-evaluation of the Technical Services of the Commission on Audit. If warranted, Notice of Disallowance will be issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GSO submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Audit Team in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 77-61, for review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The GSO submitted the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Audit Team in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 77-61.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom. Also, instruct the MPDO in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to observe timeliness/proper planning that projects programmed for the year are fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: Some projects were already implemented.
observations.reason: Late submission of Program of Works by the Engineering Office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, secure the approval of its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission and develop and operate a sanitary landfill site as final disposal site pursuant to the provisions under the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management has already closed the controlled dumpsite. The Municipality's MENRO who is also the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC).
observations.reason: Due to the workload of the assigned Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) who is also the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council (LDC) in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to submit the report.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we recommended the Local Chief Executive to:

instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit BRS of all its bank accounts pursuant to paragraph 3.0 of the COA Circular No. 96-011; and

issue a memorandum against the Municipal Accountant requiring him to submit explanation for continuously disregarding the recommendation to prepare BRS and in case of inaction, suspend the payment of his salaries and allowances until such time that he had complied.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already prepared the BRS for CY 2019
observations.reason: The BRS prepared were not submitted to the Audit Team for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management (a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to close the remaining depository accounts with the PNB – Boac Branch and transfer the same to the existing account maintained with the LBP-Boac Branch; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry vouchers to record/take up the closing of bank accounts maintained with the PNB-Boac Branch to ensure presentation of correct balance of Cash-in-bank account in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had already closed all the accounts maintained with PNB-Boac Branch and transferred the remaining funds to LBP-Boac Branch.
observations.reason: The transfer of PNB accounts were not yet adjusted in the books of the Municipality.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office has sent follow-up notices/letters to every barangay and reminded taxpayers of their timely payment for tax obligations through different advertisements posted in publicly accessible and conspicuous place and also during the conduct of barangay assembly.
observations.reason: Real property tax receivables that have been long outstanding in the books remained uncollected.

observations.managementAction: Part of the outstanding cash advances was already liquidated and that demand letters was sent accordingly to all concerned.
observations.reason: The subject AOs still failed to liquidate their cash advances despite the formal demand made by the Team

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the Management to:

instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and effect necessary adjustment to record the (i) audit disallowances totaling ₱6,006,361.36 which had become final and executory and (ii) the cash shortage of the former Municipal Treasurer totaling ₱611,898.03; and

require the Municipal Accountant to furnish this office the list of payments/settlements for the eventual issuance of the corresponding NSSDC.
observations.managementAction: Adjustment was already made in the books for audit disallowances.
observations.reason:
The amount of ₱67,961.36 were not yet recorded in the books of the Municipality.

Partial settlement of disallowances of each liable persons and the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:

enforce the immediate liquidation of fund transfers extended to various NGOs/POs and other LGUs particularly those projects already implemented as required in paragraph 4.7 and 5.4 of COA Circular No. 94-013;

require the refund or return of any unused balance to the Municipal Treasurer as required under paragraph 6.7 of the same circular; and

refrain from processing fund transfers to NGOs/POs/ and barangays with unliquidated balances to avoid accumulation of unliquidated fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant continually reminded these NGOs and/or POs and other LGUs including barangay treasurer and have already sent demand letters to promptly submit the required reports and return the unexpended balance to the Municipal Treasurer. Also, he had already stop the grant of additional fund transfers to these concerned organizations with unliquidated balances.
observations.reason: Some of the recipient organizations still failed to submit the corresponding liquidation reports for implemented projects and refund the unused balance to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above, we recommended the Management to:

require the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare the required adjusting entries in order to properly account funds of the completed projects and review proper accounting entries with regard to projects funded from other agencies;

instruct the Accounting and Municipal Treasurer to undertake measures for the obligatory remittance of the unexpended balances to the National Treasury as required under Executive Order No. 431, Section 122 of GAAM, Volume I and Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013; and

perform reconciliation with source agencies to ascertain the nature of the transferred funds, whether the funds received require liquidation or are to be treated simply as subsidy to the LGU; if due for liquidation, prepare and submit liquidation reports for the expended portion and cause the return to the source agencies of all unutilized balances of the fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) already transferred completed projects totaling ₱13,060,282.89 to its proper asset account.
observations.reason:
Completed projects amounting to ₱5,000,288.00 was not transferred to proper asset account since it was covered with NFD dated October 14, 2015 and COE dated July 11, 2016.

The Municipality still failed to revert to the National Treasury nor return the excess to the source agencies the balances of the trust receipts recorded under the said account totaling ₱995,239.47

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:

direct the Municipal Accountant to stop processing of payrolls for salaries, wages and allowances in the absence of properly filled-up and signed DTRs and other supporting documents;

instruct the Municipal Treasurer to suspend payment of salaries, wages, allowances and processing of monetization of leave credits without complete supporting documents not until such time that they have complied with the requirements; and

direct the HRMO to require all officers and employees of the municipality to log upon arrival and departure to and from the office to record their attendance and as bases of the entries in their respective DTR.
observations.managementAction: Daily Time Records of the employees were already attached to the payrolls.
observations.reason: DTRs for the month of January and February were not attached to the vouchers and some DTRs of the Municipal employees for various month were not attached in the liquidation of payrolls.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the Management to:

adhere strictly to the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to ensure efficient output, come up with projects with developmental impact that would improve more the socio-economic condition of the greater number of its constituents;

identify the programs and projects that must be prioritized for inclusion and consideration in the Annual Investment Plan. For those which are inconsistent with the governing rules and regulations, initiate amendments or realignment of the latter to those that address the needs of the constituents as far as tangible projects and programs are concerned; and

source and appropriate in the General Fund Proper expenses that are administrative in nature which meet the validity and regularity requirements under existing accounting and auditing rules.
observations.managementAction: The Management ensure that all projects were included in the Annual Investment Plan.
observations.reason:
The Municipality still failed to fully maximize its 20% Development Fund in CY 2019 amounting to ₱41,781,755.47 resulting to non-utilization of fund totaling ₱27,033,954.71. 

Ineligible expenses totaling ₱3,797,130.18 were still charged to the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer, being the focal units, strictly comply with the policy guidelines and procedures in the implementation of LGSF-ADM and LGSF-AM Program particularly the documentary requirements enumerated under Section 4.4.1 and 4.4.2(iii) of the JMC.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the policy guidelines and procedures in the implementation of LGSF-ADM and LGSF-AM Program particularly the documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to (a) ensure allocation of at least 5% of the total annual budget for the implementation of GAD-related activities as required under Section 4.1.C.3 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and (b) maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through implementation of significant GAD related PPAs in order to attain the objective of which the funds were provided.

With the foregoing, we recommended the Management to require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the members of GAD Focal Point System Council to:

institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning and policy formulation;

undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing and emerging gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and

revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, which serve as guide in identifying the data requirements for particular gender issues.

We recommended the Management to (a) ensure allocation of at least 5% of the total annual budget for the implementation of GAD-related activities as required under Section 4.1.C.3 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and (b) maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through implementation of significant GAD related PPAs in order to attain the objective of which the funds were provided.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality appropriated at least 5% of its total authorized appropriation in CY 2019 for Gender and Development
observations.reason:
The Municipality failed to established/set up a GAD database.

GAD Fund utilization was not maximized due to the non-implementation of seven (7) programmed projects in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the Management to instruct the MDRRMO to maximize the utilization of LDRRM fund in achieving its main purpose and to further strengthen the capacity of the municipality for risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: The Management utilized 40.28% or ₱4,064,024.57 of ₱10,089,021.93 total amount available for LDRRM funds in CY 2019 during the year.
observations.reason: Management continuously failed to optimize the utilization of LDRRM funds leaving the sum of ₱6,024,997.36 unexpended.

observations.managementAction: The “no segregation, no collection” policy Management was already implemented by the Municipality.
observations.reason:
The central MRF installed at Brgy. Malbog were not yet functional and the programs identified in the 2019 AIP were not implemented. 

Moreover, the creation of plantilla positios MENRO was not being prioritized.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor as head of the agency exert extra effort to require all department heads to immediately conduct physical inventory of all PPE owned by the Municipality found in their respective offices including those issued to officials and employees.  Assign at least one personnel to consolidate per office inventory reports in the RPCPPE to be submitted to the accounting office for reconciliation in their records.  Submit a copy of RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor for audit.

We recommended that:

a.	The Municipal Accountant shall conduct verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the long unliquidated cash advances, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the recognition of the impairment in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).

b.	The Municipal Mayor shall file the request for authority to write-off unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) on the following accountable officers/employees in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 with the following requirements:

I.	For the deceased accountable officers/employees:

i.	Schedule of dormant accounts by accountable officer/debtor/government entity and by account,  certified by the accountant and approved by the Head of the government entity;
ii.	Death Certificate issued by Philippine Statistics Authority (formerly National Statistics Office)

II.	For accountable officers/employees that are no longer connected with the LGU with unknown whereabouts:

i.	Schedule of dormant accounts  by accountable officer/debtor/government entity and by account,  certified by the accountant and approved by the Head of the government entity;
ii.	Certificate of no residence in the barangay of the  municipality/city of last known address;
iii.	Certification by Legal Officer of  the entity of no pending case relative to the account; and 
iv.	Certification by the responsible officials of the entity to the effect that there are no records/documents available to validate claim.

We recommended that the LDRRM Office revisit/review the MDRRMP early during the current year to allow enough time to optimize its utilization of the LGU’s Pre-Disaster Fund so that programs, projects and activities as planned will be realized, and the adverse effects of disasters will be minimized.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office revise Note 10-Financial Liabilities in the Notes to Financial Statements to include additional information on Loans Payable Account such as the principal amounts, terms of the loan and loan status as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended the following:

a.	The Municipal Mayor assign personnel temporarily in the accounting office or other offices concerned during preparation and filing of reports for submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

b.	The Municipal Mayor direct the accounting office and other concerned offices to comply with COA rules and regulations and submit accounts and monthly or quarterly reports to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period. 

c.	October to December 2018 RCIs, DVs with supporting documents, and RCDs with copy of Official Receipts (all funds) be submitted to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after receipt of this AOM.  Otherwise, submit a written justification for its non-submission.

We recommended that the management submit to the Office of the Auditor the status of five remaining uncompleted ADM funded projects as of December 31, 2018.  Henceforth, the management should comply with the projects’ implementing guidelines and procedures particularly the preparation and submission of the required reports and documents to proper authorities within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Government thru the GAD Focal Point should comply with the submission of GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment report to the Office of the Auditor assigned to the agency within the prescribed period in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended that:

The Local Chief Executive require the concerned officers and employees who are currently employed with the LGU to liquidate their outstanding cash advances immediately thru submission of liquidation report with supporting documents or pay the amount of the unliquidated cash advance.  Likewise, exert extra effort to locate and resend demand letters to those who are no longer connected with the LGU; and

The Municipal Accountant withhold the salaries of concerned personnel who are currently employed with the LGU and failed to comply with the instructions on liquidation of their long outstanding cash advances as provided under Section 16 of Executive Order No. 298 dated March 23, 2004.

We recommended that the Local/Municipal Development Council review its procedure in the appropriation of the Municipality’s 20% development fund and include only in the Local Development Plan projects/programs defined and enumerated under the above cited Circular.  Henceforth, comply with the new guideline issued by the DILG and DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the annual IRA for development projects (DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017).

We recommended that granting of special cash advance for field operating activity other than what is prescribed under Section 3.2 of the COA NO. 97-002 be stopped.  Only personnel services (salaries, wages, honorarium and allowances), and maintenance and operating expenses which is impractical to pay by check can be paid out of special cash advance of authorized personnel.

We recommended that the proposed adjustments in the GL as shown in the tables in paragraph A and B be recorded in the books to correct the overstatement of cash in bank balance in the GF and the understatement in MEEDO Fund.  Journal entry vouchers for adjustments made in the books be prepared and submitted to the office of the Auditor.

We recommended the accounting office communicate with the concerned depository banks the long outstanding bank reconciling items appearing in the Bank Reconciliation Statements for necessary adjustments in bank’s records. Likewise, revalidate all reconciling items specifically those that are long outstanding including errors in recording and returned checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Government through concerned department heads ensure that provisions on the period of implementation of BuB projects be provided in each project proposal and in the Memorandum of Agreement so that timeliness in projects implementation can be properly monitored.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant demand from NGOs/POs the unliquidated fund transfers as of December 31, 2016 through submission of the Fund Utilization Report together with the required supporting documents for proper evaluation and recording in the books; and

Submit liquidation or fund utilization reports with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Government establish separate and specific policies and procedures or guidelines on the use/operation of the Emergency Medical Service vehicles.  The guideline shall provide among others the following provisions to ensure smooth implementation of the program and avoid abusive use of vehicles:
•	Types of emergency cases covered
•	Scope of services of EMS
•	Areas covered
•	Duties and Responsibilities of MDRRMO on supervision of EMS
•	Qualifications in hiring of personnel to be assigned in the field
•	Duties and Responsibilities of assigned personnel
•	Responsibility of persons transported
•	Payment of services
•	Limitations on the use of EMS
•	Limitations on services
•	Authority on the use of EMS, and;
•	Administrative sanctions

We recommended that the Municipal Government formulate policies and procedures in undertaking solid waste collections, cleaning of waste designated area in the barangay and proper disposal of wastes at the Final Disposal Area.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant support the Land account with subsidiary ledgers and documents such as certificates of title establishing legal ownership thereto. Management should exercise due diligence in obtaining legal documents and process titling of unsupported real properties owned by the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant should also disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the composition and valuation of the Land account.

We recommend that disbursements out of the SEF be in consonance to the provisions of RA 7160 (The Local Government Code) and to pertinent implementing rules and regulations. The Municipal School Board shall give priority to programs, projects and activities enumerated in the afore-mentioned regulations. Financial assistance and membership dues of pupils and students to Boy Scouts of the Philippines and Girl Scouts of the Philippines, as well as expenditures of the academe during Tiringbanay Festival should not be charged out of the SEF.

We recommend that the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 and the individual MOA be strictly complied in the grant, utilization and accounting of fund transfers to the NGOs/POs and the immediate liquidation of fund transfers out of the SEF be enforced. Additional cash advances should not be granted to NGOs/POs who have not liquidated their previous cash advances. All transfers should be recorded in Due from NGOs/POs (Account 139) and should only be credited once the fund utilization reports with all the supporting documents are submitted and after the Municipal Accountant has determined the validity and propriety of the transaction.

We recommended that management require the concerned barangays and the Liga to submit fund utilization reports in accordance with the provisions of the MOA. We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer draw checks in the name of the agency or in the official title or designation of its head or treasurer. Management should likewise conduct proper planning, budgeting and coordination to avoid delays and other problems that may arise in the execution of its programs, projects and activities.

We recommended strict policy on the timely submission of DTRs should be issued and implemented. Heads of Departments of the Municipality should exercise due diligence in monitoring timely submission of DTRs by their subordinates. Failure to submit the DTRs and its supporting documents shall warrant the withholding of the salaries of employees concerned.  Moreover, disciplinary action should be instituted against non-complying officials and employees following laws and rules in the Civil Service.

We recommend that LGU officials, the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel in charge of the disbursement and monitoring of the financial assistance should require the recipient NGO/PO– Association of Tiringbanay Cooperators Inc. and Coast Haven Inc. to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2007-001 to ascertain the attainment of objectives for which the funds were released as well as the propriety of expenditures.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor agreed to take the necessary measures to immediately update and reconcile their records, in order that the amount collectible for the year per taxpayer could easily be determined and furnish the Municipal Accountant with a certified list of taxpayers as basis for setting up the RPT Receivables and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer furnished the Municipal Accountant with a certified list, however, receivables not set up due to voluminous workload. On-going monitoring of implementation of recommendations.
observations.reason: Finding first covered in 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer agreed to process the release of the remaining 2018 barangay RPT shares in accordance with RA 7160. Subsequently, regular releases shall be made within five (5) days after the end of each quarter. Moreover, we recommended that representations be made to the developer of the E-TRACS, to make necessary adjustments/corrections in the computerized system for an accurate computation of barangay RPT shares.
observations.reason: Finding first covered in 2018 AAR. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 4.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to ensure full implementation of the planned disaster-related PPAs to equip the Municipality and its constituents in effectively managing the effects of a disaster. Likewise, we recommended and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer agreed to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to facilitate the timely review of the disbursements charged against the 5% LDRRMF.

We recommended and Management agreed to: a. require the implementing offices to expedite the completion of the above-mentioned development projects; and b. direct the Municipal Development Council and the implementing offices to ensure that only projects which are feasible and can be completed within the budget year are included in the Annual Development Plan; and ensure that the implementing offices are provided with adequate manpower and skills in the implementation of the 20% DF projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe the installation of signboards/ billboards within the project’s location in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to promote transparency, accountability and encourage citizens’ participation.

We recommended and Management agreed to cause the submission of the necessary supporting documents listed and henceforth, ensure that all disbursements be supported with complete documentation in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies, medicines, agriculture and marine supplies and other supplies and materials regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period as inventory, except those purchased through petty cash fund, following the perpetual inventory method and an inventory control account should be maintained for each inventory item pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume 1.

It is also recommended the General Services Officer to maintain stock cards and property cards for supplies, property, plant and equipment and consolidate weekly the Requisition and Issue Slip for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI). The SSMI with the original copy of the RIS shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method as provided under Section 115 of the same manual and prepare JEV to record the expenditures using the appropriate expense accounts.
observations.managementAction: Entries were made appropriately on this year’s financial statement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management study the packages offered by mobile networks and evaluate which would provide reasonable price that would still suit the needs of its officials and employees. We likewise recommend that management refrain from paying excessive expenditures as required under COA Circular No. 2012-003 and see to it that claims are supported with adequate documentation.

Further, consider purchase of cell cards in bulk by the GSO, following the required procurement procedures and distribute the same in accordance with the guidelines
observations.managementAction: The management implemented the limit of mobile phone and internet load by ₱2,200 per head of office and the LCE and Vice Mayor are ₱5,000 and ₱3,000 respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the KALAHI-CIDDS Municipal Coordinating Team be required to submit the accomplishment report which specifically state the man-hours spent and the completed or accomplished deliverables pursuant to DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007
observations.managementAction: No honorarium for any personnel assigned in the projects of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor stop immediately the approval of payments of honoraria to members of various executive committees considering that membership to such committees do not entail them to receive honoraria and to discontinue granting thereof, pursuant to the cited provisions of government laws, rules and regulations to avoid incurrence of irregular expenditures.
observations.reason: No honorarium for any personnel assigned in the projects of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management attach to the disbursement vouchers the Monthly Report of Rice Consumption together with the list of detailed prisoners at Buenavista MPS and detailed CAFGU, AFP and SWAT personnel. 

Furthermore, resort to NFA as primary supplier for rice subsidy to detailed prisoners at Buenavista MPS and detailed CAFGU, AFP and SWAT personnel
observations.managementAction: Attached the supporting documents as recommended.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management refrain from sending personnel to trainings and seminars conducted by private institutions without securing authority from DILG. For future similar undertakings and seminars, authorization from the DILG has to be obtained first before attending the said seminar or activity in compliance with the provisions of DILG-MC No.99-64 dated April 23, 1999.
observations.managementAction: No trainings and seminars attended when conducted by private institution.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the contractor to fast track the completion of the project and impose applicable sanctions and penalties if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The contractor responded and completed the project and the contractor will be billed as soon as supporting documents are complete.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to adhere to the provisions embodied in COA Circular No. 80-101C to facilitate appropriate action on papers relative to the suspension orders on government infrastructure contracts.
observations.managementAction: The MEO assigned personnel submitted to COA all suspension orders and resume orders right after approval of the LCE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC refrain from channeling procurement opportunity to the BBATLEMCO as this is not a semblance of transparency and non-compliant to the principle of competitive bidding.  Henceforth, ensure that procurement activities be aligned with the laws, rules and regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Stopped the BBTLEMCO to quote procurement of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
To protect the interest of the government and to prevent further loss and wastage of government funds resources, we recommend that Management immediately apply the remedies available as provided in Section 8.4 of R.A 9184 for the unjustified delay in the completion of the project, which is, to rescind the contract, forfeit the contractor’s performance security and takeover the prosecution of the project or award the same to a qualified contractor through negotiated contract.

Otherwise, LGU officials responsible in the proper implementation of the project may be held liable on the impending loss and wastage of government funds spent on the project for their negligence or non- action, as provided in Section 102 and 103 of the State Auditing Government Code as afore-quoted.

We recommend further that, LGU officials have to provide safeguards against delinquent and irresponsible contractors. The project engineer has to monitor and require the contractor to submit Statement of Work Accomplished on a monthly basis and ensure that prosecution of the projects is in accordance with the timelines set in the contract. Requests for contract time extension maybe granted or allowed only for justifiable reasons as provided in Section 9.2 of Annex D of the IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Final inspection is conducted and the required supporting documents for final payment were complied. Liquidated damages were already deducted from the final billing of the contractor. The LCE reminded the Municipal Engineer to avoid the same problem with other future projects of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman/Secretariat to furnish the COA Office with copies of the perfected contracts/ purchase orders together with the complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. Every revision made to the contract such as contract time extensions, extra work orders or variation orders shall also be furnished to the Auditor for appropriate review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The LGU requested for final inspection from DILG and COA and recommended for final payment.
Accounting set accounting controls on the payment of communication allowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) in coordination with the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to clearly specify the priority projects and programs that shall funded out of the 20% Development Fund and to stop the practice.
observations.managementAction: Succeeding AIPs were formulated in accordance with the recommended actions of COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure that the 20% Development Fund shall be utilized only for those PPAs covered under section 3 of the DILG- DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01.

We therefore recommend that the appropriations allotted for the Landscaping and Beautification Project be realigned to other PPAs that conform with Environmental Management outcomes as contemplated in the JMC and focus on providing the basic services and facilities of solid waste management in the municipality.

Moreover, we recommend that formation of the Waste Management Board be initiated to formulate the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan that shall ensure the long-term management of solid waste, as well as integrate the various solid waste management plans and strategies of the Barangays in its area of jurisdiction.
observations.managementAction: Implemented in accordance with the revised scope of works of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to effect proper classification of accounts to present more accurately the balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Still on the process of making adjustments on the books in every item considered as small tangible items. Still preparing the Inventory Custodian Slip
observations.reason: The LGU is yet to complete the necessary adjustments and Inventory Custodian Slip.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management ensure the full utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund and be guided by the afore-cited Joint Memorandum Circular. We also recommend that management with the assistance of the MPDC implement the projects identified within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the people and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices of materials in order to address the needs of the community.
observations.managementAction: Fast rack implementation of projects.
observations.reason: The LGU is yet to complete the implementation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal 
Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each type of supplies, crops and other inventory items, to record acquisition, issuance and disposal.

Conduct semestral physical count of inventory items by type and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct review and validation on each of the journal entries and records made under Advances and Other Receivables accounts of the GF and SEF to determine the correctness and propriety of the subject entries and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: The reconciliation is ongoing; and adjustments have been made resulting to the decrease of the subject accounts balances as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Agriculturist to:

a. Prepare and submit the Inventory of the Biological Assets as of December 31, 2018; and

b. Furnish the Municipal Accountant with documents relative to additions, distribution, reproduction and death of the breeding stocks.
observations.managementAction: The reconciliation between the concerned offices is ongoing; and adjustments have been made resulting to the decrease of the inventory accounts balances as of December 31, 2019. However, no Inventory Report on the Biological Assets was submitted.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to agree on the dates of submission of the disbursement vouchers, collections and deposits and liquidation reports from the former Office to the latter’s, to facilitate timely submission of the said documents from the Accounting Office to COA as provided under Sec. 39 of PD 1445 and Sec. 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has improved in its submission of accounts, incurring only short delays.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR 2019

observations.recommendation:
Advise the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation and submission of the financial reports and adhere to the prescribed period for submission of the required reports to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.
Moreover, avail of the eNGAS and eBudget from the COA to facilitate systematic generation of financial reports.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR 2019

observations.recommendation:
Strictly refrain from granting additional cash advances if the previous cash advances were not yet settled.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the erring accountable officers, including retired/transferred employees to settle their outstanding cash advances immediately. 

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of officers and employees with unsettled cash advances, pursuant to Section 37 of Presidential Decree 1445. 

For cash advances of an Accountable Officer whose whereabouts could no longer be traced, the agency may file a request for authority to write-off dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of the required documents to support the propriety and validity of the transactions.

observations.recommendation: Require the BAC/BAC Secretariat to thoroughly and meticulously review and validate the eligibility of the documents submitted by the bidders before proceeding to Post Qualification and issuance of the Notice of Award.

observations.recommendation:
Stop the payment of prior year’s transaction charged to the current year’s appropriations as provided in the aforementioned provision.  

Determine in advance all expenses incurred which are due and demandable in the current year which cannot be processed during the year to be recorded as Accounts Payable.

Likewise, the Budget Office and Accountant are advised to be strict in enforcing the provision.

Establish a sustainable Sanitary Landfill consistent with the standards and criteria set under RA 9003 for the protection of the environmental and human health. 

Considering the high financial requirement of establishing a Sanitary Landfill, the Agency may look on the idea of cluster sanitary landfill where adjacent cities/municipalities may together pool their resources to establish a shared sanitary landfill as suggested in the same Act.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has started its work on the safe closure of its open dumpsite in preparation for the development of their sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: Hasten the implementation of the desired projects out from the fund transfers of the Department of Trade and Industry and monitor completion of the projects and activities funded by the BUB of DA and DOLE.
