observations.recommendation:
Ensure the prompt implementation of the planned activities and projects as incorporated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to increase the level of disaster preparedness and response capability of the LGU.

Maximize the use of funds by carefully determining the budgetary requirements of each PPA during the planning stage. As such, the Agency would have more available funds for other PPAs identified to be essential in the full attainment of the purpose/objectives of the creation of the fund.
Management coordinate with the source agencies for proper return of the said unutilized funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already returned some of the unutilized fund transfers to the source agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to:
 submit to the SA and ATL at the beginning of the year, the list of all ongoing government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year;
 inform the Auditors within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation; and
 regularly submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted the list of all on-going PPAs and those that were to be implemented during the year but failed to submit the monthly monitoring reports within the prescribed period; project signboards and notices were posted but auditors were not informed within ten days.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer submitted the list of all on-going PPAs for CY 2018 only at the end of the year but failed to submit monthly monitoring report, thus auditors were not informed on the appropriate project signboards for validation. Reiterated in Part 2, Observation No. 8 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify collections of real property and special education taxes due from the delinquent taxpayers by adopting measures and devising new strategies to improve collection efficiency.
observations.managementAction: No action taken on the part of the Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 3, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to: instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and effect necessary adjustment to record the (i) audit disallowances totaling ₱5,067,961.36 which had become final and executory and (ii) the cash shortage of the former Municipal Treasurer totaling ₱611,898.03; and require the Municipal Accountant to furnish this office the list of payments/settlements for the eventual issuance of the corresponding NSSDC.
observations.managementAction: No action taken on the part of the Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 5, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: provide allowance for impairment for dormant receivables and cash advance accounts which had been in the books for more than 10 years in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statements as required under PPSAS 29. Compute/determine impairment in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards or Philippine Financial Reporting Standards; and comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the possible request for the write off of the specified dormant receivables and cash advances accounts to the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: No action taken on the part of the Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 6, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and ensure implementation and completion of the projects identified for the benefit of its constituents; and strictly adhere to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Community Development Fund to ensure efficient output, come up with projects with developmental impact that would improve the socio-economic condition of the greater number of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: No action taken on the part of the Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 11, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant be directed to submit the required monthly and quarterly financial reports on time as prescribed under Section 70 and 73 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I or as provided by the New Government Accounting Manual (GAM). Otherwise, the administrative sanctions provided under Section 122 of PD No. 1445 will be implemented.
observations.managementAction: No action taken on the part of the Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 19, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above, we recommended that the Management should see to it that the hiring of individuals under job order are covered with contracts indicating nature, scope and quantity of work to be done. Each job order should be required to submit an accomplishment report prior to the payment of their wages to ensure that government funds are expended accordingly.
observations.managementAction: No action taken on the part of the Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 20, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: submit copies of the contracts/purchase orders together with the supporting documents, for review and technical evaluation of the contract to point out to management the defects of the contracts and the project itself, should there be any, and to effect timely corrective measures; and require the BAC and the MGSO to ensure that the contract and all its supporting documents, including those required in the auditorial and technical review, be submitted to the Auditor’s Office within five days from perfection thereof as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: No action taken on the part of the Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 21, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government adopt a system of payroll payment through the ATMs of banks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the accounting office and other concerned offices to comply with COA rules and regulations and submit accounts and monthly or quarterly reports to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive always ensure that only eligible NGOs/POs will be granted cash advances thru strict compliance to documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.  Also, always ensure that only projects/activities within the capability of the NGO/PO and in line with the SEF priority expenses as provided under DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, 01-A, and 01-B are granted to NGO/PO. Further, cause the liquidation of the above subject cash advances and stop releasing funds to NGOs/POs with unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor secure the necessary travel authority from the Governor every time he travels outside of his official station pursuant to Section 96 of R.A. 7160.  Also, persons responsible submit to the office of the Auditor lacking local travel documents enumerated above in compliance with Section 1.2.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for local travels listed in Annex 3 of this report.

We recommended that the provisions of Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2014 on the grant of transportation allowance be strictly observed.  Ensure that claims of this nature shall bear the required supporting documents.  We also recommend that persons responsible submit to the Office of the Auditor the lacking documentary requirements to support each claim of transportation allowance.

We recommended the following:

Waste materials and unserviceable properties should be properly disposed according to applicable rules and regulations on supply and property management in LGUs.

Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Waste Materials Reports (WMR) should be diligently prepared, copies of which should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer, who took charge of relief distribution and inventory, fully account the relief goods purchased and distributed by submitting the duly acknowledged distribution lists. Management also submit an explanation why relief packs distributed were insufficient compared to the number of families evacuated despite availability of relief items, and why many were left after the relief operations. Proper planning and execution of relief operations should be instituted to avoid too much surplus of relief goods.
We recommended to require the Binirayan Foundation, Inc. to submit immediately the fund utilization report copy furnished the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that prudence and judiciousness should always be observed in the disbursement of funds for travelling and training expenses. Only necessary travels and trainings should be allowed.  The Human Resources and Development Office under the Municipal General Services Office should develop an annual comprehensive training and development plan for all the employees of the Municipality which will be the basis of allowed trainings and seminars for the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer be directed to strictly adhere to the accounting policies on inventories as provided for in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs). The Perpetual Inventory System should be strictly followed as well as the Moving Average Method in costing inventories. Stock Cards, Supplies Ledger Cards and the Physical Count of Inventories should be maintained and reconciled. Physical count of inventories should be conducted semestrally and a report thereof should be made.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, and other concerned department heads and LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and implementation of the funds require the recipient NGO/PO – SAME to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 96-003 as amended by COA Circular No. 2007-001 and to ascertain the propriety of expenditures as specified in the Memorandum of Agreement.
We recommended and Management agreed that:
observations.managementAction: 1) sanctions be imposed on the defaulting officer; 2) in case of deceased employee/s, initiate write-off of unliquidated cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; 3) the Municipal Accountant send demand letters through registered mail to the last known home address of those retired/resigned employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
observations.managementAction: a. regularly provide the Provincial Accountant with data on the Province’s share in the real property and other tax collections made by the Municipality; b. remit the valid current due Province’s share in real property and other taxes which remained unremitted by the Municipality; c. trace the details of the fund transfers by requesting copies of Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) of the Province; and d. submit liquidation documents for fund transfers recorded by the Province as Due from LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that: 1. the Municipal Disaster Risk Management Officer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator ensure that the planned PPAs for disaster risk management are fully implemented on target implementation period to ensure the Municipality’s preparedness to natural and man-made hazards/disasters; 2. ensure that only pre-disaster preparedness and mitigation PPAs be funded under the 70% allocation of the MDRRMF and only authorized PPAs be charged against the fund; 3. ensure monthly submission of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and 4. hire or designate qualified personnel to occupy the vacant staff positions of the LDRRMO in accordance with item 5.5 of the JMC.

We recommended and Management agreed to: a. require the Engineering office to review the unimplemented projects if it is still viable to implement as programmed, if not, consider re-aligning the same for more feasible projects which have a better impact to its constituents; b. direct the Municipal Development Council to ensure that the development projects identified in the ADP are feasible and can be implemented within the year with the proposed budget allocation; c. require the implementation of the development projects within the time schedule as targeted in the ADP and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund; and d. cause the submission of the necessary supporting documents identified.

We recommended and Management agreed to comply with the requirements of Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445 and other existing law, rules and regulations, specifically: 1. The Accountant to strictly abide with the submission of accounting records within the deadline; 2. The Inventory Committee to furnish copy of the Report of Physical Count of PPE to the Accountant and COA Audit Team within the deadline; and 3. The Municipal Engineer to provide report on the cost of completed projects and the status of on-going projects likewise submission of detailed cost of components of all infrastructure projects, among others.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that paying capacity of each employee-borrower above the minimum mandated net take home pay should be considered prior to the approval of loan applications.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Property and Supply/General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal
Accountant for the reconciliation of records of the PPE.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
 Municipal Accountant to file the request to the Commission on Audit
for the write-off of the unaccounted receivables and those under the
Carabao Dispersal program in accordance with Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
 Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in collecting receivables from
recipients of SEA loans and send demand letters.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to make the necessary corrections/adjustments to reflect the correct balances
of the affected accounts. Also require the Municipal Treasurer to cause the
immediate transfer of funds for fair presentation of the balance of cash
available for each funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer/Accountant to deduct from the salary of concerned
officials/employees to ensure collection of the amount which was improperly
paid by the Municipality. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it
that all unexpended balances of cash advances are returned or refunded
immediately to the collecting officer and ensure the proper granting, utilization
and liquidation of all cash advances within the prescribed period in
accordance with the existing rules and regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the remittances of barangay shares
from real property taxes and to remit the same to the concerned barangay
treasurers within five days after the end of each quarter, in compliance with
Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to analyze the accounts, confirm with account owners the existence of their
claims from the Municipal Government, process payment if proper, otherwise,
revert the accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to:
 submit to the SA and ATL at the beginning of the year, the list of all
ongoing government projects/programs/activities and those that are
to be implemented during the year;
 inform the Auditors within ten days after the award of the
infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that
the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already
posted for validation; and
 regularly submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the
above cited regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC
Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of the contracts together
with the supporting documents within five working days from approval thereof.
Also, instruct the BAC to strictly adhere and comply with the pertinent
provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the prompt submission of
perfected contracts and its supporting documents, to ensure timely review,
evaluation and verification thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to:
 Exercise prudence in the preparation of Program of Work of
infrastructure projects to ensure efficiency, economy and
effectiveness in the implementation of infrastructure projects;
 Reduce the contract cost of the Concreting of Dela Paz-Mabini
(Spillway) FMR by P373,371.94 and the Construction of Seawall by
P731,977.55. Also, require the contractor to refund the excessive
contact cost in the total amount of P1,105,349.49 if full payment has
been made by the Municipality; and
 Submit the lacking technical documents for further review and
evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the GSO submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels
and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Audit Team in compliance with
the provision of COA Circular No. 77-61, for review and evaluation.

instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit BRS of all its bank accounts pursuant to paragraph 3.0 of the COA Circular No. 96-011; and
issue a memorandum against the Municipal Accountant requiring him to submit explanation for continuously disregarding the recommendation to prepare BRS and in case of inaction, suspend the payment of his salaries and allowances until such time that he had complied.

(a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to close the remaining depository accounts with the PNB – Boac Branch and transfer the same to the existing account maintained with the LBP-Boac Branch; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry vouchers to record/take up the closing of bank accounts maintained with the PNB-Boac Branch to ensure presentation of correct balance of Cash-in-bank account in the financial statement.

instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and effect necessary adjustment to record the (i) audit disallowances totaling ₱6,006,361.36 which had become final and executory and (ii) the cash shortage of the former Municipal Treasurer totaling ₱611,898.03; and
require the Municipal Accountant to furnish this office the list of payments/settlements for the eventual issuance of the corresponding NSSDC.

provide allowance for impairment for dormant receivables and cash advance accounts which had been in the books for more than 10 years in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statements as required under PPSAS 29. comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the possible request for the write off of the specified dormant receivables and cash advances accounts to the Commission on Audit.

enforce the immediate liquidation of fund transfers extended to various
NGOs/POs and other LGUs particularly those projects already implemented as required in paragraph 4.7 and 5.4 of COA Circular No. 94-013;
require the refund or return of any unused balance to the Municipal Treasurer as required under paragraph 6.7 of the same circular; and
refrain from processing fund transfers to NGOs/POs/ and barangays with unliquidated balances to avoid accumulation of unliquidated fund transfers.

instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer, being the focal units, strictly comply with the policy guidelines and procedures in the implementation of LGSF-ADM and LGSF-AM Program particularly the documentary requirements enumerated under Section 4.4.1 and 4.4.2(iii) of the JMC.
instruct the MDRRMO to maximize the utilization of LDRRM fund in achieving its main purpose and to further strengthen the capacity of the municipality for risk reduction and management.

compute the corresponding income/withholding tax on claims for overtime rendered by municipal employees and cause immediate remittance of the same with the Bureau of Internal Revenue; and
submit the corresponding accomplishment report, approved DTR and duly approved authority to render overtime.

implement the prioritized programs/projects/activities in the approved Local School Board budget/investment plan; and
appropriate funds for the implementation of Early Childhood Care Development (ECCD) program to cater the needs of younger children.

direct Municipal Accountant to submit the required monthly and quarterly financial reports and keenly observe the deadline set for the submission of trial balance and other reports as required under Section 70 and 73 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I or as provided by the New Government Accounting Manual (GAM). Otherwise, the administrative sanctions provided under Section 122 of PD No. 1445 will be implemented.

see to it that the hiring of individuals under job order are covered with contracts indicating nature, scope and quantity of work to be done. Each job order should be required to submit an accomplishment report prior to the payment of their wages to ensure that government funds are expended accordingly.

submit copies of the contracts/purchase orders together with the supporting documents, for review and technical evaluation of the contract to point out to management the defects of the contracts and the project itself, should there be any, and to effect timely corrective measures; and
require the BAC and the MGSO to ensure that the contract and all its supporting documents, including those required in the auditorial and technical review, be submitted to the Auditor’s Office within five days from perfection thereof as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor as head of the agency exert extra effort to require all department heads to immediately conduct physical inventory of all PPE owned by the Municipality found in their respective offices including those issued to officials and employees.  Assign at least one personnel to consolidate per office inventory reports in the RPCPPE to be submitted to the accounting office for reconciliation in their records.  Submit a copy of RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor for audit.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant shall conduct verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the long unliquidated cash advances, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the recognition of the impairment in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).
The Municipal Mayor shall file the request for authority to write-off unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) on the following accountable officers/employees in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 with the following requirements:
For the deceased accountable officers/employees:
Schedule of dormant accounts by accountable officer/debtor/government entity and by account, certified by the accountant and approved by the Head of the government entity;
Death Certificate issued by Philippine Statistics Authority (formerly National Statistics Office)
For accountable officers/employees that are no longer connected with the LGU with unknown whereabouts:
Schedule of dormant accounts by accountable officer/debtor/government entity and by account, certified by the accountant and approved by the Head of the government entity;
Certificate of no residence in the barangay of the municipality/city of last known address;
Certification by Legal Officer of the entity of no pending case relative to the account; and
Certification by the responsible officials of the entity to the effect that there are no records/documents available to validate claim.

We recommended that the Municipal Government thru the Office of Senior Citizens Affair and the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office submit the aforementioned documentary requirements listed above for audit.  Henceforth, all documentary requirement for the disbursement of Social Pension fund be attached to the liquidation report of cash advances to enhance transparency and accountability on the transaction.

We recommended that the LDRRM Office revisit/review the MDRRMP early during the current year to allow enough time to optimize its utilization of the LGU’s Pre-Disaster Fund so that programs, projects and activities as planned will be realized, and the adverse effects of disasters will be minimized.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office revise Note 10-Financial Liabilities in the Notes to Financial Statements to include additional information on Loans Payable Account such as the principal amounts, terms of the loan and loan status as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Mayor assign personnel temporarily in the accounting office or other offices concerned during preparation and filing of reports for submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.
The Municipal Mayor direct the accounting office and other concerned offices to comply with COA rules and regulations and submit accounts and monthly or quarterly reports to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.
October to December 2018 RCIs, DVs with supporting documents, and RCDs with copy of Official Receipts (all funds) be submitted to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after receipt of this AOM.  Otherwise, submit a written justification for its non-submission.

We recommended that the management submit to the Office of the Auditor the status of five remaining uncompleted ADM funded projects as of December 31, 2018.  Henceforth, the management should comply with the projects’ implementing guidelines and procedures particularly the preparation and submission of the required reports and documents to proper authorities within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Government thru the GAD Focal Point should comply with the submission of GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment report to the Office of the Auditor assigned to the agency within the prescribed period in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended that the accounting office exert extra effort to consider the validity and correctness of balances carried over from previous years prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Accountant and prepare adjusting entries in the books, if necessary.

We recommended that the accounting office exert extra effort to consider the validity and correctness of balances carried over from previous years prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Accountant and prepare adjusting entries in the books, if necessary.

We recommended that the accounting office exert extra effort to look into the validity and correctness of balances carried over from previous years prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Accountant and prepare adjusting entries in the books, if necessary.

We recommended that the accounting office exert extra effort to look into the validity and correctness of balances carried over from previous years prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Accountant and prepare adjusting entries in the books, if necessary.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory
Committee agreed to reconcile the PPE accounts and draw journal entry
voucher (JEV) to adjust the accounts affected.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. regularly provide data to the Provincial Accountant on the
Province’s share in the real property and other tax collections made by the Municipality and
ensure reconciliation of the balances at year-end;
b. submit details of the recorded balances of fund transfers for those
payables recorded in prior years; and
c. follow up recording of liquidation reports to ensure that these are
recorded by the Provincial Accountant and immediately submit
liquidation documents for those unliquidated fund transfers
recorded as Due from LGUs in the Province’s books.

We recommended and Management agreed that demand letters be
sent to the concerned accountable personnel and refrain from granting
additional cash advances prior to liquidation of the previous ones. Salaries
of the concerned personnel with long-overdue cash advances should be
withheld until the same are settled/liquidated.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
agreed to process the release of the remaining 2018 barangay RPT shares in
accordance with RA 7160. Subsequently, regular releases shall be made within
five (5) days after the end of each quarter. Moreover, we recommended that
representations be made to the developer of the E-TRACS, to make necessary
adjustments/corrections in the computerized system for an accurate computation
of barangay RPT shares.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit
the disbursement vouchers, payrolls, official receipts, financial reports and
statements within the prescribed timeframe for timely audit of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly enforce the
provisions of Section 48 of the GAA of CY 2018 to promote effectiveness
and efficiency in public service by ensuring adequate net take home pay of
the employees for their daily subsistence and ensure that net take home
pay will be considered prior to the approval of loan applications.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe the
installation of signboards/ billboards within the project’s location in
compliance with COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to promote
transparency, accountability and encourage citizens’ participation.

We recommended and Management agreed that copies of contracts
be submitted in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 for timely
review thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to cause the submission
of the necessary supporting documents listed and henceforth, ensure that
all disbursements be supported with complete documentation in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. require the implementing offices to expedite the completion of
the above-mentioned development projects; and,
b. direct the Municipal Development Council and the implementing
offices to ensure that only projects which are feasible and can
be completed within the budget year are included in the Annual
Development Plan; and ensure that the implementing offices are
provided with adequate manpower and skills in the
implementation of the 20% DF projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure full
implementation of the planned disaster-related PPAs to equip the
Municipality and its constituents in effectively managing the effects of
disaster.

Likewise, we recommended and the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer agreed to submit the monthly Report
on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the
end of each month to facilitate the timely review of the disbursements
charged against the 5% LDRRMF.

We recommend that management exert extra effort to complete the conduct of the physical count of its property, plant and equipment and prepare a complete and detailed report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment, in accordance with Section 124, Volume 1 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume 1.

We recommend that the physical inventory report should be reconciled with the balances in the books of accounts by the personnel concerned. This is to ensure the accuracy and validity of the balances of the asset accounts reflected in the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies, medicines, agriculture and marine supplies and other supplies and materials regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period as inventory, except those purchased through petty cash fund, following the perpetual inventory method and an inventory control account should be maintained for each inventory item pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume 1.
It is also recommended the General Services Officer to maintain stock cards and property cards for supplies, property, plant and equipment and consolidate weekly the Requisition and Issue Slip for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI). The SSMI with the original copy of the RIS shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method as provided under Section 115 of the same manual and prepare JEV to record the expenditures using the appropriate expense accounts.
Thus, we recommend the Municipal Accountant to effect proper classification of accounts to present more accurately the balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that management study the packages offered by mobile networks and evaluate which would provide reasonable price that would still suit the needs of its officials and employees. We likewise recommend that management refrain from paying excessive expenditures as required under COA Circular No. 2012-003 and see to it that claims are supported with adequate documentation.
Further, consider purchase of cell cards in bulk by the GSO, following the required procurement procedures and distribute the same in accordance with the guidelines.

We recommend that the KALAHI-CIDDS Municipal Coordinating Team be required to submit the accomplishment report which specifically state the man-hours spent and the completed or accomplished deliverables pursuant to DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor stop immediately the approval of payments of honoraria to members of various executive committees considering that membership to such committees do not entail them to receive honoraria and to discontinue granting thereof, pursuant to the cited provisions of government laws, rules and regulations to avoid incurrence of irregular expenditures.

We recommend that management attach to the disbursement vouchers the Monthly Report of Rice Consumption together with the list of detailed prisoners at Buenavista MPS and detailed CAFGU, AFP and SWAT personnel.
Furthermore, resort to NFA as primary supplier for rice subsidy to detailed prisoners at Buenavista MPS and detailed CAFGU, AFP and SWAT personnel.

We recommend that management refrain from sending personnel to trainings and seminars conducted by private institutions without securing authority from DILG. For future similar undertakings and seminars, authorization from the DILG has to be obtained first before attending the said seminar or activity in compliance with the provisions of DILG-MC No.99-64 dated April 23, 1999.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the contractor to fast track the completion of the project and impose applicable sanctions and penalties if warranted.

We recommend the management to adhere to the provisions embodied in COA Circular No. 80-101C to facilitate appropriate action on papers relative to the suspension orders on government infrastructure contracts.

We recommend that the BAC refrain from channeling procurement opportunity to the BBATLEMCO as this is not a semblance of transparency and non-compliant to the principle of competitive bidding.  Henceforth, ensure that procurement activities be aligned with the laws, rules and regulations of RA 9184.

We recommend that management ensure the full utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund and be guided by the afore-cited Joint Memorandum Circular. We also recommend that management with the assistance of the MPDC implement the projects identified within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the people and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices of materials in order to address the needs of the community.

We recommend that management  closely monitor and ensure that the LCPC planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the fund so that the intended purpose  is attained/achieved as required under Section 15 of RA 9344 and Section I and II of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

1.10 Compute depreciation expense for all PPE accounts.
1.11 Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE and devise some measures to
reconcile such report with the accounting records, so that PPE account
balances in the Statement of Financial Position can be validated.
1.12 Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the PPEs with their
beginning balances found in the accounting system which could not be
traced.

2.10 Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards
for each type of supplies, crops and other inventory items, to record
acquisition, issuance and disposal.
2.11 Conduct semestral physical count of inventory items by type and
submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the
Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

3.10 Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct review and validation on
each of the journal entries and records made under Advances and
Other Receivables accounts of the GF and SEF to determine the
correctness and propriety of the subject entries and to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries.

4.7 Require the Municipal Agriculturist to:
a. Prepare and submit the Inventory of the Biological Assets as of
December 31, 2018; and
b. Furnish the Municipal Accountant with documents relative to
additions, distribution, reproduction and death of the breeding
stocks.

5.8 Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to agree on the dates
of submission of the disbursement vouchers, collections and deposits
and liquidation reports from the former Office to the latter’s, to
facilitate timely submission of the said documents from the Accounting
Office to COA as provided under Sec. 39 of PD 1445 and Sec. 7.2.1 of
COA Circular No. 2009-006.

6.6 Advise the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation and
submission of the financial reports and adhere to the prescribed period
for submission of the required reports to facilitate immediate action on
deficiencies noted.
6.7 Moreover, avail of the eNGAS and eBudget from the COA to facilitate
systematic generation of financial reports.

7.11 Strictly refrain from granting additional cash advances if the previous
cash advances were not yet settled.
7.12 Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the erring
accountable officers, including the retired/transferred employees,
requiring them to settle immediately their outstanding cash advances.
7.13 Direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of officers and
employees with unsettled cash advances, pursuant to Section 37 of
Presidential Decree 1445.
7.14 For cash advances of an Accountable Officer whose whereabouts could
no longer be traced, the agency may file a request for authority to
write-off dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016.

8.5 Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of the
required documents to support the propriety and validity of the
transactions.

9.5 Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of the
required documents to support the propriety and validity of the
transactions.

10.5 Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate subsidiary
ledgers for the unexpended balances of LDRRMF by year of transfer,
receipts of NDRRMF by transferring agency, DRRMF from other
LGUs by LGU and other sources by donor.
10.6 Refer to COA Circular No. 2012-002 for the accounting entries to be
used for LDRRMF transactions.
10.7 Moreover, coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer for the prior
years’ unexpended/unobligated balances of LDRRMF.

11.4 Require the BAC/BAC Secretariat to thoroughly and meticulously
review and validate the eligibility of the documents submitted by the
bidders before proceeding to Post Qualification and issuance of the
Notice of Award.

12.8 Comply strictly with the submission of Monthly Report of Official
Travels, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, and completely
accomplish the Driver’s Trip Tickets as prescribed under COA Circular
No. 77-61.

13.6 Stop the payment of prior year’s transaction charged to the current
year’s appropriations as provided in the aforementioned provision.
13.7 Determine in advance all expenses incurred which are due and
demandable in the current year which cannot be processed during the
year to be recorded as Accounts Payable.
13.8 Likewise, the Budget Office and Accountant are advised to be strict in
enforcing the provision.

14.9 Establish a sustainable Sanitary Landfill consistent with the standards
and criteria set under RA 9003 for the protection of the environmental and
human health.
14.10 Considering the high financial requirement of establishing a Sanitary
Landfill, we further recommend that the Agency may look on the idea of
cluster sanitary landfill where adjacent cities/municipalities may together
pool their resources to establish a shared sanitary landfill as suggested in
the same Act.

15.8 Require the GAD Focal Point System to administer the HGDG in the
GAD Plan and Budget, and GAD Accomplishment Report.
15.9 Direct the GAD TWG or Secretariat to use the new formats of the GAD
Plan and Budget, and GAD Accomplishment Report. Refer to Annexes D
and E of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.

16.6 Demand from the contractor the immediate completion of the
construction so that another phase can be started and the purpose of the
community hospital be realized and not hampered.
Create project monitoring team to constantly follow up completion of the
projects.

17.6 Require the LGUs Project Management Team to certify as to when the
damages and missing materials happened so that liability could be
determined (Section 62.2.2.1 or 62.2.3.1 IRR RA 9184).
17.7 Account for the materials being pulled out prior to the road
construction and verify whether those were utilized favorably.
17.8 Require contractor to pay for the liquidated damages.

18.12 Monitor the implementation of the planned and on-going developmental
projects to ensure the attainment of desirable socio-economic development
and environmental management outcomes.
18.13 Follow-up the concerned barangay officials of their location for the MRF
and require the contractor of the potable water system to immediately
conduct hydro testing of pipes, otherwise, the Management shall initiate
the same, for the immediate completion of the desired projects.

19.10 Monitor the non-implemented and delayed projects funded by the
LGSF-AM to ensure immediate delivery of the basic services needed by
the constituents.
19.11 Ensure 100% accomplishment of the projects within the period
prescribed in the contract.

20.6 Take appropriate action to finish the project in order to serve its
purpose and hasten the completion of the required TESDA
accreditation documents.

21.9 Hasten the implementation of the desired projects out from the fund
transfers of the Department of Trade and Industry and monitor
completion of the projects and activities funded by the BUB of DA and
DOLE.

22.9 Ensure the prompt implementation of the planned activities and
projects as incorporated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to increase
the level of disaster preparedness and response capability of the LGU.
22.10 Maximize the use of funds by carefully determining the budgetary
requirements of each PPA during the planning stage. As such, the
Agency would have more available funds for other PPAs identified to be
essential in the full attainment of the purpose/objectives of the
creation of the fund.

23.9 Management coordinate with the source agencies for proper return of
the said unutilized funds.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Property and Supply/General Services Officer (GSO) to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of records of the PPE.
observations.managementAction: The GSO conducted physical inventory of the PPE owned by the Municipality but have in to reconcile with the Accounting records.
observations.reason: For reconciliation with Accounting records. Reiterated in Part 2, Observation No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the accountable officers to fully liquidate and refund the excess cash advance at year end in compliance with existing law, rules and regulations. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of the year for fair presentation of the FS.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances amounting P93,580.00 and P98,800.00 were liquidated on March 26, 2018 and June 18, 2018, respectively.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
 secure authority from the SB to request the COA for the write-off of the unaccounted receivables and those under the Carabao Dispersal Program in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
 require the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in collecting receivables from recipients of SEA loans and send demand letters.
Also, refrain from implementing similar projects if sustainability is not assured.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer coordinated with the MSWDO for the collections of the SEA loans receivable. SB Resolution was secured October 2018 and for submission to COA. SEA Program was no longer implemented by the Municipality.
observations.reason: encountered difficulty in the collections of loans receivable due to financial constraints, change of address of the borrowers and other hindrances that contributed to non-repayments of loans.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer/Accountant to deduct from the salary of concerned officials/employees to ensure collection of the amount which was improperly paid by the Municipality. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all unexpended balances of cash advances are returned or refunded immediately to the collecting officer in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: P22,270.00 was already refunded leaving a balance of P13,088.05. The Municipal Treasurer coordinated with the Municipal Accountant for collection actions.
observations.reason: Not acted upon by the concerned municipal officials. Reiterated in Part 2, Observation No. 4 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to maintain the SLCs and stock cards, respectively, for each commodity/stock and regularly reconcile their records. Also, require the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to submit to the Audit Team the RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively, to ensure the reliability of the reported inventory accounts.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, the Municipal Accountant and the GSO complied and maintained the SLCs and stock cards and submitted the RPCI for submission to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from procuring goods and services through reimbursement and ensure strict implementation of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Procurement of goods and services were thereafter conducted in accordance with R.A. No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 92-389 to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature.
observations.managementAction: All DVs and supporting documents were stamped "PAID" in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer after payment of the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least one percent of their IRA for strengthening and implementation of the PPAs of LCPC and provide programs, projects and activities for the 2018.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality thru the Office of the MPDO complied with the strengthening and implementation of the PPAs under Supplemental Budget Appropriation Ordinance No. 001-2018 dated April 30, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, secure the approval of its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission and develop and operate a sanitary landfill site as final disposal site pursuant to the provisions under the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO is working on the Solid Waste Management Plan and submitted revisions; the Municipality has received DENR funds for closure and rehabilitation of the controlled dump site and is implementing related projects.
observations.reason: The 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan was re-submitted and awaiting approval by the NSWMC; funds from DENR for closure and rehabilitation of the controlled dump site are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to remit all taxes withheld within the prescribed period in order for the Government to utilize the same and to free the officials concerned of any liability in terms of surcharges that may be imposed by the BIR.
observations.managementAction: Taxes withheld were remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period.

The Municipal Accountant cited that part of the outstanding each advances were already liquidated except those who were no longer in service or are no longer employed with the municipality and that demand letters was sent accordingly to all concerned.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 4, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of action: the Local Chief Executive as the primarily responsible for all the government funds and property of the municipality to reorganize the Inventory Committee and issue a Memorandum reprimanding the members of the committee that disregarded the directive to conduct physical inventory of PPE; require the Inventory Committee to expedite the conduct of physical inventory taking and the MTO to reconcile the records with the Accounting Office to ascertain the reliability of the PPE accounts and to identify and segregate those that are still serviceable from unserviceable and submit the physical inventory report thereon to COA not later than January 31 of each year; and create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee responsible for the identification and evaluation of reported unserviceable properties. The committee report includes properly accomplishment of Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and determination of the most reasonable appraisal which shall be the basis of their recommendation to the Local Chief Executive, who in turn shall request for the disposal of unserviceable properties to COA in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Hired casual/job order worker to effectively implant the executive order no. 2018-26; Sent letter to all department heads concerning the creation of inventory committee council and inform them of the supposed inventory of PPEs; conducted inventory council meeting to initially discussed the ongoing inventory; required each department heads to revised the submitted report of inventory; collected the revised inventory reports; and transmit to MTO and Accounting the revised report of each department for proper reconciliation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 8, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: expedite the implementation of programs and projects to benefit the intended beneficiaries and to avoid fast increasing prices of supplies and materials.  Initiate representation with the source agencies for unimplemented projects whose funds are not viable to succeed due to high cost of materials and request approval for them to use the fund to augment other priority programs and projects of the municipality; and require immediate remittance of the unexpended balances of projects that are already completed to the National Treasury as required under Executive Order No. 431, Section 122 of GAAM, Volume I and Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied implementation of programs and projects.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 9, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: observe provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 9344 also known as the Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006; and allocate the required amount equivalent to one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the strengthening and implementation of the programs of the LCPC.
observations.managementAction: Local Finance Committee set aside full 1% appropriation for MCPC Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management through the initiative of the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to close the depository accounts with the PNB and transfer the same to an existing account maintained with the LBP-Boac Branch.
observations.managementAction: The Management transferred to LBP-Boac Branch Cash-in-Bank amounting to ₱19,671,041.31 previously maintained in PNB-Boac Branch dated September 12, 2018.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 2, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to: compute the corresponding income/withholding tax on claims for overtime rendered by municipal employees and cause immediate remittance of the same with the Bureau of Internal Revenue; and submit the corresponding accomplishment report, approved DTR and duly approved authority to render overtime.
observations.managementAction: Attached accomplishment report and DTR in the claim of overtime.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 15, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize and expedite the preparation of the Municipality’s ten-year Solid Waste Management Plan in accordance with the requirements set forth under Republic Act No. 9003; ensure active participation of all households, commercial businesses/establishments, institutions and market vendors in segregation and proper waste disposal at source; take appropriate actions to expedite the necessary improvement of the existing Material Recovery Facility (MRF) of the Municipality to make it operational; prioritize the implementation of programs and projects identified in the Solid Waste Management Plan and Annual Investment Plan and consider procurement of machineries and equipment pertaining to solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: The Management secured the ten-year Solid Waste Management Plan as required under Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 16, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We therefore reiterate our recommendation that (i) the management pursue extensive collection of water service fee from delinquent water subscribers to augment the income generation of the municipality and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, being the concerned official, must take appropriate actions on the matter; and (ii) the Office of the Municipal Engineer must see to it that all subscribers are installed with water meters.  Once installed, require periodic inspection and calibration to ensure their reliability and accuracy to determine accurate consumption of every household.

We recommended and Management agreed that the: HRMO personnel (i) review the entries in the leave cards as against the entries in the Daily Time Records to present the accurate balance of leave; and (ii) require the submission of the approved Request for Leave for monetization of leave credits exceeding 10 days; Municipal Budget Officer refund the amount corresponding to the 15 days leave credits monetized in December 2014 but not deducted from the balance; Municipal Accountant refund his claims of monetization without basis since he intentionally did not submit DTRs from which to extract his records of leave credits; and Local Chief Executive file administrative charges against the (i) in-charge of maintaining leave cards if found to have conspired with the claimants who are not entitled to monetize leave credits and guilty of not deducting monetized leave credits; and (ii) Municipal Accountant for being remiss and negligent of his duty. It is his primary responsibility is to take charge of both the accounting and internal audit services of the local government unit concerned and shall, among others, review supporting documents before preparation of vouchers to determine completeness of requirements.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no. 10, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer: submit justification for failure to provide appropriation for monetization of leave credits for all departments and for certifying existence of appropriation on various claims other than the Office of the Mayor; ensure provision of appropriations to all departments in ensuing year; and limit the grant of monetization of leave credits within the approved appropriation.

Despite our repeated follow-up on the above deficiencies, still the Management failed to comply with Audit Team’s recommendations thus, we reiterated the following: require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and BAC Secretariat to submit all supporting documents relative to the procurement of the subject service vehicle; require the BAC, its Secretariat and Technical Working Group to strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations and ensure completeness of the documents submitted by bidders as prescribed under Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in all procurement activities of the Municipality; instruct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to review the process, relative to procurement activities of the local government unit, being undertaken by the BAC before processing and/or signing the claims of suppliers/dealers and make it a point to stop the payment of transactions with incomplete documentation; and make representation with the supplier to refund the corresponding VAT/EWT amounting to P85,260.00 supposed to be deducted from the gross purchase amount of the service vehicle.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all department heads to conduct immediately physical inventory of all PPE owned by the Municipality found in their respective offices including those issued to officials and employees.  Also, assign one personnel to consolidate per office inventory reports in the RPCPPE to be submitted to the accounting office for reconciliation in their records, and submit a copy of RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor for audit.

We recommended that:

The Local Chief Executive require the concerned officers and employees who are currently employed with the LGU to liquidate their outstanding cash advances immediately thru submission of liquidation report with supporting documents or pay the amount of the unliquidated cash advance.  Likewise, exert extra effort to locate and resend demand letters to those who are no longer connected with the LGU; 

The Municipal Accountant withhold the salaries of concerned personnel who are currently employed with the LGU and f failed to comply with the instructions on liquidation of their long outstanding cash advances as provided under Section 16 of Executive Order No. 298 dated March 23, 2004; and


The Municipal Accountant evaluate the status of long outstanding cash advances and file a request for write-off, if necessary, following the guidelines and procedures on write-off provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the:

Municipal Mayor as head of the procuring entity, ensure that all procurement activities of the Municipality be done in compliance with RA 9184, its revised IRR and GPPB issuance updates.  Reimbursements on non-petty and non-recurring expenses aligned with programs, projects and activities be stopped. Procurement on programmed expenditures not more than the threshold of P200,000.00 be undertaken thru small value procurement, or in case of unforeseen contingency requiring immediate purchase, shopping 52.1 (a); and

Municipal Government set-up petty cash fund sufficient for the recurring petty operating and emergency expenses of the Municipality for one month stated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 to address numerous reimbursements on recurring petty and emergency expenses.  Follow the guidelines on creating and maintaining of petty cash fund provided under Section 48 of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs Volume I.

We recommended that the Local/Municipal Development Council review its procedure in the appropriation of the Municipality’s 20% development fund and include only in the Local Development Plan projects/programs defined and enumerated under the above cited Circular.  Henceforth, comply with the new guideline issued by the DILG and DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the annual IRA for development projects (DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017).

We recommended that granting of special cash advance for field operating activity other than what is prescribed under Section 3.2 of the COA NO. 97-002 be stopped.  Only personnel services (salaries, wages, honorarium and allowances), and maintenance and operating expenses which is impractical to pay by check can be paid out of special cash advance of authorized personnel.

We recommended that the proposed adjustments in the GL as shown in the tables in paragraph A and B be recorded in the books to correct the overstatement of cash in bank balance in the GF and the understatement in MEEDO Fund.  Journal entry vouchers for adjustments made in the books be prepared and submitted to the office of the Auditor.

We recommended the accounting office communicate with the concerned depository banks the long outstanding bank reconciling items appearing in the Bank Reconciliation Statements for necessary adjustments in bank’s records. Likewise, revalidate all reconciling items specifically those that are long outstanding including errors in recording and returned checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Government through concerned department heads ensure that provisions on the period of implementation of BuB projects be provided in each project proposal and in the Memorandum of Agreement so that timeliness in projects implementation can be properly monitored.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant demand from NGOs/POs the unliquidated fund transfers as of December 31, 2016 through submission of the Fund Utilization Report together with the required supporting documents for proper evaluation and recording in the books; and

Submit liquidation or fund utilization reports with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Government establish separate and specific policies and procedures or guidelines on the use/operation of the Emergency Medical Service vehicles.  The guideline shall provide among others the following provisions to ensure smooth implementation of the program and avoid abusive use of vehicles:
•	Types of emergency cases covered
•	Scope of services of EMS
•	Areas covered
•	Duties and Responsibilities of MDRRMO on supervision of EMS
•	Qualifications in hiring of personnel to be assigned in the field
•	Duties and Responsibilities of assigned personnel
•	Responsibility of persons transported
•	Payment of services
•	Limitations on the use of EMS
•	Limitations on services
•	Authority on the use of EMS, and;
•	Administrative sanctions

We recommended that the Municipal Government include in its DRRM Plan the construction of adequate drainage system, riprapping of creeks or rivers and other infrastructures projects that would maximize the prevention and mitigation of the direct impacts of calamity and reduce vulnerabilities and exposure of communities to all hazards.

We recommended that the municipality appropriate funds for mandatory positions responsible for Administration and Training, and Operations and Warning and fill up the same in compliance with the guidelines on creation of LDRRM Office in the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant support the Land account with subsidiary ledgers and documents such as certificates of title establishing legal ownership thereto. Management should exercise due diligence in obtaining legal documents and process titling of unsupported real properties owned by the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant should also disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the composition and valuation of the Land account.

We recommend that disbursements out of the SEF be in consonance to the provisions of RA 7160 (The Local Government Code) and to pertinent implementing rules and regulations. The Municipal School Board shall give priority to programs, projects and activities enumerated in the afore-mentioned regulations. Financial assistance and membership dues of pupils and students to Boy Scouts of the Philippines and Girl Scouts of the Philippines, as well as expenditures of the academe during Tiringbanay Festival should not be charged out of the SEF.

We recommend that the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 and the individual MOA be strictly complied in the grant, utilization and accounting of fund transfers to the NGOs/POs and the immediate liquidation of fund transfers out of the SEF be enforced. Additional cash advances should not be granted to NGOs/POs who have not liquidated their previous cash advances. All transfers should be recorded in Due from NGOs/POs (Account 139) and should only be credited once the fund utilization reports with all the supporting documents are submitted and after the Municipal Accountant has determined the validity and propriety of the transaction.

We recommended that expenditures out of the 70% component of the LDRRMF should be utilized only for programs, projects and activities on disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness and disaster response as provided in RA 10121 and its IRR, and related DILG, DBM and NDRRMC issuances.  The Municipality should prioritize appropriating funds for the maintenance and operating expenses of the MDRRMO under the General Fund in compliance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular 2014-1.

We recommended that management require the concerned barangays and the Liga to submit fund utilization reports in accordance with the provisions of the MOA. We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer draw checks in the name of the agency or in the official title or designation of its head or treasurer. Management should likewise conduct proper planning, budgeting and coordination to avoid delays and other problems that may arise in the execution of its programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 10121 and its IRR, and COA Circular 2012-002 in the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF. The unexpended LDRRMF should be transferred to a special trust fund in the Trust Fund Books of the LGU. The Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF should be timely submitted to the Office of the Auditor monthly.

We recommended strict policy on the timely submission of DTRs should be issued and implemented. Heads of Departments of the Municipality should exercise due diligence in monitoring timely submission of DTRs by their subordinates. Failure to submit the DTRs and its supporting documents shall warrant the withholding of the salaries of employees concerned.  Moreover, disciplinary action should be instituted against non-complying officials and employees following laws and rules in the Civil Service.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant investigate and exert extra efforts to review all the documents which are available in his office. In addition, he should interview some of the personnel who are still assigned in the Office before his assumption to get some information regarding these undocumented accounts.

We recommend that LGU officials, the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel in charge of the disbursement and monitoring of the financial assistance should require the recipient NGO/PO– Association of Tiringbanay Cooperators Inc. and Coast Haven Inc. to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2007-001 to ascertain the attainment of objectives for which the funds were released as well as the propriety of expenditures.
We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to:
observations.managementAction: 1. Validate all records available by conducting actual physical count per department; 2. Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer in reconciling the RPCPPE with the accounting records; 3. Furnish the Municipal Accountant with a copy of reconciled RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor agreed to take the necessary measures to immediately update and reconcile their individual records, in order that the amount due and collectible for the year per taxpayer could easily be determined and furnish the Municipal Accountant with a certified list of taxpayers as basis for setting up the RPT Receivables and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.
We recommended and Management agreed that: the responsible offices submit the Accomplishment Report (AR) for CY 2017 and henceforth submit the same within the prescribed period.

We recommended and Management agreed to: a. require the responsible officers to scout for a site viable for the project and facilitate the immediate transfer of ownership; b. follow-up the approval of the schematic plans from DOH to hasten the implementation and completion of the BUB projects; and c. for succeeding fund transfers, execute a MOA with the Source Agency containing, among others, provision on the prescribed timelines in the execution/implementation of BUB projects so as to provide a sense of urgency in project execution.

We recommended and Management agreed that: a. the Municipal Accountant submit to the Municipal Mayor the complete list of loan beneficiaries supported with available copies of MOAs, whenever available; b. representation be made with the Punong Barangays for the determination of the addresses of the beneficiaries to facilitate the issuance of Demand and Collection Letter; c. a request for write-off be prepared together with their supporting documents and be submitted to COA for approval; and d. a time table be set to comply with the aforementioned recommendations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant ensure that cash advances for payroll are supported with approved payrolls with their net payments as basis for drawing cash advances.
We recommended and Management agreed to make written representation with the LTO to follow-up the release of government plate for dump truck.

We recommend that management direct the Accounting Office to submit all the unaccounted original vouchers and supporting documents immediately to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise disbursement pertaining thereto shall be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We also recommend that henceforth, the Local Chief Executive impose on the Municipal Accountant the timely submission of accounts to COA to comply with the provision of Section 100 and 107 of P.D. 1445 and Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA).
observations.managementAction: DVs were submitted to COA Provincial Office. And will be submitted monthly to the resident COA auditor

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording the transactions of the agency pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts as prescribed in the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS). We recommend that the Municipal Accountant stop the practice of recording under the General Services account the payment for services rendered by employees contracted/hired to perform regular agency functions such as substitute and part-time teachers of the BCC and those performing clerical functions in the LGU. Their salaries/wages ought to be taken up under the account Salaries and Wages-Casual/ Contractual as prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts of the PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant records as per obligated appropriation which belongs to MOOE General Services and not in the PS. They are also job order in nature so we are recording it to the account. The local finance conferred to come up your recommendation to the Board of Trustees to change the item from MOOE to PS the most appropriate account. Change the budget from MOOE to PS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the contracts of Job Order Workers shall contain statements of duties and responsibilities or equivalent terms to serve as standard of which to base their accomplishments which shall also be required to support their claims of wages.
observations.managementAction: Written information for job orders specific job descriptions

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the appointed LDRRM Officer to immediately assume her duties and responsibilities at the LDRRM Office in the compliance with NDRRMC- DILG- CSC JMC 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 and pursuant to RA 10121 (Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010).
observations.managementAction: LDDRRMO works was complied by the MDRRMO designate.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) in coordination with the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to clearly specify the priority projects and programs that shall funded out of the 20% Development Fund and to stop the practice
observations.managementAction: Realigned the projects and programs with specific details in accordance with DILG MC 96-263 as amended 2017 in Utilization of 20% DF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure that the 20% Development Fund shall be utilized only for those PPAs covered under section 3 of the DILG- DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01.

We therefore recommend that the appropriations allotted for the Landscaping and Beautification Project be realigned to other PPAs that conform with Environmental Management outcomes as contemplated in the JMC and focus on providing the basic services and facilities of solid waste management in the municipality.

Moreover, we recommend that formation of the Waste Management Board be initiated to formulate the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan that shall ensure the long-term management of solid waste, as well as integrate the various solid waste management plans and strategies of the Barangays in its area of jurisdiction.
observations.managementAction: Realigned the projects and programs with specific details in accordance with DILG MC 96-263 as amended 2017 in Utilization of 20% DF.

observations.recommendation:
To protect the interest of the government and to prevent further loss and wastage of government funds resources, we recommend that Management immediately apply the remedies available as provided in Section 8.4 of R.A 9184 for the unjustified delay in the completion of the project, which is, to rescind the contract, forfeit the contractor’s performance security and takeover the prosecution of the project or award the same to a qualified contractor through negotiated contract.

Otherwise, LGU officials responsible in the proper implementation of the project may be held liable on the impending loss and wastage of government funds spent on the project for their negligence or non-action, as provided in Section 102 and 103 of the State Auditing Government Code as afore-quoted.
observations.managementAction: No action from management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman/Secretariat to furnish the COA Office with copies of the perfected contracts/ purchase orders together with the complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. Every revision made to the contract such as contract time extensions, extra work orders or variation orders shall also be furnished to the Auditor for appropriate review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: No action from the management.

CY 2017 accounts and reports were not submitted within the period prescribed by the Commission

observations.recommendation: 26. We recommended that the municipality revisit the guidelines on appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the Annual Internal Revenue Allotment for development projects. Only those projects that can contribute to the attainment of socio-economic and environmental development of the municipality as defined under the cited joint circular be funded out the 20% IRA.

Expense items not related to development projects were still included in CY 2016 20% development fund budget
