Reiterated in Audit Finding No. 5/Page 32 of this year’s AAR.

observations.recommendation: 27. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive always ensure that only eligible NGOs/POs will be granted cash advances thru strict compliance to documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.  Also, always ensure that only projects/activities within the capability of the NGO/PO and in line with the SEF priority expenses as provided under DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, 01-A, and 01-B are granted to NGO/PO. Further, cause the liquidation of the above subject cash advances and stop releasing funds to NGOs/POs with unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason:
CY 2016 Special Education Fund transfers to NGOs/POs were not supported with complete required documentations. Only project proposals and Memorandum of Agreement were attached to the voucher.
The finding was first raised in CY 2015 AAR

Also, persons responsible submit to the office of the Auditor lacking local travel documents enumerated above in compliance with Section 1.2.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for local travels listed in Annex 3 of this report.

The required documents were not submitted to the Auditor

The finding was first raised in CY 2015 AAR

Letter from the Municipal Mayor informing the auditor that the appropriate signboard is already posted was not received in CY 2017.

We recommend that The Chief Executive issue a policy and the Sanggunian to pass a resolution with regard to mobile telephone usage and expenditures, review and set allocated amount of mobile telephone expenses allowed to elected and appointed officials with justifications, and course the procurement of cellphone call cards through the BAC and be guided with the provisions of RA 9184 and its revised IRR.

Disbursements of cellphone load allowances were still made thru reimbursement basis.

observations.recommendation: 43. We recommend that prudence and judiciousness should always be observed in the disbursement of funds for travelling and training expenses. Only necessary travels and trainings should be allowed.  The Human Resources and Development Office under the Municipal General Services Office should develop an annual comprehensive training and development plan for all the employees of the Municipality which will be the basis of allowed trainings and seminars for the year.
observations.reason: The Municipality has no annual comprehensive training program.

observations.recommendation: 44. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant investigate and exert extra efforts to review all the documents which are available in his office. In addition, he should interview some of the personnel who are still assigned in the Office before his assumption in order to get some information regarding these undocumented accounts.
observations.reason: These accounts remain unsupported by subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: 45. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer be directed to strictly adhere to the accounting policies on inventories as provided for in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs). The Perpetual Inventory System should be strictly followed as well as the Moving Average Method in costing inventories. Stock Cards, Supplies Ledger Cards and the Physical Count of Inventories should be maintained and reconciled. Physical count of inventories should be conducted semestrally and a report thereof should be made.
observations.reason:
The Perpetual Inventory System is not followed
The finding was first raised in CY 2008 AAR.

observations.recommendation: 47. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, and other concerned department heads and LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and implementation of the funds require the recipient NGO/PO – SAME to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 96-003 as amended by COA Circular No. 2007-001 and to ascertain the propriety of expenditures as specified in the Memorandum of Agreement.
observations.reason: SAME has not submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to: 4. conduct physical count of the hospitals, health centers and markets based on the schedule of the Accounting Office that contains the locations of these PPE items; 5. coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer in locating the transaction documents and information/data of the Other Land Improvements in CY 2012 and School Building in CY 2015; and, 6. furnish the Municipal Accountant with the CY 2016 RPCPPE. Likewise, we also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the recording of the Dump Truck from the Motor Vehicles account to Construction and Heavy Equipment account in the amount of P6,360,000.00.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor agreed to furnish the Municipal Accountant with a certified list of taxpayers as basis for setting up the RPT Receivables and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended and Management agreed that: 4) the salaries of the active employee whose cash advances as of December 31, 2011 remained unliquidated, despite final demand, be suspended immediately pursuant to Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004; 5) in case of deceased employee/s, initiate write-off of unliquidated cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and, 6) the Municipal Accountant send demand letters through registered mail to the last known home address of those retired/resigned employees; and, 7) follow up or send tracers to the Commission on Audit Central Office on the action to the submitted liquidations of cash advances for Confidential and Intelligence Fund.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: 1. Regularly provide the Provincial Accountant with data on the Province’s share in the real property and other tax collections made by the Municipality; 2. Trace the details of the fund transfers by requesting copies of Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) of the Province; 3. Submit liquidation documents for fund transfers recorded by the Province as Due from LGUs; and 4. Conduct periodic reconciliation with the Provincial Accountant.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant ensure that cash advances for payroll are supported with their approved payrolls with their net payments as basis for drawing cash advances.
We recommended and Management agreed to increase the amount of bond of the accountable officer to ensure the full coverage of her accountability.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the monthly BRS to the Auditor within the reglementary period for timely verification so as to ascertain the correctness of the Cash in Bank account balances reflected in the financial statements and reflect the book reconciling items, if any, in the period to which it pertains.

We recommended and Management agreed that BUB funds from National Government Agencies be utilized fully for the benefit of the constituents and ensure the proper use thereof in accordance with the guidelines under DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7 dated November 3, 2015.
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant submit the disbursement vouchers, payroll, official receipts and financial reports within the prescribed period.

Utilize the SEF appropriately pursuant to the laws governing the said Fund.

Direct the Municipal Budget Officer  and Municipal Accountant to observe tighter control measures to ascertain that government obligations are charged appropriately against the respective budgets for proper and correct reporting.
observations.managementAction: Disbursements mentioned in this observation were corrected, however, similar observation re: disbursements not aligned with allowable SEF expenses were incurred.

observations.recommendation:
Strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act 9003 in proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste. 

Ensure the implementation / enforcement of the programs, projects and activities embodied in the Municipality’s ESWM Plan pursuant to Republic Act 9003, such as:
a.Education and Public Information
b.Segregation at source of solid waste;
c.“No segregation, no collection” policy;
d.Creation of Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) in every barangay; and
e.Development of disposal facility in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The same observations were noted.

observations.recommendation: Intensify efforts in accomplishing the documentary requirements as enumerated under CHED Memorandum Order No. 32, series of 2016; and | AAR 2016 | Ensure that documentary requirements be in place before proceeding to the next phase of the project.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct review and validation on each of the journal entries made under Advances and Other Receivables accounts to determine the correctness and propriety of the subject entry and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
We recommended that Management coordinate with the source agencies for proper return of the said unutilized.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify collections of real property and special education taxes due from the delinquent taxpayers by adopting measures and devising new strategies to improve collection efficiency.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the Management to:
instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and effect necessary adjustment to record the (i) audit disallowances totaling ₱5,067,961.36 which had become final and executory and (ii) the cash shortage of the former Municipal Treasurer totaling ₱611,898.03; and
require the Municipal Accountant to furnish this office the list of payments/settlements for the eventual issuance of the corresponding NSSDC.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
provide allowance for impairment for dormant receivables and cash advance accounts which had been in the books for more than 10 years in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statements as required under PPSAS 29. Compute/determine impairment in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards or Philippine Financial Reporting Standards; and
comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the possible request for the write off of the specified dormant receivables and cash advances accounts to the Commission on Audit.

We recommended the following courses of action:
the Local Chief Executive as the primarily responsible for all the government funds and property of the municipality to reorganize the Inventory Committee and issue a Memorandum reprimanding the members of the committee that disregarded the directive to conduct physical inventory of PPE;
require the Inventory Committee to expedite the conduct of physical inventory taking and the MTO to reconcile the records with the Accounting Office to ascertain the reliability of the PPE accounts and to identify and segregate those that are still serviceable from unserviceable and submit the physical inventory report thereon to COA not later than January 31 of each year; and
create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee responsible for the identification and evaluation of reported unserviceable properties. The committee report includes properly accomplishment of Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and determination of the most reasonable appraisal which shall be the basis of their recommendation to the Local Chief Executive, who in turn shall request for the disposal of unserviceable properties to COA in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

In view of the above, we recommended and Management agreed to:
expedite the implementation of the project to benefit the intended beneficiaries and to avoid fast increasing prices of supplies and materials.
initiate representation with the source agencies for unimplemented projects whose funds are not viable to succeed due to high cost of materials and request approval for them to use the fund to augment other priority programs and projects of the municipality; and
require  immediate  remittance  of  the unexpended balances of  projects that are already completed to the National Treasury as  required  under Executive Order No. 431, Section 122 of GAAM, Volume I and Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and ensure implementation and completion of the projects identified for the benefit of its constituents; and
strictly adhere to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Community Development Fund to ensure efficient output, come up with projects with developmental impact that would improve the socio-economic condition of the greater number of its constituents.

We recommended the following courses of action:
observe provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 9344 also known as the Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006; and
allocate the required amount equivalent to one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the strengthening and implementation of the programs of the LCPC.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant be directed to submit the required monthly and quarterly financial reports on time as prescribed under Section 70 and 73 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I or as provided by the New Government Accounting Manual (GAM).  Otherwise, the administrative sanctions provided under Section 122 of PD No. 1445 will be implemented.

In view of the above, we recommended that  the Management should see to it that the hiring of individuals under job order are covered with contracts indicating nature, scope and quantity of work to be done. Each job order should be required to submit an accomplishment report prior to the payment of their wages to ensure that government funds are expended accordingly.

We recommended the Management through the initiative of the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to close the depository accounts with the PNB and transfer the same to an existing account maintained with the LBP-Boac Branch.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
compute the corresponding income/withholding tax on claims for overtime rendered by municipal employees and cause immediate remittance of the same with the Bureau of Internal Revenue; and
submit the corresponding accomplishment report, approved DTR and duly approved authority to render overtime.

We therefore reiterate our recommendation that (i) the management pursue extensive collection of water service fee from delinquent water subscribers to augment the income generation of the municipality and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, being the concerned official, must take appropriate actions on the matter; and (ii) the Office of the Municipal Engineer must see to it that all subscribers are installed with water meters.  Once installed, require periodic inspection and calibration to ensure their reliability and accuracy to determine accurate consumption of every household.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer:
submit justification for failure to provide appropriation for monetization of leave credits for all departments and for certifying existence of appropriation on various claims other than the Office of the Mayor;
ensure provision of appropriations to all departments in ensuing year; and
limit the grant of monetization of leave credits within the approved appropriation.

We recommended the following:
submit copies of the contracts/purchase orders together with the supporting documents, for review and technical evaluation of the contract to point out to management the defects of the contracts and the project itself, should there be any, and to effect timely corrective measures; and
require the BAC and the MGSO to ensure that the contract and all its supporting documents, including those required in the auditorial and technical review, be submitted to the Auditor’s Office within five days from perfection thereof as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all department heads to conduct immediately physical inventory of all PPE owned by the Municipality found in their respective offices including those issued to officials and employees.  Also, assign one personnel to consolidate per office inventory reports in the RPCPPE to be submitted to the accounting office for reconciliation in their records, and submit a copy of RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor for audit.

We recommended that:
The Local Chief Executive require the concerned officers and employees who are currently employed with the LGU to liquidate their outstanding cash advances immediately thru submission of liquidation report with supporting documents or pay the amount of the unliquidated cash advance.  Likewise, exert extra effort to locate and resend demand letters to those who are no longer connected with the LGU;
The Municipal Accountant withhold the salaries of concerned personnel who are currently employed with the LGU and failed to comply with the instructions on liquidation of their long outstanding cash advances as provided under Section 16 of Executive Order No. 298 dated March 23, 2004; and
The Municipal Accountant evaluate the status of long outstanding cash advances and file a request for write-off, if necessary, following the guidelines and procedures on write-off provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
We recommended that the Municipal Government adopt a system of payroll payment through the ATMs of banks.

We recommended that the:
a.	Municipal Mayor as head of the procuring entity, ensure that all procurement activities of the Municipality be done in compliance with RA 9184, its revised IRR and GPPB issuance updates.  Reimbursements on non-petty and non-recurring expenses aligned with programs, projects and activities be stopped. Procurement on programmed expenditures not more than the threshold of P200,000.00 be undertaken thru small value procurement, or in case of unforeseen contingency requiring immediate purchase, shopping 52.1 (a); and
b.	Municipal Government set-up petty cash fund sufficient for the recurring petty operating and emergency expenses of the Municipality for one month stated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 in order to address numerous reimbursements on recurring petty and emergency expenses.  Follow the guidelines on creating and maintaining of petty cash fund provided under Section 48 of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs Volume I.

We recommended that the Local/Municipal Development Council review its procedure in the appropriation of the Municipality’s 20% development fund and include only in the Local Development Plan projects/programs defined and enumerated under the above cited Circular.  Henceforth, comply with the new guideline issued by the DILG and DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the annual IRA for development projects (DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017).

We recommended that granting of special cash advance for field operating activity other than what is prescribed under Section 3.2 of the COA NO. 97-002 be stopped.  Only personnel services (salaries, wages, honorarium and allowances), and maintenance and operating expenses which is impractical to pay by check can be paid out of special cash advance of authorized personnel.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the accounting office and other concerned offices to comply with COA rules and regulations and submit accounts and monthly or quarterly reports to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.

We recommended that taxes withheld from salaries and wages of employees and the value added taxes, and expanded withholding taxes from suppliers and contractors be remitted in full to the BIR to avoid fines, penalties and surcharges that the concerned agency may impose.

We recommended that the withheld 9% Personal Share (PS) from the basic salary and loan repayments of its officials and employees together with the 12% Government Share (GS) be remitted in full to GSIS to avoid fines, penalties and surcharges that the concerned agency may impose.

We recommended that the withheld Personal Share (PS) from the basic salary and loan repayments of its officials and employees together with the Government Share (GS) be remitted in full to PAG-IBIG to avoid fines, penalties and surcharges that the concerned agency may impose.

We recommended that the withheld Personal Share (PS) from the basic salary and loan repayments of its officials and employees together with the Government Share (GS) be remitted in full to PHILHEALTH to avoid fines, penalties and surcharges that the concerned agency may impose.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
1) sanctions be imposed on the defaulting officer;
2) in case of deceased employee/s, initiate write-off of unliquidated cash advances in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and,
3) the Municipal Accountant send demand letters through registered mail to the last
known home address of those retired/resigned employees.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to:
1. Validate all records available by conducting actual physical count per department;
2. Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer in reconciling
the RPCPPE with the accounting records; and,
3. Furnish the Municipal Accountant with a copy of reconciled RPCPPE.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor agreed to
take the necessary measures to immediately update and reconcile their individual
records, in order that the amount due and collectible for the year per taxpayer could easily be determined
and furnish the Municipal Accountant with a certified list of taxpayers as basis for setting up the
RPT Receivables and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.

Likewise, immediately consult with the developer as to the problems encountered with
the system and enhance the same so that reports generated will be useful and can be relied
upon by the users.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. regularly provide the Provincial Accountant with data on the Province’s share in the
real property and other tax collections made by the Municipality;
b. remit the valid current due Province’s share in real property and other taxes which
remained unremitted by the Municipality;
c. trace the details of the fund transfers by requesting copies of Journal Entry
Vouchers (JEVs) of the Province; and
d. submit liquidation documents for fund transfers recorded by the Province as Due
from LGUs.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit a copy of the Accomplishment
Report for CY 2017 to the Audit Team and henceforth, submit the same within the
prescribed period.

Likewise, attach the required supporting documents to substantiate the payment of
honorarium including the computation on how the amount was arrived pursuant to DBM Budget
Circular No. 2007-1.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
1. the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer and the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator ensure that the planned PPAs for disaster
risk management are fully implemented on target implementation period to ensure
the Municipality’s preparedness to natural and man-made hazards/disasters;
2. ensure that only pre-disaster preparedness and mitigation PPAs be funded under
the 70% allocation of the MDRRMF and only authorized PPAs be charged
against the fund;
3. the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer comply with the
monthly submission of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before
the 15th day after the end of each month; and,
4. hire or designate qualified personnel to occupy the other two (2) vacant staff
position of the LDRRMO in accordance with item 5.5 of the JMC.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. require the Engineering office to review the unimplemented projects if it is still viable
to implement as programmed, if not, consider re-aligning the same for more feasible
projects which have a better impact to its constituents;
b. direct the Municipal Development Council to ensure that the development projects
identified in the ADP are feasible and can be implemented within the year with the
proposed budget allocation;
c. require the implementation of the development projects within the time schedule as
targeted in the ADP and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund; and,
d. cause the submission of the necessary supporting documents identified in foregoing
table.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. require the responsible officers to scout for a site viable for the project and facilitate
the immediate transfer of ownership;
b. follow-up the approval of the schematic plans from the DOH to hasten the
implementation and completion of the BUB projects; and,
c. for succeeding fund transfers, execute a MOA with the Source Agency containing,
among others, provision on the prescribed timelines in the execution /
implementation of BUB projects so as to provide a sense of urgency in project
execution.

We recommended and Management agreed to comply with the requirements of Section
39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445 and other existing law, rules and regulations on the submission of
accounts, financial records and reports, specifically:
1. The Accountant to strictly abide with the submission of accounting records reports
within the deadline;
2. The Inventory Committee to furnish copy of the Report of Physical Count of PPE to
the Accountant and COA Audit Team within the deadline; and,
3. The Municipal Engineer to provide report on the cost of completed projects and the
status of on-going projects likewise submission of detailed cost of components of
all infrastructure projects, among others.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that paying capacity of each
employee borrower above the minimum mandated net take home pay should be
considered prior to the approval of loan applications.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that Management direct the Accounting Office to submit all the unaccounted original vouchers and supporting documents immediately to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise disbursements pertaining thereto shall be suspended or disallowed in audit.
We also recommend that henceforth, the Local Chief Executive impose on the Municipal Accountant the timely submission of accounts to COA to comply with the provision of Section 100 and 107 of P.D. 1445 andSection 7.2.1 of the 2009 Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA).

RECOMMENDATION
To protect the interest of the government and to prevent further loss and wastage of government funds and resources, we recommend that Management immediately apply the remedies available as provided in Section 8.4 of R.A. 9184 for the unjustified delay in the completion of the project, which is, to rescind the contract, forfeit the contractor's performance security and takeover the prosecution of the project or award the same to a qualified contractor through negotiated contract.
Otherwise, LGU officials responsible in the proper implementation of the project may be held liable on the impending loss and wastage of government funds spent on the project for their negligence or non-action, as provided in Section 102 and 103 of the State Auditing Government Code as afore-quoted.
We recommend further that, LGU officials have to  provide safeguards against delinquent and irresponsible contractors.  The project engineer has to monitor and require the contractor to submit Statement of Work Accomplished on a monthly basis and ensure that prosecution of the project is in accordance with the timelines set in the contract. Requests for contract time extension may be granted or allowed only for justifiable reasons as provided in Section 9.2 of Annex D of the IRR of RA 9184.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman/ Secretariat to furnish the COA Office with copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders together with the complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.  Every revision made to the contract such as contract time extensions, extra work orders or variation orders shall also be furnished to the Auditor for appropriate review and evaluation.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording the transactions of the agency pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts as prescribed in the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).  We recommend that the Municipal Accountant stop the practice of recording under the General Services account the payment for services renderedby employees contracted/hired to perform regular agency functions such as the substitute and part-time teachers of the BCC and those performing clerical functions in the LGU.  Their salaries/wages ought to be taken up under the account Salaries and Wages- Casual/Contractual as prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts of the PPSAS.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that the contracts of Job Order workers shall contain statements of duties and responsibilities or equivalent terms to serve as standard of which to base their accomplishment which shall also be required to support their claims of wages.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) in coordination with the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to clearly specify the priority projects and programs that shall be funded out of the 20% Development Fund and to stop the practice of allocating lump sum appropriations from the fund. Instruct the MPDC and the BAC to prepare the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) so that appropriate timelines shall be followed in the procurement of projects and programs that shall be funded from the Fund.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, the Municipal Engineer and the BAC to closely coordinate on the immediate implementation of the priority development projects, programs and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund during the year the funds are appropriated in order to ensure the attainment of its desired result.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure that the 20% Development Fund shall be utilized only for those PPAs covered under Section 3 of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01.
We therefor recommend that  the appropriations allotted for the Landscaping and Beautification Project be realigned to other PPAs that conform with Environmental Management outcomes as contemplated in the JMC and focus on providing the basic services and facilities of solid waste management in the municipality.
Moreover, we recommend that  formation of the WasteManagement Board be initiated to formulate the Municipal  Solid Waste Management Plan that shall ensure the long-term management of solid waste, as well as integrate the various solid waste management plans and strategies of the barangays in its area of jurisdiction.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that Management require the appointed LDRRM Officer to immediately assume her duties and responsibilities at theLDRRM Office in compliance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 and pursuant toRA 10121 (Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010) .

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that management require the LDRRM Officer to prepare and submit the LDRRMF Investment Plan at the beginning of the year and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified by the Municipal Accountant to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, for audit and verification.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that Management refrain from releasing more funds to these leagues unless the required official receipts of the government are issued by the recipient Leagues concerned for proper accounting and uphold observance of accountability and transparency of all government transactions.
In any case, the LGU has to demand proper accounting and liquidation reports from the concerned leagues as to how the funds released were utilized.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that management establish a Local Council for the Protection of Children and require appropriation of at least 1% of the IRA for the implementation of LCPC programs.

Prepare the RPCPPE by classifying the PPE according to their type for purposes of comparison/reconciliation with the general ledger balances.
Compute depreciation expense for all PPE accounts.
Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE so that reconciliation with accounting records can be attained, and so that reliability of the value of PPE in the Statement of Financial Position can be ascertained.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct review and validation on each of the journal entries made under Advances and Other Receivables accounts to determine the correctness and propriety of the subject entries and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct review and validation on each of the reconciling items to determine the nature/detail/breakdown of these items and make the necessary adjusting entries in order to present correct balances of the Prior Period Adjustment account and other affected accounts in the Financial Statements.

Strictly refrain from granting additional cash advances if the previous cash advances were not yet settled.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the erring accountable officers, including retired/transferred employees to settle their outstanding cash advances immediately.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of officers and employees with unsettled cash advances, pursuant to Section 37 of Presidential Decree 1445.

Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to agree on the dates of submission of the disbursement vouchers, collections and deposits and liquidation reports from the former Office to the latter’s, to facilitate timely submission of the said documents from the Accounting Office to COA as provided under Sec. 39 of PD 1445 and Sec. 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

Advise the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to hasten the preparation and submission of the financial reports and SAAOB and adhere to the prescribed period for submission of the required reports to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.
Moreover, upgrade the computerization of the Accounting and Budget Offices, respectively.

Monitor regularly the progress made by the implemented SWM programs, projects and activities.
Strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act 9003 in proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste, and use of sanitary landfill.

Ensure that the SEF shall be utilized solely for its purpose; such as programs, projects and activities enumerated in Section 272 of RA 7160, DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

Direct the MSWDO to attend the training/workshop on how to administer the HGDG tool or invite an expert that could perform gender analysis using the said tool.
Require the GAD Focal Point System to administer the HGDG in the GAD Plan and Budget, and GAD Accomplishment Report and use the new formats as prescribed under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.

Monitor closely the implementation of planned developmental programs and projects to ensure the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.  Follow-up and monitor those projects with documentary requirements to enable their implementation.
Ensure the implementation of the planned activities and projects as incorporated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to increase the level of disaster preparedness and response capability of the LGU.

Intensify efforts in accomplishing the requirements for the establishment of the institution to avoid possible wastage of scarce resources by abandoning the project and so that the deserving constituents can benefit from the project.
Submit updates or documents on the status of the application for TESDA.
Advise the Municipal Engineer to meticulously prepare and review all ABCs and POWs for the contract of Infrastructure Projects to avoid excessive expenditures in the future.
Coordinate with the source agencies for proper return of the said unutilized funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Property and
Supply/General Services Officer (GSO) to coordinate with the Municipal
Accountant for the reconciliation of records of the PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the accountable
officers to fully liquidate and refund the excess cash advance at year end in
compliance with existing law, rules and regulations. Also, require the Municipal
Accountant to see to it that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of the
year for fair presentation of the FS.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:

> secure authority from the SB to request the COA for the write-off of
the unaccounted receivables and those under the Carabao Dispersal
Program in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016;

» require the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in collecting
receivables from recipients of SEA loans and send demand letters;
and

» refrain from implementing similar projects if sustainability is not
assured.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer/Accountant to deduct from the salary of concerned
officials/employees to ensure collection of the amount which was improperly
paid by the Municipality. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it
that all unexpended balances of cash advances are returned or refunded
immediately to the collecting officer in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-
002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and GSO-Designate to maintain the SLCs and stock cards,
respectively, for each commodity/stock and regularly reconcile their records.
Also, require the GSO-Designate to submit to the Audit Team the RPCI not
later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semesters, respectively, to ensure the reliability of the reported inventory
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from procuring goods and
services through reimbursement and ensure strict implementation of R.A. No.
9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular
No. 92-389 to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of
same nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of
funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Budget Officer to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for LCPC and provide
programs, projects and activities for the strengthening and implementation of
the PPAs of LCPC as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-
120.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer to:

« submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities
and those that are to be implemented during the year;

e submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above
cited regulations; and

e inform the Auditors that the appropriate project signboards and/or
public notices were already posted within the prescribed period for
validation.

We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board, secure the approval of its 10-year Solid Waste
Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission
and make provisions in their plan for the closure or eventual phase out of the
controlled dump sites within the period specified under the framework and
develop and operate a sanitary landfill site as final disposal site pursuant to the
provisions under the above cited regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to pursue extensive collection of water service fee from delinquent water subscribers to augment the income generation of the municipality.  The OIC-Municipal Treasurer, being the concerned official, must take appropriate actions on the matter. In addition, the Office of the Municipal Engineer must see to it that all subscribers are installed with water meters.  Once installed, require periodic inspection and calibration to ensure their reliability and accuracy to determine accurate consumption of every household.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting for inventories as provided under the NGAS Manual.  The Supply/ Property Officer should maintain stock cards to determine reorder point and prepare the necessary report on materials issued to support recording of expenses in the books of accounts, then submit to the Municipal Accountant the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued supported by Requisition and Issue Slip to adjust the concerned inventory account.  It is also advisable to have a stockroom provided for the inventory items of the agency to protect them from spillage/ spoilage and unauthorized use.

strictly adhere to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Community Development Fund to ensure efficient output, come up with projects with developmental impact that would improve the socio-economic condition of the greater number of its constituents.

instruct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to review the process, relative to procurement activities of the local government unit, being undertaken by the BAC before processing and/or signing the claims of suppliers/dealers and make it a point to stop the payment of transactions with incomplete documentation.

5. We recommended that the Municipal Government through concerned department heads ensure that provisions on the period of implementation of BuB projects be provided in each project proposal and in the Memorandum of Agreement so that timeliness in projects implementation can be properly monitored.

	Time schedule for release of funds, periodic inspection/evaluation, reporting requirements, date of commencement of the project/activity and date of completion

observations.recommendation: 23. Also, the management refrain from granting of additional cash advances to officers and employees whose previous cash advances are not liquidated and accounted for in the books as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002.

observations.recommendation: 24. We recommended that the municipality comply with the provisions of NDRRM-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 on proper appropriation of the LDRRM Fund. The 70% LDRRMF be appropriated to cover operational expenses of all four (4) priority areas of the NDRRMP. Wages of hired Job Orders, MOOE and capital outlay for administrative purposes should not be funded out of the LDRRM Fund.

25. We recommended that the municipality appropriate funds for mandatory positions responsible for Administration and Training, and Operations and Warning and fill up the same in compliance with the guidelines on creation of LDRRM Office in the municipality.

observations.recommendation: 31. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant support the Land account with subsidiary ledgers and documents such as certificates of title establishing legal ownership thereto. Management should exercise due diligence in obtaining legal documents and process titling of unsupported real properties owned by the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant should also disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the composition and valuation of the Land account.
observations.reason:
Preparation of legal documents necessary in establishing legal title is still on-going.
The finding was first raised in CY 2014 AAR.

observations.recommendation: 34. We recommend that disbursements out of the SEF be in consonance to the provisions of RA 7160 (The Local Government Code) and to pertinent implementing rules and regulations. The Municipal School Board shall give priority to programs, projects and activities enumerated in the afore-mentioned regulations. Financial assistance and membership dues of pupils and students to Boy Scouts of the Philippines and Girl Scouts of the Philippines, as well as expenditures of the academe during Tiringbanay Festival should not be charged out of the SEF.
observations.reason: Some items not priority expenses of SEF were still charge to the fund

observations.recommendation: 35. We recommend that the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 and the individual MOA be strictly complied in the grant, utilization and accounting of fund transfers to the NGOs/POs and the immediate liquidation of fund transfers out of the SEF be enforced. Additional cash advances should not be granted to NGOs/POs who have not liquidated their previous cash advances. All transfers should be recorded in Due from NGOs/POs (Account 139) and should only be credited once the fund utilization reports with all the supporting documents are submitted and after the Municipal Accountant has determined the validity and propriety of the transaction.
observations.reason: ANS-PTA was still granted four additional cash advances in CY 2016 despite non-liquidation of their previous cash advances.

observations.recommendation: 36. We recommend that expenditures out of the 70% component of the LDRRMF should be utilized only for programs, projects and activities on disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness and disaster response as provided in RA 10121 and its IRR, and related DILG, DBM and NDRRMC issuances.  The Municipality should prioritize appropriating funds for the maintenance and operating expenses of the MDRRMO under the General Fund in compliance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular 2014-1.
observations.reason: Appropriations and utilization of 20% development fund in CY 2016 still included administrative expenses such as wages of job orders.

observations.recommendation: 37. We recommend that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer, who took charge of relief distribution and inventory, fully account the relief goods purchased and distributed by submitting the duly acknowledged distribution lists. Management also submit an explanation why relief packs distributed were insufficient compared to the number of families evacuated despite availability of relief items, and why many were left after the relief operations. Proper planning and execution of relief operations should be instituted to avoid too much surplus of relief goods.
observations.reason: No justifications submitted as of report date.

observations.recommendation: 38. We recommend that management require the concerned barangays and the Liga to submit fund utilization reports in accordance with the provisions of the MOA. We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer draw checks in the name of the agency or in the official title or designation of its head or treasurer. Management should likewise conduct proper planning, budgeting and coordination to avoid delays and other problems that may arise in the execution of its programs, projects and activities.
observations.reason: Fund utilization reports were not submitted as of report date.

observations.recommendation: 40. We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 10121 and its IRR, and COA Circular 2012-002 in the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF. The unexpended LDRRMF should be transferred to a special trust fund in the Trust Fund Books of the LGU. The Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF should be timely submitted to the Office of the Auditor monthly.
observations.reason:
The transfer of unutilized MDRRMF from Special Trust Fund in the General Fund to a separate Trust Fund books and bank account is on-going.
The finding was first raised in CY 2013 AAR.

observations.recommendation: 42. We recommend strict policy on the timely submission of DTRs should be issued and implemented. Heads of Departments of the Municipality should exercise due diligence in monitoring timely submission of DTRs by their subordinates. Failure to submit the DTRs and its supporting documents shall warrant the withholding of the salaries of employees concerned.  Moreover, disciplinary action should be instituted against non-complying officials and employees following laws and rules in the Civil Service.
observations.reason: Some employees were still not submitting their DTRs in a timely manner.

observations.recommendation: 46. We recommend that LGU officials, the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel in charge of the disbursement and monitoring of the financial assistance should require the recipient NGO/PO– Association of Tiringbanay Cooperators Inc. and Coast Haven Inc. to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2007-001 to ascertain the attainment of objectives for which the funds were released as well as the propriety of expenditures.
observations.reason: Liquidation report of Coast Haven, Inc. was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that: a. the Municipal Accountant submit to the Municipal Mayor the complete list of loan beneficiaries supported with available copies of MOAs, whenever available; b. representation be made with the Punong Barangays for the determination of the addresses of the beneficiaries to facilitate the issuance of Demand and Collection Letter; c. a request for write-off be prepared together with their supporting documents and be submitted to COA for approval; and, d. time table be set to comply with the aforementioned recommendations.

We recommended and Management agreed that: stop granting additional cash advances to officers and employees whose previous ones were not yet liquidated/settled and excess cash, if any, be immediately refunded. Likewise, execute certification that previous cash advances have been liquidated before granting another.
We recommended and the collecting officer agreed that the collecting officer prepare RCD at the close of each business day and effect remittance intact daily.

We recommended and Management agreed to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption per department and submit to the Office of the Mayor for consolidation. Afterwards, the consolidated report be submitted to the Auditor using the prescribed format.
We recommended and Management agreed to make written representation with the LTO to follow-up the release of government plate for the dump truck.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Local Development Council, the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, and the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2011 dated April 13, 2011 and ensure that only development projects be charged against the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed that in cases of lump-sum appropriations wherein no specific projects and no sufficient details were provided, a separate Sangguniang Bayan ordinance/resolution must always be secured to serve as an authority to enter into contract in accordance with Section 22(c) of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local School Board strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 implemented by DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated April 14, 1998, 01-A dated May 14, 2000 and 01-B dated June 25, 2001 in the utilization of SEF.
We recommended and Management agreed that a policy be formulated for proper monitoring or feedback mechanism and verification of the utilization of grants.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Supply Officer furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of every AIR within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance and inspection of the procured items in consonance with Section 6.09 of COA Circular No. 95-006 and COA Circular 92-386.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to observe the disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements the information and details of the unexpended DRRMF in accordance with the reporting guidelines set in COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

We recommend that Management   enforce strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.    Cash advances for special      purpose should be fully        liquidated after the purpose is    accomplished to protect        government funds from possible losses.  We also recommend that payment of contracts for supplies and materials and of services or for prizes/rewards during such activities be made by check    direct to the concerned payees to ensure payments are made to the rightful claimants and to avoid misappropriation of substantial amounts of cash advances transferred to other officials without proper documentation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Cash advances for Payroll Fund appears to have been fully liquidated if based on the cashbook of the Disbursing Officer.  However, we noted that there were payroll already recorded as fully paid when some payees have not signed or signify their acknowledgement of receiving the amounts indicated in the payrolls.
observations.reason: Some payees lack interest in affixing their signatures in the payrolls because the amounts have already been given in advance by the disbursing officer in the form of loans by loan sharks.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the designated CBRM Project Manager to submit the status report of loan accounts of individual loan borrowers; b. Require the Accounting Unit to prepare the billing of Loans Receivable account of (individual creditors) to be sent to the individual loan borrowers; c. Require the Municipal Accountant to furnish the Audit Team the List of Individual Loan Borrowers for confirmation and validation of account.
observations.reason: No accounting controls were established by Management to ensure repayment of loan borrowers.  After the release of the loans to the borrower-beneficiaries, no monitoring on the repayment of these loans had been undertaken by the CBRM Management, thus resulting unaccounted losses of unpaid loan accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the Accounting Office to record the transactions for the operations of the Eco-Tourism Business Center Facilities in subsidiary ledgers maintained for this special account and to prepare the corresponding financial statements thereof to form part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balances as required under the afore-stated provisions of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.  This will also serve as a tool in informing Management on whether or not the income generated by the economic enterprise/public  utility is sufficient to meet its operating costs.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommend Management to direct the Local Finance Committee to be prudent in the preparation of the Annual Budget by observing strictly the budgetary rules so that the authorized budget will be effective in carrying out the functions, projects and activities of the local government unit.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team for audit.  Moreover, we urge the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure the validity and correctness of the claims for honoraria before releasing payment thereof as provided in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: (NS/ND shall be issued once the transaction vouchers from July 2016 to December 2017 will be submitted).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management stop the practice of processing and approving cash advances and payments relative to the monthly RATA without complying the required supporting documents as provided in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: (NS/ND shall be issued once the transaction vouchers from July 2016 to December 2017 will be submitted).

observations.recommendation: We recommend Management to direct the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the budgetary rules so that the incurrence of overdraft in appropriations and unauthorized disbursements could be avoided.
observations.managementAction: Adjusted effected the JEV taking up the SEF RPT share as income in the SEF books.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit justification on the incurrence of personal services in excess of the PS limitation. Otherwise, salary increases and of other benefits granted in CY 2016 shall be disallowed in audit. We further recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to charge wages of casual employees performing administrative/ clerical functions  in the different offices of the municipality to its appropriate account Salaries and Wages-  Casual (5-01-01-020) as provided in the Chart of Accounts under the PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: Management still failed to control the hiring of casuals mostly of which are political protégées the wages of which cannot be accommodated by PS appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that : Management stop the old procurement practice and adopt public bidding for all government purchases through the BAC who is responsible for the determination of the complying bidders/suppliers to ensure that procurement of goods and  services are obtained at prices which are most advantageous to the government and adhere with the principles of transparency and competitiveness in government procurement.  Consolidate the requisitions from different offices to facilitate the conduct of appropriate procurement method, which is through the competitive public bidding. In cases where public bidding is impractical, the BAC, after a thorough deliberation, shall recommend the appropriate method of procurement to the Head of Procurement Entity or the local chief executive by way of a resolution; and In cases where Shopping (or small-value procurement) is employed, the BAC must ensure that only bonafide suppliers with known business establishments are being canvassed.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management exercise prudence in the use of government funds specifically: Seminars, trainings whose participants come from the LGU be conducted within the LGU in order to minimize the cost of the conduct of these activities and save funds for other worthwhile projects/activities; Send participants only to seminars within their area of concern; Documentary requirements such as Activity Design, Post-Activity Report and other necessary documents (such as approval/authority from the DILG Regional Director if seminar is conducted outside the province) be attached.
observations.managementAction: No action taken as the LGU continue to conduct seminar-workshops outside their locality incurring unnecessary expenses on venue rentals and travel expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management ask a formal approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) on the use of above bank accounts based on the provisions in DOF Circular 001-2005 and transfer immediately any amount in excess of the authorized cash balance to any Government Financial Institutions (GFIs) referred in Section 5.2 of the circular.
observations.managementAction: The LGU’s bank balance with FCB as of year-end appears to have reduced but only by an insignificant amount and still exceeded by P4 million over the maximum maintaining cash balance allowed by the DOF Circular.
observations.reason: The LGU insistence on the ease and accessibility of depositing with the said private bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: a. Retrieve or secure the documentary requirements as required by COA Circular 2012-001 pertaining to all lot acquisitions and submit the same to this Office to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances of the said transactions. b.  Keep and maintain an updated file of the real properties acquired by the LGU. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Real Property Ledger Card to record the acquisition, description, addition/ improvements, disposal and other information for each class of real property (land). Transactions affecting each real property such as cost of survey, etc. shall be posted from source documents promptly therein. c. Create a task force to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots. Funds should be sufficiently appropriated and be made available to defray expenses relative to titling.
observations.managementAction: The process of transfer of land ownership is reportedly on-going.  Copies of Deed of Sale/Donation of some lots were submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Lack of supporting documents such as the approved subdivision plan of acquired land. The former municipal assessor who had retired from the service was reportedly tasked to work on the titling of lots. Auditor’s Rejoinder: We reiterate our recommendation to management to create a task force for this purpose and to allocate the necessary funds for the purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: Require that the Driver’s Trip Tickets be used in addition to the gas slip, the same being the official document required to support official travels; Require that Driver’s Trip Tickets should be properly and completely filled up; Prepare and submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels; Require the strict implementation of Regulations 7, 4 and 2 of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. Henceforth, support all claims for gasoline expenses with Driver’s Trip Tickets, Monthly Reports of Travels and Fuel Consumptions.
observations.managementAction: Mayor’s staff prepared the Driver’s Trip Tickets before each official travel.
observations.reason: The drivers still failed to completely fill up all the required data in Trip Tickets. Moreover, the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels were

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management revisit its procurement process suited for the implementation of the Supplemental Feeding Program in the LGU and apply one that is more simple, economical and practical.  Petty cash fund may be granted to the day care workers at amounts equivalent to one-month food supplies requirement for the listed children beneficiaries. This will be utilized in procuring indigenous or locally produced food supplies under the supervision and guidance of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer.
observations.managementAction: Petty cash advances were allowed to the day care workers to purchase some food items and subject for liquidation.
observations.reason: The procurement process had become more complicated and entails a tedious task of tracing the utilization of the food items by the target beneficiaries in the 35 barangays, some procured in bulk through public bidding while the others through cash purchase from petty cash fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit a written justification for the grant of PEI  in excess of  the amount authorized under Section 1 of Executive Order No. 80 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2014-3, which set the Guidelines on the Grant of the Productivity Enhancement Incentive (PEI) to Government Employees for Fiscal Year (FY) 2014.  Otherwise, require refund of the excess amount by the officials and employees concerned.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted their justification as they believe that they are entitled to the PEI.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit the following for further evaluation, viz: Justification why the LGU undertook the procurement for a project where the intended beneficiaries were not its constituents; Justification why procurement was hastily done only right after the Court’s temporary injunction on the use of PDAF on September 10, 2013; Authenticated copy of Letter-Request/Resolution from the requesting Barangays/ association; Authenticated copy of MOA between LGU-Buenavista and the Office of  Congressman Aumentado on the implementation of the project; Explanation or Justification why the end-user barangays were not represented in the procurement/bidding process; Acknowledgement Receipt or Acceptance and Inspection Report duly accomplished/signed by responsible officials of the recipient barangays/association; Explanation/Justification why the Audit Team was not furnished with copies of the Purchase Order within five (5) days from perfection.
observations.managementAction: Complied.
observations.reason: (This Audit Finding is one of the subject complaint filed by a concerned citizen with the Office of the Ombudsman, thus an on-going investigation is being conducted)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: Stop immediately the practice of including the payments of loan amortizations to private lending institutions as deduction from the payroll. Terminate immediately the arrangement (MOA) with the FCB and with other private lending institutions. For regular employees who wish to avail of such loan facility, they may transact directly with the private lending institution concerned without involving the LGU personnel and resources to avoid incurring unnecessary expenses. For regular employees who wish to avail of such loan facility, they may transact directly with the private lending institution concerned without involving the LGU personnel and resources to avoid incurring unnecessary expenses.
observations.managementAction: The LGU claimed they already stopped signing MOAs with the private bank for the auto-matic deduction of monthly loan amortizations from the monthly salaries of employees.
observations.reason: There are still loan borrowers who have not yet fully paid their accounts to the bank, thus deductions were still made from the payroll as some LGU officials are still obliged to do so under the MOA, but promised no renewal of such MOA shall be made.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to withhold/deduct the sum of Php1,597,728.52  from the amounts payable to the Contractor, otherwise,  a Notice of Disallowance will be issued, in case of non-deduction from the disbursement voucher.  

Instruct the Municipal Engineer to meticulously prepare and review all the other Approved Budget for the Contract of Infrastructure Projects so that the same technical observation will not occur in the upcoming projects.
observations.managementAction:
Variation order of some projects had been done.   





For re-inspection






The Variation Order was compensated, however, transverse cracks and scaling of PCCP surface were noted and recommended for back job.  No comment from Management was received.




Retierated in Finding No.  12






For inspection

observations.recommendation:
Adhere to the requirement in the submission of financial accounts and reports to COA.

Instruct the concerned Accounting and Treasury to agree on the dates of submission of the disbursement vouchers, Reports of Collections and Deposits with its supporting documents so that the Accountant can submit the financial reports on time.

Consider providing additional manpower to the Accounting Office who are qualified to perform accounting functions. 

Further, Section 122 (2) of P.D. 1445 provides that failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission.
observations.managementAction: Delay in the submission of accounts and FS were still observed.

observations.recommendation:
Ensure appropriate compliance with COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the various activities that needs to be included in the Peace and Order Plan.

Require the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to be guided with the above observation in the succeeding preparation of budget and payment thereof.

Ensure timely submission of the related disbursement vouchers and complete supporting documents to the concerned COA Audit Team Leader, as required under Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

Expedite the processing of these deficient documents, more importantly, the Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) and Tax Declaration in the name of the LGU to the LGU, to protect the interest of the government.
Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the supporting documents in all transactions before certifying the completeness and propriety thereof. Require those in-charge of the project/program to attach the necessary documents as required in the claim.
Ensure the implementation of the planned activities and projects as incorporated in the LDRRMFIP to increase the level of disaster preparedness and response capability of the LGU.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the guidelines set in Sections 9.1.1 and 9.1.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 before effecting payments thereof.

Require the Municipal Engineer to submit the lacking necessary documents for review.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer already submitted some of the lacking necessary documents.

observations.recommendation: Refrain from hiring  consultant for legal matters instead follow the provision of Section 481 of RA 7160.

Conform with Sections 10, 49 or 53 of the IRR of  RA 9184 whichever is applicable in procuring the services of a consultant.  Moreover, refer to Annex B of the same IRR in case the LGU may engage  the services of a consultant.
  
Direct the Municipal Accountant to meticulously check the validity and completeness of the documents attached to the claim before certifying the same.

Refrain from granting additional cash advances if the previous cash advances were not yet settled.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the erring accountable officers, including retired/transferred employees to settle their outstanding cash advances immediately. 

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of officers and employees with unsettled cash advances, pursuant to Section 37 of Presidential Decree 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Management had verbally demanded to the concerned official and had refrained from granting additional cash advances to erring AOs.

observations.recommendation:
Refrain from granting honoraria except if it is for special projects as defined in Budget Circular No. 2007-2.

Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to make sure that specific appropriations are available for the claims before obligating the same.

Reconcile the amounts of PPE in the Subsidiary Ledgers and in the Statement of Financial Position.

Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE so that reconciliation with accounting records can be attained, and so that reliability of the value of PPE in the Statement of Financial Position can be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted an incomplete RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: Establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics, in compliance with the PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already institutionalized its GAD database, however, they did not administer HGDG tool in the conduct of gender analysis.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to:

Conduct a regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected either by the Treasury or Accounting Office will be adjusted and posted in their respective books.

Record and adjust immediately in its respective books all reconciling items found to be valid.

Review and analyze the prior years’ reconciling items which causes and origins remained to be unknown up to this date and locate the supporting documents especially of the bank debits and unrecorded issuance of checks to validate the issuance thereof.

Advise the bank to make adjustments if errors originate from the bank.

Closely supervise the assigned personnel especially those assigned in the recording of the books of the treasurer and accounting, as well as those who will prepare the BRS. Thoroughly review and scrutinize documents for the proper booking up of the reconciling items.
observations.managementAction: All LGU cash in bank accounts were already reconciled.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Treasurer and the Budget Officer to coordinate and update each other  on the monthly actual collections of its regular income so that release of allotment by the latter can be monitored.   

Ensure that estimated income be realistic and attainable to meet the desired obligations and finance the planned PPAs during the year.

We recommended that Management immediately implement the said projects for an efficient, effective and economical utilization of government funds and timely attainment of project objectives to intended beneficiaries.

Reorganize the Inventory   Committee and issue a      Memorandum reprimanding the members of the committee   that disregarded the directive to conduct physical inventory of PPE.
We reiterated our recommendation for the local chief executive  to require the Inventory Committee to expedite the conduct of physical inventory taking  to ascertain the reliability of the PPE accounts and to identify and segregate those that are still serviceable from unserviceable and submit the physical inventory report thereon to COA not later than January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction:
No action taken  by the Management.
The municipality failed to submit the prescribed Report of Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) for the year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in     finding no. 1 in  CY 2016 AAR (pages29 to 30)

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated that his Office had been coordinating with the NGO/PO for the submission of pertinent documents for the liquidation of the account.
observations.reason: Reiterated in     finding no. 4 in  CY 2016 AAR (pages34 to 36)

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant cited that part of the outstanding cash advances were already liquidated except those who were no longer in the service or are no longer employed with the municipality and that demand letters will be sent accordingly to all concerned.
observations.reason: Reiterated in     finding no. 2 in  CY 2016 AAR (pages30 to 32)

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort and find ways and means in collecting the overdue accounts receivable and the former to maintain subsidiary ledgers pertaining to these accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated that his Office has been reminding the parties concerned for the settlement of their obligations and he will coordinate with the OIC-Municipal Treasurer for the issuance of demand letters/notices to remind all concerned officials and employees relative to their obligations. He added that accountable officials who are still in the service are being subjected to payroll deduction to settle their obligations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in     finding no. 3 in  CY 2016 AAR (pages32 to 33)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to submit the required documents relative to the purchase of land by the Municipality to avoid suspension in audit. Moreover, require strict adherence to the guidelines set forth in Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 known as the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team that the pertinent documents were forwarded to the Office of the Municipal Assessor.
observations.reason: Reiterated in     finding no. 18 in CY 2016 AAR (pages62 to 65)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management to:

Cause the adequate monitoring of the garbage collection and disposal activities for the moment that an open-pit dumping is being adopted to avoid or reduce its impact on the environment;

Direct the official in-charge of the program to undertake/initiate the preparation of annual plans and programs, in coordination with the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, to be anchored on the 15-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality that has to include significantly the proposed funding/budget for each activity so that annually, the corresponding specific program/project/activity and time frame will be set for implementation; and

Ensure compliance with the provisions of Republic Act 9003 on the role of the local government unit in the effective solid waste management program/project implementation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer stated that currently their open pit dumpsite including the Material Recovery Facility (MRF) is located at Sitio Lumbangan, Barangay Libas, whereby the latter is still not operational.  He added that, in CY 2016 there will be a fund allocation for the purchase of 5,000-square meter lot to serve as the permanent site of the new operational Material Recovery Facility.  Likewise, the full implementation of the segregation scheme, the most pertinent provision of RA 9003, rests on the discretion of the Municipal Mayor.
observations.reason: Reiterated in     finding no. 22 in CY 2016 AAR (pages70 to 71)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management the following:

Require Municipal Accountant to facilitate the prompt preparation and submission of financial reports, vouchers and supporting documents.  Likewise, monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts should be regularly prepared and submitted within the prescribed period to the Audit Team to establish the correct balance of cash;
