Instruct him to enjoin office accounting personnel to give utmost importance and attention, particularly in the preparation of their reports to pave the way for the early submission of financial reports and accounts; and

Closely supervise the personnel assigned to specific functions and encourage them to remain focused on their respective duties and responsibilities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant disclosed to the Audit Team that the delay in submission or their failure to submit their reports/accounts on time was due to the lack of personnel.  Inter-Office transaction was also a factor, as the accounting and budget records are not systematically reconciled, especially in the determination of balances as to obligations made for various transactions.
observations.reason: Reiterated in     finding no. 15 in CY 2016 AAR (pages57 to 58)

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to require the concerned officials to submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (DRRMF) to the office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month so that timely review and evaluation in terms of compliance with the requirements of applicable laws, rules and regulations of the projects, programs and activities disadvantageous to the government could be immediately detected.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, designated MDRRMO admitted to the Audit Team to submit that they failed to submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Reiterated in     finding no. 19 in CY 2016 AAR (pages65 to 66)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:

Direct the GAD focal person/official, with the aim of ensuring that the genuine local gender issues will be addressed, to conduct study, data gathering, and coordination with barangays, social welfare/development official/s and the Philippine National Police to identify activities that affect the residents, especially the welfare of women and/or violations committed against them that are vital for the formulation of programs/projects that have to be in accordance with the provisions of the republic act;

Ensure that the Annual GAD Plan and Budget was approved by the DILG before incorporating to the Annual Budget so that the programs that will be implemented under GAD are directed to improving the municipal government’s capacity for gender mainstreaming, gender issues and/or promoting gender equality; and

Observe strict adherence to the guidelines set forth in the preparation of Annual GAD Plan and Budget as provided for under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction:
No action taken  by the  Management.
The GAD Plan for CY 2016 were still not supported with the Certificate of Approval by the DILG.
observations.reason: Reiterated in     finding no. 21 in CY 2016 AAR (pages67 to 69)

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to the following: a. That subsequent MOAs to be entered with NGO shall conform with the guidelines set in Sections 4.5.3 and 4.5.8 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 and Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; b. BCHCAI be required to submit the liquidation reports and all other required documents for fund releases enumerated in COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2012-001. c. Additional fund release should not be granted unless prior funds released were liquidated.
observations.reason: non-submission of Utilization/Liquidation Reports for fund transfers totaling P3,920,893.00, thus necessary control measures for transparency, accountability and monitoring of implementation of the projects cannot be established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant exert full efforts to update their records in order to establish and record the RPT/SET receivables accruing to the Municipality and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year duly supported with certified list of taxpayers.
observations.reason: Absence of a certified list of Real Property Taxpayers as basis in recording the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to the following: a. Set a time table to complete the latest addresses of the beneficiaries; b. Prepare Demand and Collection Letters for immediate issuance to loan borrowers; c. Furnish copy of Demand and Collection Letters to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to effect collection; and, d. Update loan records of all borrowers.
observations.reason: Demand letters were still not sent because there are no available records of the beneficiaries

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of the payable accounts. Further, liquidation of fund transfers be made to fully eliminate the balances.
observations.reason: reconciliation not effected between the Municipality and the Province; differences due to different recording of transactions

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The salaries of employees who are still active in service and whose cash advances as of December 31, 2011 remained unliquidated, be suspended pursuant to Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 and the salaries of those who have unliquidated cash advances granted from CY 2012 be withheld in accordance with Section 5.9 of COA Circular 97-002. b. In cases of deceased employees, initiate write-off of unliquidated cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997; c. The Municipal Accountant avoid granting additional cash advances to those who have unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed that: a. Cash advances for salaries, wages and other expenses will be granted based on the net amount of the payrolls together with all documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001. b. The Municipal Accountant execute certification that previous cash advances had been liquidated before granting of another cash advance.

We recommended that the Acting Supply Officer: a. Cause the immediate submission of the 2015 Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor; b. For CY 2016 and subsequently, summarize the driver’s trip tickets and prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and submit the same to the Auditor; and, c. Faithfully adhere to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 77-61 and Section 361 of GAAM, Volume I.
We recommended that the Supply Officer include in the property records of the Municipality the vehicle Ford Fiera to establish ownership of the said vehicle.
observations.reason: The Ford Fiera is not used anymore while the dump truck’s plate number has yet to be released by the LTO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 as implemented by DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated April 14, 1998, No. 01-A dated March 14, 2000 and No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001 in the utilization of SEF.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct review and validation on each of the journal entries made under Advances and Other Receivables accounts to determine correctness and propriety of the subject entry and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, when necessary.
observations.managementAction: Review/validation has been conducted and they are reconciling the available data.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the CY 2016 AAR per AOM NO. 2017-011(16) dated 02/16/17

observations.recommendation: Ensure appropriate compliance with COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the various activities that needs to be included in the Peace and Order Plan.
observations.managementAction: Directed the OIC-Accountant to strictly comply with the proper procedure / requirement.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR per AOM No. 2016-005(16) dated 12/06/16

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Administrator was tasked to closely monitor the inventory and compliance of COA findings.
observations.reason: Similar observation re: incomplete submission of RPCPPE and unreconciled PPE in Subsidiary Ledger and Statement of Financial Position will be included in the CY 2016 AAR per AOM NO. 2017-012(16)

observations.recommendation:
Ensure completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all contracts submitted to COA for review in accordance with COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009; and

Require the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts and its supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required documents for each of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure the completeness of the documents submitted to COA for review.
observations.managementAction: Directives were issued and the BAC Secretariat was directed to follow the checklist in the submission of contracts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the CY 2016 AAR per AOM No. 2017-005(16)

observations.recommendation:
Stop the practice of charging the financial incentives to Barangay Rescue Volunteers against the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund, without prejudice to the issuance of notice of disallowance for the said transactions due to lack of legal basis; and

Ensure strict compliance with RA 10121 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations and other related rules and regulations on the allocation and utilization of the LDRRM Fund.
observations.reason:
Notice of Disallowance was already  issued.  

Honoraria were still granted to Rescue Teams in CY 2016. Reiterated in CY 2016 per AOM NO.  2017-009-100(16) dated 02/14/17

observations.recommendation: Ensures timely submission of the related disbursement vouchers and complete supporting documents to the concerned COA Audit Team Leader, as required under Section 6.3.1 (f) of COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 and submit the required documents.
observations.managementAction: Directives were already issued for strict compliance.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR per AOM NO. 2016-006(16) dated 12/09/16

observations.recommendation: Strengthen its internal control system to ensure that the Special Education Fund should only be utilized to programs, projects and activities which are enumerated in Section 272 of RA 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001.
observations.managementAction: Issued directives to ensure such mistake be avoided.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 per AOM NO. 2016-007(16) dated 12/23/16

observations.recommendation:
Establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics, in compliance with the PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

Coordinate with the GAD Focal Point to ascertain the alignment of the GAD Plan with the GAD Agenda and the correctness and alignment of the entries in each column of the GPB template, e.g. if the proposed activities respond to the identified gender issue or cause of the issue, the issues are correctly identified or formulated, if there are clear indicators and targets, if the proposed budget is realistic, if the number of proposed activities are doable within the year.
observations.managementAction: Instructions to the GAD Focal Person were properly drawn up to effectively institutionalize GAD Database.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 per AOM No. 2017-013(16)

observations.recommendation: Ensure strict compliance to the provisions of Republic Act 9003 in proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste. Ensure the implementation of segregation at source of solid waste, enforcement of “no segregation, no collection” policy, creation of Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) in every barangay, and the development of disposal facility in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum was issued.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR per AOM No. 2017-003(16)

observations.recommendation: Ensure that expected income incorporated in the appropriation ordinance will be attained; otherwise, employ conservative planning/forecasting of expected income onwards.
observations.managementAction:
Treasurer’s Office tasked to exert effort.

BPLO directed to massively campaign for the business licenses/permits.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
Intensify efforts in accomplishing the requirements for the establishment of LCU required documents as enumerated under CHED Memorandum Order No. 32, series of 2006; and

Ensure that prior to the implementation of projects in the future, required compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations should have been made to avoid possible wastage of scarce government funds.
observations.managementAction: Directives were issued.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR per AOM NO. 2017-004(16) dated 01/31/17

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


 ➢ secure authority from the  Sangguniang  Bayan  to  request  the
   Commission on  Audit  for  the  write-off  of  the  unaccounted
   receivables and those under the Carabao  Dispersal  program  in
   accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005;


 ➢ require the Municipal Treasurer to pursue the  collection  from
   recipients of SEA loans; and


 ➢ refrain from implementing similar  projects  if  sustainability
   is not assured.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require  the  accountable
officers to fully liquidate and refund the excess cash  advance  at  year
end in compliance with existing regulations for fair presentation of  the
financial statements.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
Accountant to reclassify the amount of P46,863.81 from Other Payables  to
Prior Years’ Adjustments for fair presentation of the affected accounts in
the financial statements.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
Treasurer/Accountant   to   deduct   from   the   salary   of   concerned
officials/employees  to  ensure  collection  of  the  amount  which   was
improperly paid by the Municipality.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
Treasurer to remit to the Barangay Treasurers the barangay shares on RPT
and other collections within five days after the  end  each  quarter,  in
compliance with existing regulation so as not to deprive the barangay  of
its utilization.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  establish  the  Local
Councils for the Protection of Children and require the Municipal  Budget
Officer to provide the necessary appropriation representing  one  percent
of the IRA for programs, projects and activities for  the  protection  of
children.

Require the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort and find ways and means in collecting the overdue accounts receivable and the former to maintain subsidiary ledgers pertaining to these accounts.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to refrain from granting the monthly telephone allowance without legal basis. Further, require the persons liable therefore to refund the amount received by them.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting for inventories as provided under the NGAS Manual.  The Supply/ Property Officer should maintain stock cards to determine reorder point and prepare the necessary report on materials issued to support recording of expenses in the books of accounts, then submit to the Municipal Accountant the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued supported by Requisition and Issue Slip to adjust the concerned inventory account.  It is also advisable to have a stockroom provided for the inventory items of the agency to protect them from spillage/spoilage and unauthorized use.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant strictly observe/follow updated description of accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and reclassify the expenses to proper inventory or expense account; and
Municipal Budget Officer stop charging of expenses not aligned with the appropriation for a corresponding expense account for fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended to seek the refund of the respective amounts paid to the former Municipal Elective Officials that represent the cash equivalent of the five-day mandatory leave that were not deducted from their respective vacation leave credits.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer:
submit justification for failure to provide appropriation for monetization of leave credits for all departments and for certifying existence of appropriation on various claims other that the Office of the Mayor;
ensure provision of appropriations on all departments in ensuing year; and
limit the grant of monetization of leave credits within the approved appropriation.

We recommended the Management to:
maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and ensure implementation and completion of the projects identified for the benefit of its constituents.
strictly adhere to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Community Development Fund to ensure efficient output, come up with projects with developmental impact that would improve the socio-economic condition of the greater number of its constituents.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive:
provide the necessary fund for LCPC to ensure that all plans for CICL are implemented. Outsourcing or partnership with private organizations and entities that provide financial assistance for the implementation of LCPC programs is encouraged;
instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to check and review whether the amount allotted for LCPC is equivalent to 1% of IRA and the identified PPAs are   in conformance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012; and
instruct all those concerned in the preparation of PPAs to make reference to RA 9344 with the full consideration that such activities are designed solely for Children at risk and Children in Conflict with the Law and not for children in general.

We recommended the following:
Submit copies of the contracts/purchase orders together with the supporting documents, for review and technical  evaluation of the contract to point out to management the defects of the contracts and the project itself, should there be any, and to effect timely corrective measures.
Require the BAC and the MGSO to ensure that the contract and all its supporting documents, including those required in the auditorial and technical review, be submitted to the Auditor’s Office within five days from perfection thereof as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant be required to submit the abovementioned required documents relative to the purchase of land by the Municipality to avoid suspension in audit. Moreover, require strict adherence to the guidelines set forth in Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 known as the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions.

We recommended the management to require the concerned officials to submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (DRRMF) to the office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month so that timely review and evaluation in terms of compliance with the requirements of applicable laws, rules and regulations of the projects, programs and activities disadvantageous to the government could be immediately detected.
We recommended the management to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately process the remittance of unutilized PDAF to the National Treasury

We recommended that the:
Local Chief Executive mandated to reorganize its respective Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) to develop a solid waste management plan that ensure long-term management of solid waste in its area of jurisdiction and submission to the national council for review and approval, and thereby implementation by the agency; and
MSWMB submit immediately to the Provincial Committee on Solid Waste Management for their consolidation to come up with a Consolidated 10-year Solid Waste Development Program.

We recommended the management:
discontinue the use of open dumpsite and identify possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as final disposal site of solid wastes of the Municipality. This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines enumerated in RA No. 9003; and
ensure compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9003 on the role of the local government unit in the effective implementation of solid waste management program/projects and activities.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to:
1. conduct physical count of the hospitals, health centers and
markets based on the schedule of the Accounting Office that
contains the locations of these PPE items;
2. coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Engineer in locating the transaction documents and
information/data of the Other Land Improvements in CY 2012 and
School Building in CY 2015; and,
3. furnish the Municipal Accountant with the CY 2016 RPCPPE.
Likewise, we also recommended and the Municipal Accountant
agreed to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the recording of
the Dump Truck from the Motor Vehicles account to Construction and Heavy
Equipment account in the amount of P6,360,000.00.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to furnish the
Municipal Accountant with a certified list of taxpayers as basis for setting
up the RPT Receivables and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
1) the salaries of the active employee whose cash advances as of
December 31, 2011 remained unliquidated, despite final demand,
be suspended immediately pursuant to Section 9.2 of COA Circular
No. 2012-004;
2) in case of deceased employee/s, initiate write-off of unliquidated
cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016;
3) the Municipal Accountant send demand letters through registered
mail to the last known home address of those retired/resigned
employees; and,
4) follow up or send tracers to the Commission on Audit Central
Office on the action to the submitted liquidations of cash advances
for Confidential and Intelligence Fund.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
1. Regularly provide the Provincial Accountant with data on the
Province’s share in the real property and other tax collections
made by the Municipality;
2. Trace the details of the fund transfers by requesting copies of
Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) of the Province;
3. Submit liquidation documents for fund transfers recorded by the
Province as Due from LGUs; and,
4. Conduct periodic reconciliation with the Provincial Accountant.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. the Municipal Accountant submit to the Municipal Mayor the
complete list of loan beneficiaries supported with available copies
of MOAs, whenever available;
b. representation be made with the Punong Barangays for the
determination of the addresses of the beneficiaries to facilitate
the issuance of Demand and Collection Letter;
c. a request for write-off be prepared together with their supporting
documents and be submitted to COA for approval; and,
d. time table be set to comply with the aforementioned
recommendations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant ensure that cash advances for
payroll are supported with approved payrolls with their net payments as
basis for drawing cash advances

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to stop
granting additional cash advances to officers and employees whose
previous ones were not yet liquidated/settled and excess cash, if any, be
immediately refunded. Likewise, execute certification that previous cash
advances have been liquidated before granting another.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed that the
collecting officer prepare RCD at the close of each business day and effect
remittance intact daily.

We recommended and Management agreed to increase the amount of
bond of the AO to ensure the full coverage of her accountability.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit
the monthly BRS to the Auditor within the reglementary period for timely
verification so as to ascertain the correctness of the Cash in Bank account
balances reflected in the financial statements and reflect the book
reconciling items, if any, in the period to which it pertains.

We recommended and Management agreed that BUB funds from
National Government Agencies be utilized fully for the benefit of the
constituents and ensure the proper use thereof in accordance with the
guidelines under DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7
dated November 3, 2015.

We recommended and Management agreed to prepare the Monthly
Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption per
department and submit to the Office of the Mayor for consolidation. Afterwards,
the consolidated report be submitted to the Auditor using the prescribed format.

We recommended and Management agreed to make written
representation with the LTO to follow-up the release of government plate for
the dump truck.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers, payroll, official receipts
and financial reports within the prescribed period.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Local
Development Council, the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator, and the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the guidelines
provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2011 dated
April 13, 2011 and ensure that only development projects be charged
against the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed that in cases of lump-
sum appropriations wherein no specific projects and no sufficient details
were provided, a separate Sangguniang Bayan ordinance/resolution must
always be secured to serve as an authority to enter into contract in
accordance with Section 22(c) of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local School
Board strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 as
implemented by DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated April 14,
1998, No. 01-A dated March 14, 2000 and No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001 in the
utilization of SEF.

We recommended and Management agreed that a policy be
formulated for proper monitoring or feedback mechanism and verification of
the utilization of grants.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Supply Officer
furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of every AIR within twenty four (24)
hours from acceptance and inspection of the procured items in consonance with
Section 6.09 of COA Circular No. 95-006 and COA Circular 92-386.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to observe
the disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements the information and
details of the unexpended DRRMF in accordance with the reporting
guidelines set in COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that Management enforce strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. Cash advances for special purpose should be fully liquidated after the purpose is accomplished to protect government funds from possible losses.  Consequently, this will redound to the presentation of correct balances of the cash and government equity accounts at year-end.
We also recommend that payment of contracts for supplies and materials and of services or for prizes/rewards during such activities be made by check direct to the concerned payees to ensure payments are made to the rightful claimants and to avoid misappropriation of substantial amounts of cash advances transferred to other officials without proper documentation.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that Management instruct the Accounting Office to record the transactions for the operations of the Eco-Tourism Business Center Facilities in subsidiary ledgers maintained for this special account and to prepare the corresponding financial statements thereof to form part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balances as required under the afore-stated provisions of the NGAS Manual, Volume I. This will also serve as a tool in informing Management on whether or not the income generated by the economic enterprise/public utility is sufficient to meet its operating costs.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend Management to direct the Local Finance Committee to be prudent in the preparation of the Annual Budget by observing strictly the budgetary rules so that the authorized budget will be effective in carrying out the functions, projects and activities of the local government unit.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team for audit.  Moreover, we urge the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure the validity and correctness of the claims for honoraria before releasing payment thereof as provided in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that Management stop the practice of processing and approving cash advances and payments relative to the monthly RATA without complying the required supporting documents as provided in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend Management to direct the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the budgetary rules so that the incurrence of overdraft in appropriations and unauthorized disbursements could be avoided.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that Management submit justification on the incurrence of personal services in excess of the PS limitation. Otherwise, salary increases and of other benefits granted in CY 2016 shall be disallowed in audit.
We further recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to charge wages of casual employees performing administrative/ clerical functions  in the different offices of the municipality to its appropriate account Salaries and Wages-  Casual (5-01-01-020) as provided in the Chart of Accounts under the PPSAS.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that Management demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter and uphold observance of accountability and transparency of all government transactions. Failure to support these disbursements with the official receipt of the Philippine Government shall cause the issuance of the Notice of Suspension for these payments.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that :
Management stop the old procurement practice and adopt public bidding for all government purchases through the BAC who is responsible for the determination of the complying bidders/suppliers to ensure that procurement of  goods and  services are obtained at prices which are most advantageous to the government and adhere with the principles of transparency and competitiveness in government procurement.
Consolidate the requisitions from different offices to facilitate the conduct of appropriate procurement method, which is through the competitive public bidding. In cases where public bidding is impractical, the BAC, after a thorough deliberation, shall recommend the appropriate method of procurement to the Head of Procurement Entity or the local chief executive by way of a resolution; and
In cases where Shopping (or small-value procurement) is employed, the BAC must ensure that only bonafide suppliers with known business establishments are being canvassed.

Reconcile the amounts of PPE in the Subsidiary Ledgers and in the Statement of Financial Position.
Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE so that reconciliation with accounting records can be attained, and so that reliability of the value of PPE in the Statement of Financial Position can be ascertained.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct review and validation on each of the journal entries made under Advances and Other Receivables accounts to determine the correctness and propriety of the subject entry and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, when necessary.

Refrain from granting additional cash advances if the previous cash advances were not yet settled.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the erring accountable officers, including retired/transferred employees to settle their outstanding cash advances immediately.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of officers and employees with unsettled cash advances, pursuant to Section 37 of Presidential Decree 1445.

Ensure appropriate compliance with COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the various activities that needs to be included in the Peace and Order Plan.
Require the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to be guided with the above observation in the succeeding preparation of budget and payment thereof.

Ensure timely submission of the related disbursement vouchers and complete supporting documents to the concerned COA Audit Team Leader, as required under Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

Refrain from granting honoraria except if it is for special projects as defined in Budget Circular No. 2007-2.
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to make sure that specific appropriations are available for the claims before obligating the same.
Refrain from hiring consultant for legal matters instead follow the provision of Section 481 of RA 7160.

Conform with Sections 10, 49 or 53 of the IRR of  RA 9184 whichever is applicable in procuring the services of a consultant.  Moreover, refer to Annex B of the same IRR in case the LGU may engage  the services of a consultant.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to meticulously check the validity and completeness of the documents attached to the claim before certifying the same.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the guidelines set in Sections 9.1.1 and 9.1.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 before effecting payments thereof.
Require the Municipal Engineer to submit the lacking necessary documents for review.

Intensify efforts in accomplishing the documentary requirements as enumerated under CHED Memorandum Order No. 32, series of 2016; and
Ensure that documentary requirements be in place before proceeding to the next phase of the project.

Expedite the processing of these deficient documents, more importantly, the Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) and Tax Declaration in the name of the LGU to the LGU, to protect the interest of the government.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the supporting documents in all transactions before certifying the completeness and propriety thereof. Require those in-charge of the project/program to attach the necessary documents as required in the claim.

Adhere to the requirement in the submission of financial accounts and reports to COA.
Instruct the concerned Accounting and Treasury to agree on the dates of submission of the disbursement vouchers, Reports of Collections and Deposits with its supporting documents so that the Accountant can submit the financial reports on time.
Consider providing additional manpower to the Accounting Office who are qualified to perform accounting functions.
Establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics, in compliance with the PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

Direct the Treasurer and the Budget Officer to coordinate and update each other  on the monthly actual collections of its regular income so that release of allotment by the latter can be monitored.
Ensure that estimated income be realistic and attainable to meet the desired obligations and finance the planned PPAs during the year.
Ensure the implementation of the planned activities and projects as incorporated in the LDRRMFIP to increase the level of disaster preparedness and response capability of the LGU.

Strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act 9003 in proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste.
Ensure the implementation/enforcement of the programs,  projects and activities embodied in the Municipality’s ESWM Plan pursuant to Republic Act 9003, such as:
Education and Public Information
Segregation at source of solid waste;
“No segregation, no collection” policy;
Creation of Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) in every barangay; and
Development of disposal facility in the Municipality.

Utilize the SEF appropriately pursuant to the laws governing the said Fund.
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer  and Municipal Accountant to observe tighter control measures to ascertain that government obligations are charged appropriately against the respective budgets for proper and correct reporting.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to withhold/deduct the sum of P1,597,728.52 from the amounts payable to the Contractor, otherwise,  a Notice of Disallowance will be issued, in case of non-deduction from the disbursement voucher.
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to meticulously prepare and review all the other Approved Budget for the Contract of Infrastructure Projects so that the same technical observation will not occur in the upcoming projects.
We recommended that Management coordinate with the source agencies for proper return of the said unutilized.

We recommended that Management immediately implement the said projects for an efficient, effective and economical utilization of government funds and timely attainment of project objectives to intended beneficiaries.
We recommended that the Chief Accountant monitor the settlement of suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to depreciate the asset account in compliance with existing regulation for fair presentation of affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation was already provided for the Water Supply System starting January 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO-Designate to secure copy of the Deed of Donation that will be the basis of the Accountant in taking up in the books of such donation in compliance with Section 63 of P.D. No. 1445 for fair presentation of the PPE-Motor Vehicles account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Since the donor could not provide the Deed of Donation, the vehicle was recorded at an appraised value of P75,000.00 in December 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the amount of P1,371,378.27 from Due to LGUs to Due to Other Funds of P1,354,378.27 and Income of P17,000.00 for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The account was adjusted in July 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letter to concerned officials for the collection of the amount, otherwise, impose the withholding of their salaries.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P29,942.50 was deducted from the payroll, leaving a balance of P13,683.80.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer inadvertently forgot to deduct the remaining amount from their salaries during pay period. Reiterated in Part ll, Observation No.4

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require Ms. Erlita C. Targa to refund immediately the amount of P14,507.00, otherwise require the Municipal Treasurer to withhold her salary corresponding to the amount of cash advance. We recommended further that in case of failure of the Municipal Treasurer to deduct the amount from the salary of the Accountable Officer, require the Municipal Treasurer to personally settle the cash advance.
observations.managementAction: The cash advance of Ms. Erlita C. Targa in the amount of P14,507.00 was already refunded under OR No. 9028839 dated July 14, 2016. The amount was refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9679 on the maximum limit of Pag-IBIG Contribution-Government Share at P100.00 per month for each employee-member. Also, require employees who were benefited to refund the amount of government share in excess of P100.00 per month.
observations.managementAction: The Pag-IBIG Contribution-Government Share is now fixed at P100.00 per month for each employee-member. Partial refund in the amount of P46,863.81 was deducted from the terminal leave of those who retired as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: Late receipt of Notice of Disallowance Nos. 2016-003(15) dated June 14, 2016 and 2017-001-101(15-16) dated January 16, 2017 by all persons liable.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: Municipal Treasurer to send collection notices to concerned project beneficiaries to be able to collect the amount of P540,054.92 and to ensure the continuity of livelihood projects/programs and to give other prospective beneficiaries equal opportunity in availing capital for their livelihood projects. Municipal Agriculture Officer and MSWD Officer to assist the MTO in sending collection and/or demand letters to the beneficiaries. Municipal Accountant to exert efforts to locate the documents for the P77,074.37 unaccounted receivable.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent only to recipients under the Carabao Dispersal Program, while no demand letters were sent to SEA recipients. Municipal Agriculture Officer sent collection letters.
observations.reason: Most of the recipients under the Carabao Dispersal Program were either already dead or no longer residing in the Barangay. Reiterated in Part ll, Observation No.1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a Disposal Committee and prioritize the immediate sale of obsolete and unserviceable assets thru public auction or other modes of disposal to avoid further deterioration of the same and earn at least the salvage value of these assets and also, unclog the very limited office space/storage or donate to other government agencies those that are still serviceable but no longer used.
observations.managementAction: Disposal of unserviceable properties is now in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to include in the Notes to Financial Statements the reconciliation of the surplus/deficit with the net cash flow from operating activities.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the surplus/deficit from operating activities for CY 2016 was prepared and included in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following measures:

Look into the necessity of the rendition of various personnel activities of the agency in the end that minimizes its expenditures for registration fees as well as travel allowances during their attendance/participation to conventions, seminars, and trainings will be undertaken;

Identify what seminars/trainings are to be considered as vital to the municipal government’s operation in the same manner that only the legitimate and specific target participants, who can reecho the various topics and pointers discussed in these activities to their colleagues, will be authorized to go on official travel; and

Conduct a study and analysis of the frequency of official travels of municipal officials wherein in some cases, the farthest venues are being chosen despite the more accessible and nearer locations.  It must be taken into consideration that the frequent absence in the official station/workplace will somehow affect the officials’ work efficiency.
observations.managementAction:
The Management adhered to the recommendations of the Audit Team.
Claims of travel had been limited to those that are necessary and related to the functions of the official concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the submission of copies of contracts/purchase orders together with the supporting documents, within five days from perfection thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001, for prompt evaluation by Auditor and to point out to management the defects of the orders/contracts and the project itself, should there be any, and to finally effect timely corrective measures.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the required documents

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to pursue extensive collection of water service fee from delinquent water subscribers to augment the income generation of the municipality.  The OIC-Municipal Treasurer, being the concerned official, must take appropriate actions on the matter. In addition, the Office of the Municipal Engineer must see to it that all subscribers are installed with water meters.  Once installed,  require periodic inspection and calibration to ensure their reliability and accuracy to determine accurate consumption of every household.
observations.managementAction: Agency’s efforts to collect water service fee’s delinquencies have been continuously being exerted by sending notices to concerned subscribers to remind them of their obligations and be compelled to pay.  Other steps have being undertaken.
observations.reason: Reiterated in     finding no. 6 in  CY 2016 AAR (pages39 to 40)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality’s Bids and Awards Committee be advised to abide by the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 especially Section 13 thereof in the conduct of competitive public bidding.
Likewise, instruct them to immediately nullify the result of the said procurement activity attributed to the representations herein made.
observations.managementAction: The agency has been sending invitation/s to observers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:

Require the BAC, its Secretariat and Technical Working Group to strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations and ensure completeness of the documents submitted by bidders as prescribed under Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in all procurement activities of the Municipality; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to review the process, relative to procurement activities of the local government unit, being undertaken by the BAC before processing and/or signing the claims of suppliers/dealers and make it a point to stop the payment of transactions with incomplete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Partial submission of documents was undertaken by the Management
observations.reason: Reiterated in     finding no. 17 in CY 2016 AAR (pages60 to 62)

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to require the inclusion of Tax Clearance as part of the Class “A” legal eligibility documents and comply with the requirements of GPPB Resolution No. 21-2013 dated July 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The BAC has included the Tax Clearance as a requirement on the legal eligibility documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management the following:

Require the payments of RATA to Officers and employees conform to the guidelines or scheme set forth under DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013;

Seek refund from concerned officers/officials of the amount in excess of  amount allowed in audit; and

Require submission of the minutes of sessions/actual attendance by the members of Sangguanian Bayan for further evaluation and verification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office started observing adherence to prescribed guidelines in the payment/granting of  RATA to department heads prior to its payment/granting.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  the Management the following:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to see to it that true copies of DTRs or bundy cards were attached to the payrolls before processing payments of salaries to ensure reliable basis for actual attendance/services rendered by the employee; and

Require the Acting Human Resource Officer to ensure that actual copies of DTRs/bundy cards  were properly accomplished and the supporting documents such as application for leave, travel orders and certificate of appearance from seminars attended must be properly attached therewith
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Human Resource Management Officer ensure that upon checking of DTRs by municipal employees, they immediately attached them to payrolls for salaries and allowances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer or the General Services Officer to see to it that in the procurement of goods, the corresponding Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) conforms with the PBDs;

Direct the above Officer, the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to facilitate the preparation of the ABC for every procurement of infrastructure activity in accordance with the provisions of the foregoing department order and its attachment; and

Ensure that the subject document embodies the realistic quantities and costs based on the existing rates and estimates in order for the local government unit to conform with the guidelines set forth on this matter.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality required submission of ABC in all procurement activity.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Submit copies of the contracts together with the supporting documents, for review and technical evaluation of the contract to point out to management the defects of the contracts and the project itself, should there be any, and to effect timely corrective measures.

Require the BAC and the MGSO to ensure that the contract and all its supporting documents, including those required in the auditorial and technical review, be submitted to the Auditor’s Office within five days from perfection thereof as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to perform the following courses of action:

Make representations with the Department of Budget and Management to cause the release/transfer of the Municipal Government’s Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) to the LBP account of the local government unit;

Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the eventual changes  in both the cashbooks and accounting records; and

See to it that the provisions of the DOF Department Order No.  27-05 dated December 9, 2005 will be strictly adhered to.
observations.managementAction: Starting June 2017, the IRA of the agency was credited to the Land Bank of the Philippines. The Municipal Treasurer stated that they have already started to withdraw the cash gradually from the Philippine National Bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately process the remittance of unutilized PDAF to the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The Management has already remitted the amount of P200,000.00 to the National Treasury.
observations.reason: Reiterated in     finding no. 20 in CY 2016 AAR (pages66 to 67)

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the following recommendations:

Adhere strictly to the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 to ensure efficient output, come up with projects with developmental impact that would improve more the socio-economic condition of the greater number of its constituents;

Identify, first and foremost, the programs and projects that must be prioritized for inclusion and consideration in the Annual Investment Plan.

For those which are inconsistent with the governing rules and regulations, initiate amendments or realignment of the latter to those that address the needs of the constituents as far as tangible projects and programs are concerned; and

Source and appropriate in the General Fund Proper expenses that are administrative in nature which meet the validity and regularity requirements under existing accounting and auditing rules.
observations.managementAction: Various non-capital projects were still being charged to 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in     finding no. 13 in CY 2016 AAR (pages52 to 53)

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that a Property Custodian/Supply Officer be appointed
observations.managementAction: municipality had conducted a physical count of all the property, plant and equipment but the RPCPPE was not prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation.
observations.reason: The RPCPPE was not prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the prescribed guidelines under RA 7305 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the payment of hazard pay.

We recommended the following: a. That the MDRRM Coordinator and the Municipal Accountant ensure that only expenditures directly related to disaster risk management activities shall be charged against the 5% LDRRMF. b. That a separate budget for the MDRRMO be prepared to finance the activities and supplies needed.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to transfer the unexpended balance of the 5% MDRRMF to the TF and its details should be disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended the following: a. The MPDC ensure immediate implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund to ensure optimal utilization of the fund. b. The MPDC, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant comply strictly with the provisions of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that the Municipal Government along with Local Planning Development Office (LPDO) establish and maintain GAD Database for storage of necessary information in aid of gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation and mainstreaming.

We recommended that: a. The Municipal Engineer ensure speedy completion of the site preparation and all other facilities and requirements needed in the operation of the Commercial Complex and Public Utility Terminal, to have it operational as soon as possible. b. Extra efforts should be exerted to hasten the approval of the New Market Code by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan. c. In subsequent projects, careful planning should be done to prevent delays in their operations and loss of income that should have been realized through the project.

We recommend that management ask a formal approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) on the use of above bank accounts based on the provisions in DOF Circular 001-2005 and transfer immediately any amount in excess of the authorized cash balance to any Government Financial Institutions (GFIs) referred in Section 5.2 of the circular.
observations.managementAction: Received from FCB the authorization from Central Bank that FCB is granted or authorized to accept government deposits.
observations.reason: Auditor’s Rejoinder: FCB may be authorized by Central Bank to accept government deposits as any other bank. However, Management may have missed the gist of our audit recommendation which is to ask a formal approval from the BLGF on the use of the private bank (FCB) as   depository of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: Require the Municipal Treasurer to record completely, chronologically and in sequential order all collections and the corresponding deposits in his cashbooks daily to be able to readily determine his accountabilities at any time. Require the accountable officers concerned to reconcile their cashbook balances with accounting records monthly; Require the Municipal Treasurer to record in sequential order the Reference OR/Voucher Number assigned to the Report of Collection and Deposit (RCD) in his cashbooks for easy reference during reconciliation with accounting records. Require the Accounting Unit to attach the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to the Report of Collection and Deposits (RCDs) after checking them against the official receipts and deposit slips to facilitate verification and audit. Require the Municipal Treasurer/Accountant to furnish the Audit Team with copies of the Abstract of General and Real Property Tax (RPT) Collections monthly to facilitate verification of the receipts and reconciliation with the cashbook and accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Changed system of posting of collections and deposits which should be in chronological order. It was implemented upon order of the BLGF after examination of the MTO and found the same error and deficiencies as that of the Auditors. Recording was followed accordingly as recommended Attached the JEV accordingly as recommended. Monthly Abstracts of General and Real Property Tax (RPT) Collection are being submitted to COA for compliance as recommended.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit justification on the late submission of the cash advance voucher for post-audit and to submit succeeding cash advance voucher/s within seven (7) days after release of check. Also, we recommend that Management limit the granting of cash advance to three (3) months requirement only and refrain from processing and granting additional cash advances for confidential/intelligence expenses unless credit notices for the liquidation of previous cash advances are already received from the Office of the COA Chairperson.
observations.managementAction: Management already complied with the COA_ DBM_DILG Joint Memo Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 pertaining to Confidential Fund Liquidation and Cash Advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: Initiate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties by creating an Inventory Committee composed of a representative each from the Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned by the Municipality; Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all the properties recorded in the books and those not yet recorded but actually found in the LGU offices/premises and to prepare the corresponding inventory reports thereof; and reconcile them with the accounting records to come up with the correct account balances in the financial statement. Require the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain stock cards and property ledger cards, respectively to properly account the receipt and disposition of property and supplies.
observations.managementAction: The inventory committee started the Physical Count of Unserviceable Property which were identified and reported. Records are kept intact in the GSO and Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Management still failed to conduct a complete physical count of all the properties owned by the LGU due to lack of determination and prioritization, thus the required report (RPCPPE) could not be prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: Require the Municipal Accountant to drop unserviceable properties from the PPE accounts pursuant to Section 79 of P. D. 1445 and classify to its appropriate account pending disposal. Require the Disposal Committee to complete the processes/instructions to be undertaken on the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIURP) as prescribed in Sections 51 and 59 of the NGAS Manual Volume II.
observations.managementAction: The accounting section dropped the unserviceable properties from the PPE accounts pursuant to Section 79 of P.D. 1445 and classified to its appropriate account pending disposal. Require the Disposal Committee to complete the processes/instructions to be undertaken on the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIURP) as prescribed in Sections 51 and 59 of the NGAS Manual Volume II.
observations.reason: The LGU is awaiting for the COAs approval of dropping the unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: a. Retrieve or secure the documentary requirements as required by COA Circular 2012-001 pertaining to all lot acquisitions and submit the same to this Office to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances of the said transactions. b.  Keep and maintain an updated file of the real properties acquired by the LGU.  Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Real Property Ledger Card to record the acquisition, description, addition/ improvements, disposal and other information for each class of real property (land). Transactions affecting each real property such as cost of survey, etc. shall be posted from source documents promptly therein. c. Create a task force to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots. Funds should be sufficiently appropriated and be made available to defray expenses relative to titling.
observations.managementAction: The process of transfer of land ownership is reportedly on-going. Copies of Deed of Sale/Donation of some lots were submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Lack of supporting documents such as the approved subdivision plan of acquired land. The former municipal assessor who had retired from the service was reportedly tasked to work on the titling of lots. Auditor’s Rejoinder: We reiterate our recommendation to management to create a task force for this purpose and to allocate the necessary funds for the purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to review the transactions pertaining to the recording of estimates as well as collection procedures of the basic real property tax and special education tax and compare with those prescribed in the afore-cited Sections of NGAS Manual Volume I. Adjustments shall be made after review of these transactions from the setting up of receivables to the collection procedures and recording of distribution of shares. Henceforth, we recommend both the municipal treasurer and municipal accountant to comply with the recording and collection procedures prescribed in Sections 20, 81, 84 and 86 of the NGAS Manual Volume I. Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant cause the preparation and submission of the Abstract of Real Property Tax Collections to the Audit Team monthly to serve as basis of our verification of the correctness of the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Income accounts, including the distribution of the shares of the province, the municipality and the barangays.
observations.managementAction: Corrected and complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: Require that the Driver’s Trip Tickets be used in addition to the gas slip, the same being the official document required to support official travels; Require that Driver’s Trip Tickets should be properly and completely filled up; Prepare and submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels; Require the strict implementation of Regulations 7, 4 and 2 of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. Henceforth, support all claims for gasoline expenses with Driver’s Trip Tickets, Monthly Reports of Travels and Fuel Consumptions.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor’s staff prepared the Driver’s Trip Tickets before each official travel.
observations.reason: The drivers still failed to completely fill up all the required data in Trip Tickets.  Moreover, the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels were

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team as of December 31, 2015 for audit and verification. Henceforth, the report shall be prepared and submitted to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted a Report  to the DILG as to the Appropriations and Disburse-ments of DRRM Funds.
observations.reason: The report submitted to COA was not in the prescribed format as required in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management revisit its procurement process suited for the implementation of the Supplemental Feeding Program in the LGU and apply one that is more simple, economical and practical. Petty cash fund may be granted to the day care workers at amounts equivalent to one-month food supplies requirement for the listed children beneficiaries. This will be utilized in procuring indigenous or locally produced food supplies under the supervision and guidance of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer.
observations.managementAction: Petty cash advances were allowed to the day care workers to purchase some food items and subject for liquidation.
observations.reason: The procurement process had become more complicated and entails a tedious task of tracing the utilization of the food items by the target beneficiaries in the 35 barangays, some procured in bulk through public bidding while the others through cash purchase from petty cash fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: Stop the practice of paying procured goods and services on reimbursement basis. Acquisition of goods and services is recommended to pass through the regular mode of procurement pursuant to R.A. 9184 and disbursement procedures in accordance with the NGAS; Strictly adhere to the requirement that all payments must be made by check in the name of the supplier/contractor/payee pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Complied as recommended.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the complete supporting documents pertaining to the loan including the Loan Amortization Schedule and furnish the Auditor’s Office copies of the said documents for review and evaluation. We also recommend that the Municipal Account maintain a special account or subsidiary ledger exclusively for the BCC-DPB Loan account, to facilitate audit, review and monitoring of the loan accounts.
observations.managementAction: Submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management design, document and monitor the effectiveness of its Risk Management Process for controlled and systematic hiring of Job Order Workers, set its limit in the number of workers to be hired to avoid wasteful utilization of resources and depleting funds which could be used instead, for other more beneficial purposes.
observations.reason: The JOs are being justified according to work assignments and work load.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: Regulate the proper implementation of communication allowance/benefit to ensure that irregular, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for communication are minimized. Require the SB to conduct deliberations on the proper implementation of the fund allocation for communication allowance/benefit by LGU officials/employees. There should be limitations set on the grant of such privilege/benefit to officials and selected employees for official purposes. Consider the post-paid plan options offered by the different communication providers so that communication facilities most economical and most advantageous to the LGU can be acquired. Support claims with certification that telephone/mobile phone calls paid out of public funds are all official calls in addition to the statement of account and official receipt.
observations.reason: Auditor’s Rejoinder: The necessity of the amount of communication expenses shall be duly supported with the actual bills incurred on the official communication expenses of the official/department head concerned, as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001. Hence, claims for communication expenses without these required supporting documents shall then be suspended or disallowed in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: Plan and implement GAD programs that will raise peoples’ awareness and building GAD related capabilities and putting in place policies, structures, systems, mechanisms that would facilitate and institutionalize the pursuit of gender equality and women’s empowerment; Monitor, evaluate, and make a report on the agency’s gender mainstreaming and women’s empowerment efforts. It is recommended that henceforth management maximize the utilization of the GAD budget so that activities, programs and other relevant issues relevant to GAD will be addressed and prioritized rather than misappropriating substantial portion of the budget to other expenditures thereby defeating the purpose for which the GAD budget was established.
observations.managementAction: Management averred that GAD appropriations were being culled out from the General budget as they are about to genderize all PPAs as much as possible as mandated by law.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management send demand letters to the concerned loan borrowers through the cooperatives who received the financial assistance for the CBRM livelihood projects; Henceforth, Management see to it that releases of funds to private individuals/entities should adhere to the guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 96-003.  A Memorandum of  Agreement (MOA) with intended beneficiaries should be executed upon release; Releases should first be recorded as receivable under account 138-Due from LGUs or account 139- Due from NGOs/POs, as the case maybe, to enjoin the grantees to submit liquidation documents as proof of implementation of the program or project for which municipal funds were released. Donations and/or financial assistance made to private entities/individuals should be properly evaluated to ensure that the amount requested is for public purpose and not for self-interest.  The letter-request should be accompanied with a board resolution in case of associations supported with details of proposed expenditures to be incurred. Assistance to barangay cooperatives should not be granted as donation or mere dole-out but should be as loan at a very minimal interest. With this, officials and members of the association will have accountability on the fund received, thus, they will exercise utmost care and responsibility in handling thereof for the growth of the association, thus uplifting the standard of living of the members.
observations.reason: Management justified that the CBRMP (Community-Based Resource Management Project) was intended for income generating projects with MOA at hand but the beneficiary of the POs did not pay yet so the POs also cannot pay to the LGU. Auditor’s Rejoinder: Adherence to the guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 96-003 has to be ensured before release of financial assistance. Moreover, monitoring of liquidation through implementation of projects or programs and/or the repayment of loans has to be done as often as necessary so that loan borrowers are constantly reminded of their obligations, thus protecting government funds from loss and wastage.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: submit a written justification for the grant of PEI in excess of the amount authorized under Section 1 of Executive Order (EO) No. 80 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2014-3, which set the Guidelines on the Grant of the Productivity Enhancement Incentive (PEI) to Government Employees for Fiscal Year (FY) 2014. Otherwise, require refund of the excess amount by the officials and employees concerned.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted their justification as they believe that they are entitled to the PEI.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit the following for further evaluation, viz: Justification why the LGU undertook the procurement for a project where the intended beneficiaries were not its constituents; Justification why procurement was hastily done only right after the Court’s temporary injunction on the use of PDAF on September 10, 2013; Authenticated copy of Letter-Request/Resolution from the requesting Barangays/ association; Authenticated copy of MOA between LGU-Buenavista and the Office of  Congressman Aumentado on the implementation of the project; Explanation or Justification why the end-user barangays were not represented in the procurement/bidding process; Acknowledgement Receipt or Acceptance and Inspection Report duly accomplished/signed by responsible officials of the recipient barangays/association; Explanation/Justification why the Audit Team was not furnished with copies of the Purchase Order within five (5) days from perfection.
observations.managementAction: Complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: Stop immediately the practice of including the payments of loan amortizations to private lending institutions as deduction from the payroll. Terminate immediately the arrangement (MOA) with the FCB and with other private lending institutions. For regular employees who wish to avail of such loan facility, they may transact directly with the private lending institution concerned without involving the LGU personnel and resources to avoid incurring unnecessary expenses. Follow strictly the provisions of Section 262 of the GAAM, and the pertinent provisions of PD 1445 and RA 7160.

We recommend that management devise effective measures to ensure collection of the receivables, otherwise, if proved futile, request from the Commission on Audit for the write-off of those accounts determined to be uncollectible pursuant to Section 36 of PD 1445 so as not to overstate the assets of the municipality.
observations.reason: It was intended for Income Generating Project CBRMP with MOA at hand but the beneficiary of the People’s Organizations (POs) did not continue payng their dues so the POs also cannot pay to the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: Verify the Memorandum of Agreement entered into with the donor-agencies to ascertain the proper recording in the books of account. Use the funds received in accordance with the project proposals on a timely manner to maximize the beneficial use of government projects. Effect the immediate submission of liquidation reports to the concerned agencies and remit the remaining balances of the funds downloaded after the completion of the project unless otherwise not stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to be returned to the source agency.
observations.managementAction: Other funds received which remained unimplemented were returned to the donor agencies. Liquidation Reports have been submitted subject to further audit and verification.

observations.recommendation: Require the Accountant to prepare and submit to COA the required bank reconciliation statements and to conduct review on the discrepancies noted in the bank confirmations against the balances presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Submitted Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts.
observations.reason:
Reconciling items in the BRS are doubtful.

Reiterated in the CY 2016 AAR per AOM NO. 2017-014(16)

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to further impose stricter control in the grants and liquidations of cash advances to ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002. Also, determine the status of employment of the accountable officers with long outstanding advances and if they are still employed in the Municipality, issue an order of payment, and the failure to liquidate the said cash advances shall be a valid ground for the withholding of his/her salary and other sanctions, as warranted. For those accountable officers who are no longer connected to the Municipality, issue a demand letter requiring them to liquidate all long outstanding advances.
observations.managementAction: Closely monitoring the cash advances and memorandum was already issued.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 per AOM No. 2017-008(16) dated 02/16/17

observations.recommendation: Refrain from granting cash advances to appointed officials and employees who were not designated as Special Disbursing Officer for purposes other than for their official traveling expenses, in conformity with the existing rules and regulations. Demand from the concerned accountable officer for the immediate liquidation of the said cash advances; and ensure strict compliance on the laws, rules and regulations on the grant and liquidation of cash advances to avoid possible misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Strictly implemented the guidelines.

observations.recommendation:
In view thereof, we reiterated our previous audit recommendations that for the existing inventory account balances, we recommend that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of inventories to establish actual and existing inventory items, the Supply Unit to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued based on the duly accomplished Requisition and Issue Slips and the Accounting Unit to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

Moreover, for the onward transactions, we recommended that Management:

Ensure that the heads of the Accounting Unit and Supply Unit to strictly adhere to existing rules and regulations on the maintenance of Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards.
Require the Supply Unit to religiously observe the preparation and weekly submission of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the Accounting Unit for appropriate recording of the said issuances.
Ensure the conduct of the required semestral physical count of inventories and timely reconciliation with the Accounting and Supply records against the related Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards to facilitate timely verification and adjustment of any discrepancies noted; and
Install proper and effective internal control in the recording and keeping of inventory accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Corresponding memorandums were issued to concerned office.

Supply Unit has been directed to closely coordinate with the Accounting Office.
observations.reason:
SSMI and Ledger cards are to be inspected/validated

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Engineer to accomplish the necessary Status of Work Accomplished for every infrastructure projects implemented by administration, which will be validated by the Municipality’s Inspectorate Committee and will be the basis for the issuance of the related Certificates of Completion and Acceptance by the head of agency.

Afterwards, submit to COA the Status of Work Accomplished, Inspection Report, pictures and Certificates of Completion and Acceptance for said infrastructure projects implemented by administration.
